EDCS-00380787.text_edition: \n Genio Luci R B Ursinus servus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-00380787.translation: To the Genius of the Place, Ursinus, a slave, willingly and happily fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-00380787.summary: A vow to the Genius Loci by a slave named Ursinus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-00380788.text_edition: \n Atestas Attonis filius Lutussa Bittonis filia mater Bucca Gannici filia uxor Matugenta filia\n
EDCS-00380788.translation: Atestas, son of Atto; Lutussa, daughter of Bitto; Bucca, daughter of Gannicus, wife; Matugenta, daughter.
EDCS-00380788.summary: This inscription records family members, likely a funerary monument for Atestas and his relatives, including his wife Bucca, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380789.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Imperatoris [Commodi] Augusti Pii qui templum vii sic conlapsum impendio suo restituerunt et mortalitatis causa convenerunt Marullo et Aeliano consulibus VI Kalendas Iulias Iulius Secundinus pater obitus Trebius Zoticus pater Rufius Severinus Iulius Optatus Septimius Mercator Septimius Marcus Tertinius Tertinus Mamilius Dionysius Sabinius Hermaiscus obitus Sentius Hermes Priscius Oppidanus Varius Fortis Titius Ruso Annius Syrillio Lydacius Charito Baienius Axio obitus Rufius Fuscus Marius Achilleus Claudius Quintilianus Iulius Carpus Publilius Moderatus Mamilius Crescentinus obitus Varius Secundus Marius Eutyches obitus Atticius Sextus pater Atticius Tacitus Annius Calocaerus Claudius Mercuralis Brittannius Syrus Mercatorius Crispinus Lydacius Ingenuus Claudius Quintianus Cornelius Maturus Cornelius Florentinus Trebius Alfius pater Valerius Hermophilus Baienius Homuncio Licinius Marcianus Iulius Philostra<t>us Volusius Atti<c>us Aelius Successus Marcius Marcianus Claudius Felix Aelius Marcianus Varius Sabinus Caius Baebius Cupitus Auconius Eucarpus Aelius Fuscus Marius Eutychus Baebius Bassianus Quintus Baienius Ingenuus Caius Flavius Nectareus pater Publius Aelius Fuscianus Marcus Marius Zosimus Titus Auconius Callimorfus Lucius Fac Onesimus Titus Mestrius Respectus Quintus Septimius Speratus Lucius Albius Aristo Aulus Tappetius Chionius Lutuccius Maro Marcus Marius Severianus pater Caius Iulius Maternus Lucius Candidius Aper Caius Priscius Crescentinus Quintus Iulius Adrastus Trosius Tertullinus Aelius Seccundianus sic Calendinus Successi Antonius Atticianus Claudius Primitivus Veponius Chrestus Auconius Boniatus Tiberius Claudius Curio Gaius Iulius Secundinus Claudius Tiberius Dignus Lucius Valerius Valerianus Gaius Baebius Ursulus Gaius Marius Tertullinus Bellicius Senecio Summianius Maximus Publius Aelius Cupitus Asconius Severinus Gemellius Gemellianus Marcus Ulpius Atticus Aelius Celerinus Marcus Ulpius Atticianus Speratus servus Lucius Vibius Iuvenis Helvius Varronianus Lucius Vibius Aeliomarus Rufius Saturninus Marcus Mansuetius Mansuetus Marcus Bellicius Reditus Tiberius Claudius Optatus Gaius Secundius Reditus Successius Florianus Lucius Quartinius Quartus Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus ob dedicationem templi tabulam aeream donum dedit et camaram picturis exornavit\n
EDCS-00380789.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, for the safety of the Emperor Commodus Augustus Pius, who restored this seventh temple, collapsed as it was, at his own expense, and who gathered for mortal matters. In the consulship of Marullus and Aelianus, on the sixth day before the Kalends of July. Julius Secundinus pater, deceased; Trebius Zoticus pater, deceased; Rufius Severinus, Julius Optatus, Septimius Mercator, Septimius Marcus, Tertinius Tertinus, Mamilius Dionysius, Sabinius Hermaiscus, deceased; Sentius Hermes, Priscius Oppidanus, Varius Fortis, Titius Ruso, Annius Syrillio, Lydacius Charito, Baienius Axio, deceased; Rufius Fuscus, Marius Achilleus, Claudius Quintilianus, Julius Carpus, Publilius Moderatus, Mamilius Crescentinus, deceased; Varius Secundus, Marius Eutyches, deceased; Atticius Sextus pater, Atticius Tacitus, Annius Calocaerus, Claudius Mercuralis, Brittannius Syrus, Mercatorius Crispinus, Lydacius Ingenuus, Claudius Quintianus, Cornelius Maturus, Cornelius Florentinus, Trebius Alfius pater, Valerius Hermophilus, Baienius Homuncio, Licinius Marcianus, Julius Philostratus, Volusius Atticus, Aelius Successus, Marcius Marcianus, Claudius Felix, Aelius Marcianus, Varius Sabinus, Caius Baebius Cupitus, Auconius Eucarpus, Aelius Fuscus, Marius Eutychus, Baebius Bassianus, Quintus Baienius Ingenuus, Caius Flavius Nectareus pater, Publius Aelius Fuscianus, Marcus Marius Zosimus, Titus Auconius Callimorfus, Lucius Fac Onesimus, Titus Mestrius Respectus, Quintus Septimius Speratus, Lucius Albius Aristo, Aulus Tappetius Chionius, Lutuccius Maro, Marcus Marius Severianus pater, Caius Julius Maternus, Lucius Candidius Aper, Caius Priscius Crescentinus, Quintus Julius Adrastus, Trosius Tertullinus, Aelius Seccundianus, the so-called Calendinus, Successi, Antonius Atticianus, Claudius Primitivus, Veponius Chrestus, Auconius Boniatus, Tiberius Claudius Curio, Gaius Julius Secundinus, Claudius Tiberius Dignus, Lucius Valerius Valerianus, Gaius Baebius Ursulus, Gaius Marius Tertullinus, Bellicius Senecio, Summianius Maximus, Publius Aelius Cupitus, Asconius Severinus, Gemellius Gemellianus, Marcus Ulpius Atticus, Aelius Celerinus, Marcus Ulpius Atticianus Speratus, a slave, Lucius Vibius Iuvenis, Helvius Varronianus, Lucius Vibius Aeliomarus, Rufius Saturninus, Marcus Mansuetius Mansuetus, Marcus Bellicius Reditus, Tiberius Claudius Optatus, Gaius Secundius Reditus, Successius Florianus, Lucius Quartinius Quartus, Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus, gave a bronze tablet as a gift and decorated the painted ceiling.
EDCS-00380789.summary: A lengthy inscription commemorating the dedication of a temple to Mithras, restored at the expense of the Emperor Commodus, and listing numerous individuals who contributed or attended, dating to June 24, 180 CE.

EDCS-00380790.text_edition: \n IL dae Iuni sorori filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-00380790.translation: Sons made this for their sister, Iunia.
EDCS-00380790.summary: A funerary epitaph for a sister named Iunia, created by her sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000731.translation: Of Licinius Rufus, legate of the emperor.
EDCS-03000731.summary: This inscription records Licinius Rufus, an imperial legate, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300721.text_edition: \n Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici Imperatoris ex Noricis\n
EDCS-03300721.translation: Of the camp of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, from Noricum.
EDCS-03300721.summary: This inscription likely refers to the camp of the emperor Caligula (Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus) in Noricum, dating to his reign (37-41 CE).

EDCS-03300725.text_edition: \n Iovi -ONN-C --I--- Onirus et Aelia Copia ex imperio\n
EDCS-03300725.translation: To Jupiter... Onirus and Aelia Copia by order.
EDCS-03300725.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Onirus and Aelia Copia, possibly related to a military or administrative order, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300726.summary: A dedication to the deified Victoria Augusta, likely from a shrine or altar, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300727.text_edition: \n Sextus Calventius Ingenuus vivus fecit sibi et Mattiae Meltimae coniugi annorum XL et Cupito filio annorum X et Quintiliano filio annorum VII\n
EDCS-03300727.translation: Sextus Calventius Ingenuus made this while alive for himself and for his wife Mattia Meltima, aged 40, and for his son Cupitus, aged 10, and for his son Quintilianus, aged 7.
EDCS-03300727.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Calventius Ingenuus and his family, indicating he dedicated it during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300729.text_edition: \n Resimarus Tocionis sic filius nnorum XXXX hic situs est Vercaius et Vibius filii vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-03300729.translation: Resimarus, son of Tocius, aged 40, lies here. Vercaius and Vibius, sons, made this while alive.
EDCS-03300729.summary: A funerary epitaph for Resimarus, son of Tocius, with the monument erected by his sons Vercaius and Vibius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300731.text_edition: \n Primus Secundi libertus vivus fecit sibi et Boioniae Surae coniugi annorum LX Censorinae sorori annorum L\n
EDCS-03300731.translation: Primus, freedman of Secundus, erected this while alive for himself and for his wife Boionia Sura, aged 60, and his sister Censorina, aged 50.
EDCS-03300731.summary: This epitaph records a man named Primus who built his own tomb and that of his wife and sister, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03300732.text_edition: \n Quintus Ennius Quinti filius C vivus fecit sibi et Enniae Quinti libertae Secundae uxori optimae piissimae annorum LX et suis\n
EDCS-03300732.translation: Quintus Ennius, son of Quintus, erected this while alive for himself and for Ennia Secunda, freedwoman of Quintus, his excellent and most dutiful wife, aged 60, and for his own family.
EDCS-03300732.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Quintus Ennius for himself and his wife Ennia Secunda, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03300733.summary: This is a very brief epitaph for a man named Bonillo, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-03300734.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibio Marcello annorum C\n
EDCS-03300734.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Vibius Marcellus, aged 100.
EDCS-03300734.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the deceased for Vibius Marcellus, who lived to be 100 years old, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300735.text_edition: \n Silvanus Sabini filius annorum LXX filius fecit hic situs est Aleto Batavi filius annorum LXX Bonio Silvani filius annorum LX Spectata PVIIE filia annorum LX Boniatus Bonionis annorum L\n
EDCS-03300735.translation: Silvanus, son of Sabinus, aged 70, erected this; he lies here. Also Aleto, son of Batavus, aged 70; Bonius, son of Silvanus, aged 60; Spectata, daughter of [---], aged 60; Boniatus, son of Bonio, aged 50.
EDCS-03300735.summary: This is a tomb inscription listing several individuals, including Silvanus who erected it for himself and others buried there, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300739.text_edition: \n Rustico Secundi filio annorum LX et Gaixuni coniugi OF \n
EDCS-03300739.translation: For Rusticus, son of Secundus, aged 60, and for Gaixunus, his wife, [---].
EDCS-03300739.summary: This epitaph is a dedication to Rusticus and his wife Gaixunus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700652.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundino Sexti Claudi Alpini @(obito) annorum LX Ursa coniux Ursa con(iux) viva fecit et Secundina filia\n
EDCS-03700652.translation: To the Divine Spirits: For Secundinus, upon his death at the age of 60, his wife Ursa erected this, and also for Secundina, his daughter.
EDCS-03700652.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated to Secundinus by his wife Ursa, also mentioning their daughter Secundina, and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700653.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Congonneti filius Troucillus mil(es) coh(ortis)? Mont(anorum) primae? stipendiorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-03700653.translation: Tiberius Iulius Congonneti f. Troucillus, soldier of the [---] cohort of the Montani, stationed here.
EDCS-03700653.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Iulius Troucillus, a soldier likely of Germanic origin serving in a Roman auxiliary unit, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700654.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Bellicius Victor <II>vir decurio civitatis Celeienssium sic Ursina liberta patrono optimo et bene merito @(obito) annorum LXXX fecit\n
EDCS-03700654.translation: To the Divine Spirits: For Bellicius Victor, former holder of the IIvirate and decurion of the citizen body of Celeia, aged 80, his freedwoman Ursina erected this for her excellent and well-deserving patron upon his death.
EDCS-03700654.summary: This epitaph honors Bellicius Victor, a local official from Celeia, and was erected by his freedwoman Ursina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900768.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely records two names, Anna Ma and Aelius Fuscus, possibly as a dedication or on a shared monument from the Roman period.

EDCS-04900769.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae sacrum Vibenus votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-04900769.translation: Vibenus willingly fulfilled his vow to the August Victory.
EDCS-04900769.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victoria Augusta, records the fulfillment of a vow by Vibenus.

EDCS-04900774.summary: This inscription records the name and age of a young girl named Optata.

EDCS-04900776.translation: On the 16th day before the Kalends of September, Isauricus.
EDCS-04900776.summary: This inscription records a date and a personal name, possibly indicating an event or a death.

EDCS-04900780.translation: Vibenus, son of...? and ...nucco, son of...? Quartus.
EDCS-04900780.summary: This fragmentary inscription lists names and patronymics, likely part of a longer genealogical or ownership record.

EDCS-04900781.text_edition: \n Publio Giamillio Primigenio annorum LXX et Censorinae coniugi annorum L\n
EDCS-04900781.translation: To Publius Giamillius Primigenius, aged 70, and to Censorina, his wife, aged 50.
EDCS-04900781.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Publius Giamillius Primigenius and his wife Censorina, including their ages.

EDCS-04900783.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Epona, possibly by or for Quadratus Vitalis.

EDCS-04900784.text_edition: \n Caio Talamerio Sabino AO SRIA NA O\n
EDCS-04900784.summary: This inscription is a dedication or commemoration to Gaius Talamerius Sabinus.

EDCS-04900786.text_edition: \n Satuco Tutionis fili- annorum IX et Bacaucuni Baustionis F et Sammo eto annorum XXV\n
EDCS-04900786.translation: Satuco, son of Tution, aged 9, and Bacaucus, son of Baustio, and Sammo, aged 25.
EDCS-04900786.summary: This inscription records the names, parentage, and ages of three individuals, likely children or youths, possibly for funerary purposes.

EDCS-04900788.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a daughter and a wife, likely part of a funerary or familial dedication.

EDCS-04900791.text_edition: \n Macrino A<c=G>ili Censorini servo Satulla soror fecit \n
EDCS-04900791.translation: Satulla, his sister, made this for Macrinus, son of A<c=G>ili, freedman of Censorinus.
EDCS-04900791.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Satulla for her brother Macrinus, who was a freedman of Censorinus.

EDCS-04900793.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Euphrosynus vivus fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-04900793.translation: Titus Flavius Euphrosynus made this monument for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen while he was alive; he will not be followed by an heir.
EDCS-04900793.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Flavius Euphrosynus for himself and his freedmen and freedwomen, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900794.summary: This is a tombstone identifying Vettia Prima, daughter of Caius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-04900799.text_edition: \n Vervicio Nemetilli filio et niciae Varconis filiae et verae Vervici filiae pater vivus fecit etto Vervici filio\n
EDCS-04900799.translation: The father, while alive, made this for Vervicius, son of Nemetilla, and for Nicia, daughter of Varco, and for Vera, daughter of Vervicius, and also for his son Vervicius.
EDCS-04900799.summary: This funerary monument records a father dedicating it for his children, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-04900800.text_edition: \n Vegeto Secundi filio et Saturninae Gemelli filiae coniugi\n
EDCS-04900800.translation: For Vegetus, son of Secundus, and for Saturnina, daughter of Gemellus, his wife.
EDCS-04900800.summary: This is an epitaph dedicating a tomb to Vegetus, son of Secundus, and his wife Saturnina, daughter of Gemellus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-04900801.text_edition: \n Velina Severus ma de pecunia sua\n
EDCS-04900801.translation: Velina Severus from his own money.
EDCS-04900801.summary: This records an unspecified dedication or construction funded by Velina Severus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900804.text_edition: \n Volkano sic Augusto sacrum Publius Sextius V \n
EDCS-04900804.translation: Sacred to Vulcan, thus to Augustus. Publius Sextius V [made it].
EDCS-04900804.summary: This is a dedication to Vulcan and Augustus by Publius Sextius V, possibly indicating a joint dedication during the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900805.text_edition: \n Attius Avitus sibi et Maximillae M uxori optimae\n
EDCS-04900805.translation: Attius Avitus for himself and for Maximmilla, his excellent wife.
EDCS-04900805.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Attius Avitus for himself and his wife Maximmilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900810.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Soli maritimi votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-04900810.translation: They gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the unconquered Sun god of the sea.
EDCS-04900810.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the god Sol Invictus Maritimus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900814.text_edition: \n Lucius Egnatius Secundus testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-04900814.translation: Lucius Egnatius Secundus ordered it to be made in his will.
EDCS-04900814.summary: This funerary monument indicates that Lucius Egnatius Secundus commissioned its construction through his will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900815.text_edition: \n Marcus? Claudius Marci filius Claudia Rufus IIvir iure dicundo sibi et Corneliae Cai filiae Crispinae uxori optimae et Claudio filio annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-04900815.translation: Marcus? Claudius Rufus, son of Marcus Claudius, a *duovir* with judicial authority, for himself and for Cornelia Crispina, daughter of Caius, his excellent wife, and for his son Claudius, 18 years old.
EDCS-04900815.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated by Marcus? Claudius Rufus, a local magistrate, for himself, his wife Cornelia Crispina, and his son Claudius, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-04900816.translation: To Magius Dato, to Aelia Ingenua, while they were alive.
EDCS-04900816.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Magius Dato and Aelia Ingenua for themselves while alive.

EDCS-05200537.text_edition: \n Domestico Sexti Curi Bassi liberto et Pontiae Secundae uxori vivae filii vivi fecerunt et Avito fratri nnorum X Pontio Secundo annorum L\n
EDCS-05200537.translation: His living sons made this for Domesticius, freedman of Sextus Curius Bassus, and for his wife Pontia Secunda, while they were alive, and for their brother Avitus, aged 10, and for Pontius Secundus, aged 50.
EDCS-05200537.summary: This funerary monument was erected by sons for their deceased father Domesticius, his wife Pontia Secunda, and their brother Avitus, with ages specified for Avitus and Pontius Secundus.

EDCS-05200538.text_edition: \n tus nis filius et a Elvissi f(ilia)? vivi fecerunt sibisibi Vero filio et Verino nepoti\n
EDCS-05200538.translation: [---] his son and daughter of [---] Elvissus, while alive, made this for themselves, for their son Vero, and for their grandson Verinus.
EDCS-05200538.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by parents for themselves and their descendants, Vero and Verinus.

EDCS-05200539.text_edition: \n Sentio Nigro decurioni alae Petrianae civium Romanorum torquatae decurioni Virunensium Sentia Nigrina filia fecit\n
EDCS-05200539.translation: Sentia Nigrina, daughter, made this for Sentius Niger, decurion of the ala Petriana of Roman citizens, decorated with torques, and decurion of the Virunensians.
EDCS-05200539.summary: This monument was dedicated by Sentia Nigrina to her father, Sentius Niger, who held military and civic decurional ranks, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200540.text_edition: \n Aelius Maximus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Serotinae uxori dignissimae \n
EDCS-05200540.translation: Aelius Maximus made this for himself and his most worthy wife, Iulia Serotina, while alive.
EDCS-05200540.summary: Aelius Maximus dedicated this monument for himself and his wife, Iulia Serotina, during their lifetime.

EDCS-05200541.text_edition: \n Aulus Terentius Felix et Fusca Suri filia vivi fecerunt sibi et \n
EDCS-05200541.translation: Aulus Terentius Felix and Fusca, daughter of Surus, while alive, made this for themselves and [---]
EDCS-05200541.summary: This funerary inscription records Aulus Terentius Felix and Fusca Suri f. dedicating a monument for themselves and likely other family members.

EDCS-05200543.text_edition: \n Iuvenalis et Bausu B H servi vivi fecerunt sibi et Ursulo filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-05200543.translation: Iuvenalis and Bausu, slaves, made this for themselves while alive, and for their son Ursulus, aged 25.
EDCS-05200543.summary: This funerary monument was erected by two slaves, Iuvenalis and Bausu, for themselves and their son Ursulus.

EDCS-05200544.text_edition: \n Bonia Vivani sibi et Mulioni coniugi defuncto nnorum LXX Talio Urbici sibi \n
EDCS-05200544.translation: Bonia Vivani for herself, and for her husband Muolus, who died at age 70, and for Talio, son of Urbicus, for himself.
EDCS-05200544.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Bonia Vivani for herself, her deceased husband Muolus, and Talio Urbici.

EDCS-05200545.text_edition: \n Proclo Sexti filio annorum LXX et Mentitae Vindicis filiae coniugi\n
EDCS-05200545.translation: For Proclus, son of Sextus, aged 70, and for Mentita, wife of Vindex.
EDCS-05200545.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Proclus, aged 70, and his wife Mentita.

EDCS-05200546.translation: For Masculus, son of Ni[---], aged 5, and [---]
EDCS-05200546.summary: This fragment appears to be a funerary dedication for a young boy named Masculus, aged 5.

EDCS-05200547.translation: Quadratus, son of T(itus?), son of T(itus?).
EDCS-05200547.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary tombstone or funerary dedication for an individual named Quadratus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05200549.text_edition: \n Boionius Primus Firmino fratri carissimo et coniugi eius fecit\n
EDCS-05200549.translation: Boionius Primus made this for his dearest brother Firminus and his wife.
EDCS-05200549.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Boionius Primus for his brother Firminus and the brother's wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200550.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Ulpius Nativos sic decurio alae I \n
EDCS-05200550.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Ulpius Nativos, decurion of the First Wing, set this up.
EDCS-05200550.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Ulpius Nativos, a decurion of the First Wing, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100473.summary: This is a tombstone recording the name, parentage, and age of Ursio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100477.text_edition: \n Iuliae Titi gena et Avitae Martiali Aeliae Rufillae Rufiae Mart Ael Mai\n
EDCS-06100477.translation: To Iulia, daughter of Titus, and Avita Martialis, daughter of Aelia Rufilla, and Rufia Martialis, daughter of Aelia Maior.
EDCS-06100477.summary: This inscription lists the names of three women, possibly indicating a shared tomb or family monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100495.text_edition: \n et Potentinia CL coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Aureliae Antoniae filiae vivivae\n
EDCS-06100495.translation: And Potentinia, wife of C(aius) L(ucius), made this for herself and for Aurelia Antonia, their living daughter.
EDCS-06100495.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Potentinia and her husband for themselves and their living daughter Aurelia Antonia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000801.text_edition: \n Lolliae Atticae @(obitae) annis XII Lollia Onesime filiae pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-07000801.translation: To Lollia Attica, who died at the age of 12, Lollia Onesime made this for her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-07000801.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Lollia Onesime for her daughter Lollia Attica, who died at age 12, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400585.text_edition: \n Tito Vario Titi filio Cla(udia) Cel(eia) Prisco procuratori provinciarum Mauretaniae Tingitanae Daciae inferioris praefecto classis Britannicae praefecto alae I Ulpiae contariorum miliariae praefecto alae I Taurianorum torquatae victricis praefecto alae I Hispanorum Campagonum civium Romanorum tribuno legionis praefecto cohortis I Lusitanorum equitatae praesidi optimo decuriones alaries cohortales centuriones et officiales provecti ab eo\n
EDCS-07400585.translation: To Titus Varius, son of Titus, procurator of the provinces of Mauretania Tingitana and Dacia Inferior, prefect of the British fleet, prefect of the First Wing of Contarii, prefect of the First Wing of the Tauriani Torquata Victrix, prefect of the First Wing of the Spanish Campagones of Roman citizens, tribune of a legion, prefect of the First Cohort of Lusitanians with cavalry, the best governor, the decurions of the alae, cohort officials, centurions, and promoted officers who served under him.
EDCS-07400585.summary: This is a commendatory inscription honoring Titus Varius, a high-ranking military and administrative official, by various military units and subordinates, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400586.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Opelli Severi Macrini Pii Felicis Augusti et Marci Opelli Antonini Diadumeniani nobilissimi Caesaris Aurelius Aquila et Aurelius Bassus et Varinus sacerdotes templum vi ignis cunsumptum \n
EDCS-07400586.translation: For Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, Aurelius Aquila, Aurelius Bassus, and Varinus, priests, (dedicated this) temple consumed by fire...
EDCS-07400586.summary: This is a dedication of a temple to Jupiter Dolichenus for the health of Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, made by priests, likely dating to the reign of Macrinus (217-218 AD).

EDCS-07400588.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XI consul II pro consule pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix tribunicia potestate III pro consule et Publius Septimius Geta Caesar miliaria vetustate conlabsa sic restituerunt curante Marco Iuventio Suro Proculo legato pro praetore a Celeie sic milia passuum III\n
EDCS-07400588.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the ninth time, imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, father of the country, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, with tribunician power for the third time, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, restored the milestones, worn out by age, under the supervision of Marcus Iuventius Suro Proculus, legate pro praetore, from Celeia, 3 miles.
EDCS-07400588.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, under the supervision of the legate Proculus, likely dating to around 202-208 AD.

EDCS-07400589.text_edition: \n Acutus C S servus vivus fecit sibi et Verecundae nnorum filio Supero annorum IX et Domestica filia Casto C V servo annorum \n
EDCS-07400589.translation: Acutus, freedman of C. S., made this alive for himself, and for Superus, son of Verecunda, aged 9 years, and for Domestica, daughter of Castus, freedman of C. V., aged...
EDCS-07400589.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a freedman for himself and his family members, including children of specified ages, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600337.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Sabinius Aelio Moderatus Cai filius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600337.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Sabinius Aelius Moderatus, son of Caius, a beneficiary of the consular governor of the Second Italian Legion, willingly paid his vow as deserved.
EDCS-07600337.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by a soldier serving in a Roman legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600343.text_edition: \n Vibe sic sacrum Mosiqu sic Qupiti sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600343.translation: To Vibe, sacred; to Mosiqu, sacred; to Qupiti, sacred; [he/she/they] willingly paid [his/her/their] vow as deserved.
EDCS-07600343.summary: This is a short votive inscription to unknown deities or spirits, likely from a rural or local cult in the Roman provinces, during the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600344.text_edition: \n Privato Turturi et Secundinae Fannionis Plus filius vivus sibi et parentibus\n
EDCS-07600344.translation: Privatus, son of Turturus and Secundina, daughter of Fannio, set this up alive for himself and his parents.
EDCS-07600344.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a living individual for himself and his parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600345.text_edition: \n Valentis filio et Bononiae Cattunis filiae coniugi filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-07600345.translation: His children made this for Valens' son and Bononia, daughter of Cattunis, his wife.
EDCS-07600345.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by children for their deceased father and mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600346.text_edition: \n Caius Attius Cai filius Sedatus sibi et Domitiae Palumbi filiae uxori karissimae et Attio Vibio filio et Attiae Vibiae nepti et Titiae Successae nurui pientissimae\n
EDCS-07600346.translation: Gaius Attius Sedatus, son of Gaius, [set this up] for himself, and for Domitia Palumbi, daughter of Palumbus, his dearest wife, and for Attius Vibius, his son, and Attia Vibiae, his granddaughter, and for Titia Successa, his most dutiful daughter-in-law.
EDCS-07600346.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a man for himself and his family members, including his wife, son, granddaughter, and daughter-in-law, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08400430.text_edition: \n A Iucundus et Vibenus Finiti filii fratres votum solverunt\n
EDCS-08400430.translation: Iucundus and Vibenus, sons of Finitus, brothers, paid their vow.
EDCS-08400430.summary: This is a votive inscription by two brothers for a vow they fulfilled, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08600546.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Marina ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-08600546.translation: Marina placed this statue for Juno Regina in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-08600546.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by a woman named Marina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08600547.text_edition: \n Iovi Dolicheno Aelius Secundianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600547.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus, Aelius Secundianus willingly paid his vow as deserved.
EDCS-08600547.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus by Aelius Secundianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08600548.text_edition: \n Dolicheno Iovi Optimo Maximo Successus Potentis votum reddidit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-08600548.translation: Successus, son of Potens, returned his vow to Dolichenus Jupiter Best and Greatest, willingly and joyfully, as deserved.
EDCS-08600548.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus by Successus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08600549.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Duligeno sic votum solvit laetus merito Facundus\n
EDCS-08600549.translation: Facundus joyfully and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus.
EDCS-08600549.summary: This is a votive inscription by Facundus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600550.text_edition: \n Matugena Iovi Dulicheno sic votum solvit laeta libens Matugena Iunoni votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-08600550.translation: Matugena joyfully and willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Dulichenus, and Matugena joyfully and willingly fulfilled her vow to Juno.
EDCS-08600550.summary: This inscription records vows made by Matugena to Jupiter Dulichenus and Juno, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600551.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Mer solvit votum libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-08600551.translation: Aurelius Mer joyfully and deservedly willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-08600551.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Mer to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600552.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulicheno sic Vera votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600552.translation: Vera willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus.
EDCS-08600552.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vera to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600553.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulicheno sic Apollonius Runna votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-08600553.translation: Apollonius Runna joyfully and deservedly willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus.
EDCS-08600553.summary: This is a votive inscription by Apollonius Runna to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Duligenus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600554.text_edition: \n Dulicheno sic palmam sc<ri=IR>(ipsi) libens laetus merito \n
EDCS-08600554.translation: He willingly and deservedly fulfilled the palm [of victory] to Dulichenus.
EDCS-08600554.summary: This inscription records a vow or offering made to Dulichenus, possibly related to a victory, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600555.text_edition: \n Dulicheno sic Veneria votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600555.translation: Veneria willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Dulichenus.
EDCS-08600555.summary: This is a votive inscription by Veneria to Dulichenus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600556.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Surus votum solvit libens merito MOI Surus\n
EDCS-08600556.translation: Surus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Surus.
EDCS-08600556.summary: This is a votive inscription by Surus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600557.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aestivus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-08600557.translation: Aestivus joyfully and deservedly willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-08600557.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aestivus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600567.text_edition: \n Claudio Tacito et Restitutae Montani filiae uxori filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-08600567.translation: The sons made this for Claudius Tacitus and Restituta, daughters of Montanus, his wife.
EDCS-08600567.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by sons for their father Claudius Tacitus and their mother Restituta, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08600570.text_edition: \n Iomatio Velsonis filio militi cohortis IIII Tungrorum stipendiorum XXXIII Firminae filiae annorum III filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08600570.translation: His children had this made for Iomatius, son of Velsonis, a soldier of the IV Cohort of Tungri with 33 years of service, and for Firmina, daughter of his son, aged 3 years.
EDCS-08600570.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument commissioned by children for their father, a soldier, and their younger sibling. The mention of the Tungri cohort suggests a date likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08900508.text_edition: \n Successus Celeianorum nnorum XXXX hic situs est Successus Celeianorum nnorum XXV Exoratus Celeianorum annorum XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-08900508.translation: Successus, aged 40, is buried here. Successus, aged 25. Exoratus, aged 25. are buried here.
EDCS-08900508.summary: This is a double tombstone recording the burial of three individuals named Successus and Exoratus, all from the Celeianorum family and of specified ages.

EDCS-08900509.text_edition: \n coniugi nnorum X Lucio Fabio Fortunato filio nnorum XXXX\n
EDCS-08900509.translation: To his wife, aged 10 years, and to Lucius Fabius Fortunatus, aged 40 years.
EDCS-08900509.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records dedications or burials for a wife and son, mentioning their ages.

EDCS-08901074.summary: This inscription records Surulus Volubilitanus selling or presenting 550 rings, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901075.summary: This inscription details Orosius Volubilitanus presenting or selling 110 platters and an unstated number of bowls.

EDCS-08901076.text_edition: \n N et Mo coniux et Quin Tinianus filius annorum XXV filii f(ecerunt)\n
EDCS-08901076.translation: N. and his wife, and Quintus Tinianus, son, aged 25, had [this] made.
EDCS-08901076.summary: This funerary inscription records that N., his wife, and their son Quintus Tinianus, aged 25, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-09301158.text_edition: \n Aelius Ingenuus obitus annorum LX et Iucunda filia annorum VI Iulia coniunx et Festo annorum XX filio fecit et Novellus filius\n
EDCS-09301158.translation: Aelius Ingenuus, who died at the age of 60 years, and Iucunda, his daughter, aged 6 years. Iulia, his wife, and Festus, aged 20 years, his son, made [this] and Novellus, his son.
EDCS-09301158.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by a wife and two sons for their deceased husband/father, daughter, and another son.

EDCS-09301159.text_edition: \n Lupius Severis libertus obitus annorum XX Ius uxor eius marito <e=F>t sibi et suis viva fecit \n
EDCS-09301159.translation: Lupius, freedman of Severus, who died at the age of 20 years. His wife Ius made [this] for her husband and for herself and her family while alive.
EDCS-09301159.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by a wife, Ius, for her deceased husband, Lupius, and for herself and her family, indicating a self-funded or pre-arranged burial.

EDCS-09301160.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely refers to a person named Rustic and indicates a familial relationship.

EDCS-09400579.summary: This brief inscription appears to identify an individual named Adtressa and her origin from Vibebos.

EDCS-09400582.text_edition: \n VS VRSIIS votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09400582.translation: He/She willingly and deservedly fulfilled his/her vow.
EDCS-09400582.summary: A vow fulfilled by an unknown person, likely on a funerary or religious monument.

EDCS-09400587.translation: To the holy Mercury, Aticus dedicated this.
EDCS-09400587.summary: A dedication to the god Mercury by Aticus, likely on a small altar or religious object.

EDCS-09401144.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mythrae sic sacrum Maturus servus actor domini nostri\n
EDCS-09401144.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras: Maturus, slave and agent of our lord, made this.
EDCS-09401144.summary: A dedication to the god Mithras by Maturus, a slave and agent, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09401153.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Craconi Ulpia Nundina viva fecit sibi et Ingenuus pater\n
EDCS-09401153.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpia Nundina, alive, made this for herself and for her father Ingenuus.
EDCS-09401153.summary: A funerary dedication made by Ulpia Nundina for herself and her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401155.text_edition: \n Aquoni sacrum Caius Statius Masculus et Publicius Ianuarius votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-09401155.translation: Caius Statius Masculus and Publicius Ianuarius willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Aquon.
EDCS-09401155.summary: A vow fulfilled by Caius Statius Masculus and Publicius Ianuarius to the deity Aquon, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-09401156.text_edition: \n Aquoni Abascantus Luci T P servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09401156.translation: Abascantus, slave of Lucius T. P., willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Aquon.
EDCS-09401156.summary: A vow fulfilled by Abascantus, a slave, to the deity Aquon, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-09401158.text_edition: \n noni annorum L ius filius fecit et AEC PI\n
EDCS-09401158.translation: Ius, son of Noni, aged 50 years, made this, and...
EDCS-09401158.summary: A funerary dedication for Ius, son of Noni, who lived to be 50, the rest of the inscription is lost.

EDCS-09500109.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Voltian? viv- fecit sibi et Ve \n
EDCS-09500109.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed? Voltia? ... made this for herself and...
EDCS-09500109.summary: A damaged funerary dedication, possibly for a woman named Voltia, mentioning self and another person.

EDCS-09500110.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus)? Mansueto Covnerti et Covnert erti carissim-- VE I \n
EDCS-09500110.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed? For Mansuetus, son of Covnertus, and Covnert... dearest...
EDCS-09500110.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication likely for a man named Mansuetus and possibly other family members.

EDCS-09500111.text_edition: \n Quarti filius sibi et niae coniugi et Quarto et Boniae parentibus et Marino fratri pientissimo et Quartillae sorori karissimae et Prisc-- Lascivae fili-- et Amandae \n
EDCS-09500111.translation: For Quarti, son of..., for himself and for his wife Niae, and for his parents Quarto and Bonia, and for his most dutiful brother Marinus, and for his dearest sister Quartilla, and for his daughter Prisc-- Lasciva, and for Amanda...
EDCS-09500111.summary: A complex funerary dedication detailing multiple family members, including parents, siblings, wife, and children, likely for a head of household.

EDCS-09500112.text_edition: \n Vitali et Sextae et Ampliato Perennis servus pientissimus vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09500112.translation: Perennis, the most dutiful slave, made this while alive for Vitalis, Sexta, and Ampliatus.
EDCS-09500112.summary: A funerary monument erected by a slave for his patrons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500116.text_edition: \n IIRIII s et Valeri-- RecI et Varia Respecta donum dederunt T eis ne defuncto NNMFE nio s us Quarti Verinus Ingenui inus inani s us i Primitivi nus s s tus i s i i i s i Aurelius Iulius Valentinus Primitivi Claudius Claudianus Vitalius Aprilis Iunilius Martius Genialis Ingenui Pompeius Chryseros Volusius Ursus Gemellinus Balosi Iulius Nicephor Quartus Balosi Serenianus Placidi Ursus Sabini Marius Thallus Antonius Primus Valerius Nigrinus Valerius Respectus Successus Baieni Atilius Felix Troianius Flavinus\n
EDCS-09500116.translation: The group of men [list of names] gave this offering to them, [intended for] Quarti Verinus, son of Ingenui, [in a state of] emptiness, and the deceased NNMFE.
EDCS-09500116.summary: A lengthy list of names associated with a dedication or funerary offering, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500122.summary: A simple dedication or funerary marker made by Agathangelus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500126.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae Cassius Restutus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09500126.translation: Cassius Restutus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Great Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-09500126.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the Magna Mater by Cassius Restutus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500127.translation: The First Aelian Cohort of Britons, the Antoninian.
EDCS-09500127.summary: The designation of a Roman military unit, the First Aelian Cohort of Britons, likely dating to the Antonine period (2nd century AD).

EDCS-10000404.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Sextus Valerius.
EDCS-10000404.summary: A funerary or commemorative stone simply recording the name and filiation of Lucius Valerius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10700593.text_edition: \n sibi et Iuliae Censorinae filiae et Iulio Sabino filio et Claudio Decriano marito @(obito) annorum L decurioni ornato aput sic municipium Altinatium\n
EDCS-10700593.translation: To himself, and to his daughter Iulia Censorina, and to his son Iulius Sabinus, and to his husband Claudius Decrianus, decorated councilman of the municipality of Altinum, who died aged 50.
EDCS-10700593.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudius Decrianus, a decorated councilman of Altinum, mentioning his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300912.summary: A funerary inscription recording the name and age of IIINdita, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300984.text_edition: \n Savo et Atsallutae sic Caius Iulius Iustus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11300984.translation: Caius Iulius Iustus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Savo and Atsallutae.
EDCS-11300984.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Savo and Atsallutae by Caius Iulius Iustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300985.text_edition: \n Anta Ressati filia viva fecit sibi et Castori coniugi annorum LX\n
EDCS-11300985.translation: Anta, daughter of Ressatus, made this while alive for herself and for her husband Castor, aged 60.
EDCS-11300985.summary: A funerary monument erected by Anta for herself and her deceased husband Castor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300986.text_edition: \n abili annorum LXX uro filio annorum coniugi annorum nnrum XX\n
EDCS-11300986.translation: Of [my son] Abilius, aged 70, and of my husband [name illegible], aged [illegible], and of my children [names illegible], aged 20.
EDCS-11300986.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages of family members, likely from a tombstone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301657.text_edition: \n Cautopati Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus cum suis\n
EDCS-11301657.translation: Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus with his family [dedicated this].
EDCS-11301657.summary: This is a dedication possibly to a deity, made by Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500006.text_edition: \n Vibebos posuerunt Serenus et Maru votum solverunt\n
EDCS-11500006.translation: Serenus and Marus erected this and fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-11500006.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Serenus and Marus, likely associated with a temple or shrine, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12000826.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinu[s] Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate co(n)s(ul) II pater patriae consul proconsul et Marcus Opelliu[s] Antoninus Diadumenian[us nobilissimus] Caesar princeps iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt a Cetio milia passuum XVI\n
EDCS-12000826.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul twice, father of the country, consul and proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, most provident Augustus, made this [road] from Cetius, 16 miles.
EDCS-12000826.summary: This is a milestone inscription commissioned by Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, recording the construction of a road, dating to 217-218 AD.

EDCS-12000827.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto\n
EDCS-12000827.translation: To Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-12000827.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Licinius, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-12000831.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Secundinus, son of Buccio, possibly on a tombstone or monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100877.text_edition: \n Publi Atti Severi Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae \n
EDCS-12100877.translation: Of Publius Attius Severus, of Lucius Pullius Daphnus, of Marcus Servilius Geta.
EDCS-12100877.summary: This inscription lists the names of three individuals, likely Roman citizens, possibly in a funerary context or as part of a group dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12300302.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300302.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae nomina militum qui in praetorio meo militaverunt in cohortibus decem I II III IIII V VI VII VIII VIIII X item urbanis quattuor X XI XII XIIII subieci quibus fortiter et pie militia functis ius tribuo conubi dumtaxat cum singulis et primis uxoribus ut etiamsi peregrin<i=T> iuris feminas matrimonio suo iunxerint proinde liberos tollant ac si ex duobus civibus Romanis natos ante diem XII Kalendas Martias Marco Gavio Squilla Gallicano Sexto Carminio Vetere consulibus cohors III praetoria Publio Cornelio Publi filio Claudia Crispino Agunto descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pon<t=I>ifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae nomina militum qui in praetor<i=T>o meo militaverunt in cohortibus X I II III IIII V VI VII VIII VIIII X item urbanis IIII X XI XII XIIII subieci quibus fortiter et pie militia functis ius tribuo conubi dumtaxat cum singulis et primis uxoribus ut etiamsi peregrini iuris feminas matrimonio suo iunxerint \n
EDCS-12300302.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-nephew of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator twice, consul four times, father of the country. I have appended the names of the soldiers who served in my praetorian guard in ten cohorts: I, II, III, IIII, V, VI, VII, VIII, VIIII, X, and also in the four urban cohorts: X, XI, XII, XIIII. To whom, having faithfully and dutifully completed their service, I grant the right of legal marriage, but only with a single, first wife. So that even if they should join women of peregrine status in marriage, they may have children as if born from two Roman citizens. Dated on the 12th day before the Kalends of March, during the consulship of Marcus Gavius Squilla Gallicanus and Sextus Carminius Vetus. Cohors III praetoria. Copy and recognition from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the deified Augustus, near the Temple of Minerva. Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-nephew of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator twice, consul four times, father of the country. I have appended the names of the soldiers who served in my praetorian guard in ten cohorts: X, I, II, III, IIII, V, VI, VII, VIII, VIIII, X, and also in the four urban cohorts: IIII, X, XI, XII, XIIII. To whom, having faithfully and dutifully completed their service, I grant the right of legal marriage, but only with a single, first wife. So that even if they should join women of peregrine status in marriage.
EDCS-12300302.summary: This is an imperial rescript issued by Antoninus Pius, granting the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers and praetorian guards, with a list of cohorts and consuls, dating to 140 AD.

EDCS-12700112.text_edition: \n Nixibus sanctis pro salute Constitutes sic\n
EDCS-12700112.translation: By sacred rituals for the safety of Constitutus, thus [is it decreed/dedicated].
EDCS-12700112.summary: This is a religious inscription pertaining to rituals performed for the well-being of someone named Constitutus, likely from a pagan context, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302410.translation: Harigastus, of the 3rd vexillation of the Illyrian Alae.
EDCS-13302410.summary: This inscription identifies a soldier, Harigastus, belonging to a specific military unit, the 3rd vexillation of the Illyrian Alae, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400135.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari domini nostro Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici Invicto Augusto et principi iuventutis Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari ab Agunto milia passuum LVII\n
EDCS-13400135.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, and to the prince of the youth, Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, from Aguntum, 57 miles.
EDCS-13400135.summary: This is a milestone recording a distance of 57 miles from Aguntum in honor of Emperors Carus and Carinus, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600390.summary: This is a tombstone or dedication by Publius Cipius Polybius, with no specific date inferable.

EDCS-13600398.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae sacrum Tiberius Iulius Ursulus veteranus votum retttulit pro se et suis libens laetus merito solvit\n
EDCS-13600398.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Tiberius Julius Ursulus, a veteran, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-13600398.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, dedicated by a veteran named Tiberius Julius Ursulus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13700338.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Hilarinus Hilari filius votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-13700338.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Hilarinus, son of Hilarius, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13700338.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, dedicated by Hilarinus, son of Hilarius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13800200.text_edition: \n Philodamus Laribus dono dedit libens merito\n
EDCS-13800200.translation: Philodamus gave this offering to the Lares, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-13800200.summary: This is a small dedication to the household gods (Lares) by Philodamus, with no specific date inferable.

EDCS-13800202.text_edition: \n O RO Sacrificium capricorni Caio Vibio Postumo consule pridie Nov<e=I>mbres Gallus fecit Iu |(quincuncem) vini |(trientem) crusti |(bessem) murrae LIT QVISVO \n
EDCS-13800202.translation: A sacrifice of a Capricorn: Gallus made it on the day before the Kalends of November, in the consulship of Caius Vibius Postumus. [He offered] wine, [a portion of] fine flour, and myrrh.
EDCS-13800202.summary: This is a record of a sacrifice made by Gallus during the consulship of Caio Vibio Postumo (likely 2nd century CE), detailing offerings of wine and myrrh.

EDCS-13900087.text_edition: \n Herquli sic Augusto Aurelius Mer solvit votum libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-13900087.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Aurelius Mer willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13900087.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules Augustus, dedicated by Aurelius Mer, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13900440.summary: This inscription likely marks the property or tomb of Publius Clodius Quirinalis, with no specific date inferable.

EDCS-14400010.translation: Restitutius Tertullus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14400010.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Restitutius Tertullus for himself during his lifetime, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14400011.text_edition: \n Attico Tuti filio et Quartae filiae Tiberius Claudius Uppius et Muso Secci filia mater et Belatulla Atuci filia uxor vivi fecerunt et sibi\n
EDCS-14400011.translation: Tiberius Claudius Uppius and Muso, daughter of Seccus, and Belatulla, daughter of Atucus, the mother, made this for their children Atticus and Quarta, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14400011.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Tiberius Claudius Uppius, Muso, and Belatulla for their children and themselves, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14400012.text_edition: \n Lucio Barbio Luci filio Sperato et Barbiae Quinti filiae Augurinae Sextus Barbius Finitus filius fecit\n
EDCS-14400012.translation: Sextus Barbius Finitus made this for his father, Lucius Barbius Speratus, son of Lucius, and his mother, Barhia Augurina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-14400012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Barbius Speratus and Barhia Augurina, set up by their son Sextus Barbius Finitus.

EDCS-14400013.text_edition: \n Masculina Masculi viva fecit sibi et Tertio Cupiti coniugi optimo\n
EDCS-14400013.translation: Masculina made this for herself and for her excellent husband, Tertius Cupitus, during her lifetime.
EDCS-14400013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Masculina and her husband Tertius Cupitus, set up by Masculina.

EDCS-14400014.text_edition: \n Corinthiae Kani filiae matri pientissimae Censorinus Candidi filius et sibi fecit\n
EDCS-14400014.translation: Censorinus, son of Candidus, made this for his most dutiful mother, Corinthia, daughter of Kanis, and for himself.
EDCS-14400014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Corinthia, set up by her son Censorinus, who also commemorated himself.

EDCS-14400016.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Clementi veterano ex |(centurione) et Claudiae Carae uxori filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14400016.translation: The sons made this for Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran centurion, and for his wife, Claudia Cara.
EDCS-14400016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the veteran centurion Publius Aelius Clemens and his wife Claudia Cara, set up by their sons.

EDCS-14400017.text_edition: \n Cornelia Quinti filia viva fecit sibi et Finito et CI \n
EDCS-14400017.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, made this for herself and for Finitus and C. I.
EDCS-14400017.summary: This funerary epitaph records Cornelia making provision for herself and two other individuals, Finitus and C. I.

EDCS-14400019.text_edition: \n Titus Fannius Quartus sibi et Valeriae Iuliae uxori et Fannio Valerio filio annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14400019.translation: Titus Fannius Quartus (made this) for himself, his wife Valeria Iulia, and his son Fannius Valerius, aged 30.
EDCS-14400019.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Titus Fannius Quartus, his wife Valeria Iulia, and their son Fannius Valerius.

EDCS-14400020.text_edition: \n Martio vivus fecit sibi et Lupo filio karissimo\n
EDCS-14400020.translation: Martius made this for himself and for his dearest son Lupos during his lifetime.
EDCS-14400020.summary: This funerary epitaph records Martius making provision for himself and his son Lupos.

EDCS-14400021.text_edition: \n Fontanis Nymphis Publius Cornelius Diadumenus et Iulia Threpte votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400021.translation: Publius Cornelius Diadumenus and Iulia Threpte gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the Fontan Nymphs.
EDCS-14400021.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the Fontan Nymphs by Publius Cornelius Diadumenus and Iulia Threpte.

EDCS-14400022.translation: They (plural) made this during their lifetime.
EDCS-14400022.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph, possibly indicating a couple made provision for themselves.

EDCS-14400023.text_edition: \n Elvissio Bellici defuncto annorum XXX Matiu Lucreto filio et coniugi carissimo viva fecit\n
EDCS-14400023.translation: She (feminine) made this during her lifetime for Elvissius Bellicus, who died aged 30, and for her dearest husband and son.
EDCS-14400023.summary: This funerary epitaph records a woman setting up a monument for her deceased son Elvissius Bellicus and her husband.

EDCS-14400024.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto sacrum pro salute Cassiani Baebiae Maturae et Gaviani et Honorati filiorum Ianuarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400024.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Ianuarius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the health of Cassianus, Baebia Matura, and their sons Gavianus and Honoratus.
EDCS-14400024.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars Augustus by Ianuarius for the well-being of Cassianus, Baebia Matura, and their sons.

EDCS-14400025.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ripanio Flaviano aedilicia potestate Claudi Viruni @(obito) nnorum LX et Rufia sic Primula sic coniux sic eiius Ripanius Adnamus filius parentibus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14400025.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ripanius Flavianus, who held the office of aedile, aged 60 years, and Rufia Primula, his wife. Their son Ripanius Adnamus had this made for his parents.
EDCS-14400025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ripanius Flavianus and his wife Rufia Primula, set up by their son.

EDCS-14400027.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Privatus S<e=P>necionis vivus fecit et Terentiae coniugi \n
EDCS-14400027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Privatus, son of Seneccio, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Terentia.
EDCS-14400027.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Privatus for himself and his wife Terentia.

EDCS-14400028.text_edition: \n Iulius Acceptus et Iulia Quinta Iuli<ae=EA> Cassiae liberti vivi fecerunt sibi et Acceptae filiae annorum V \n
EDCS-14400028.translation: Iulius Acceptus and Iulia Quinta, freedmen of Iulia Cassia, made this while alive for themselves and their daughter Accepta, aged 5 years.
EDCS-14400028.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by freedmen Iulius Acceptus and Iulia Quinta for themselves and their young daughter.

EDCS-14400029.text_edition: \n Latobio sacrum Caius Speratius Vibius et Valeria Avita pro incolumitate filiorum suorum voto suscepto navale vetustate conlapsum restituerunt votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400029.translation: Sacred to Latobius. Gaius Speratius Vibius and Valeria Avita, after undertaking a vow for the safety of their sons, restored this naval shrine, which had collapsed from age, and gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400029.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a naval shrine to the god Latobius by Gaius Speratius Vibius and Valeria Avita for the well-being of their sons.

EDCS-14400030.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Sabini Censorini et Liciniae Atticae patronis optimis Ianuarius et Serena liberti votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400030.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. For the health of Sabinus Censorinus and Licinia Attica, their most excellent patrons. Ianuarius and Serena, freedmen, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400030.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by freedmen Ianuarius and Serena for the health of their patrons Sabinus Censorinus and Licinia Attica.

EDCS-14400031.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Publi Aeli Finiti beneficiarii consularis et Tulliae Felicitati patronis merentibus Publius Aelius Surus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400031.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. For the health of Publius Aelius Finitus, a beneficiary of the consular legate, and Tullia Felicitas, their deserving patrons. Publius Aelius Surus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400031.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus was made by Publius Aelius Surus for the health of his patrons, Publius Aelius Finitus and Tullia Felicitas.

EDCS-14400032.text_edition: \n Secundinae Florae libertae fili- fec et Sequndae sic nepoti\n
EDCS-14400032.translation: For Secundina Flora, freedwoman, daughter, and for Secunda, granddaughter.
EDCS-14400032.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning Secundina Flora, her daughter, and granddaughter.

EDCS-14400033.text_edition: \n Tiae Marci libertae Candalae et suis Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14400033.translation: For Tia, freedwoman of Marcus, and Candala, and for themselves. Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus made this while alive.
EDCS-14400033.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus for Tia, a freedwoman of Marcus, and Candala, and for himself.

EDCS-14400034.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Vibius Fortunatus \n
EDCS-14400034.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Repeller (of evil). Vibius Fortunatus.
EDCS-14400034.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, identified as Depulsor, by Vibius Fortunatus.

EDCS-14400035.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus Agilis Quersoris cum suis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-14400035.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the gods and goddesses, Agilis Quersoris, with his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400035.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and other deities by Agilis Quersoris, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Seio Supero @(obito) annorum L Matura Cupiti marito karissimo viva fecit et sibi hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14400037.translation: To the Spirits of the departed: Matura Cupiti erected this monument for her dearest husband, Seius Superus, who died at the age of 50, and for herself; she will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-14400037.summary: This funerary inscription records Matura Cupiti's erection of a tomb for her husband Seius Superus and herself, indicating she did not intend for her heirs to inherit it, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400040.text_edition: \n Romano Claudi- Virun- us fratri defunct- annorum XX\n
EDCS-14400040.translation: To Romano Claudius, brother of Virun us, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-14400040.summary: This fragmented inscription records the death of Romano Claudius, brother of Virun us, at the age of 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400041.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pervinca Ex filia viva fecit sibi et Decorato coniugi carissimo optimo\n
EDCS-14400041.translation: To the Spirits of the departed: Pervinca, from her daughter, erected this for herself and for her dearest and best husband, Decoratus.
EDCS-14400041.summary: This funerary inscription records Pervinca's erection of a monument for herself and her husband Decoratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Probina viva fecit sibi et Iuventino coniugi karissimo\n
EDCS-14400043.translation: To the Spirits of the departed: Probina, alive, erected this for herself and for her dearest husband, Iuventinus.
EDCS-14400043.summary: This funerary inscription records Probina's erection of a monument for herself and her husband Iuventinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400046.text_edition: \n Vibio Vindicis filio annorum XXX Tattu Varronis filia sibi et marito\n
EDCS-14400046.translation: To Vibius, son of Vindicis, aged 30. Tattu, daughter of Varro, for herself and her husband.
EDCS-14400046.summary: This fragmented inscription records Vibius, son of Vindicis, aged 30, and Tattu, daughter of Varro, dedicating a monument for herself and her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400050.text_edition: \n Tertius Quarti filius Secundae coniugi karissima e\n
EDCS-14400050.translation: Tertius, son of Quarti, for his dearest wife Secunda.
EDCS-14400050.summary: This inscription records Tertius, son of Quarti, dedicating something for his wife Secunda, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400057.text_edition: \n us fec coniugi karissimo @(obito) annorum o interfecto a barbaris \n
EDCS-14400057.translation: ... to her dearest husband, who died at the age of ... killed by barbarians.
EDCS-14400057.summary: This heavily damaged funerary inscription mentions a husband who died young, killed by barbarians, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400058.text_edition: \n Homuncio filius Tertius Togionis filius et Atouta filia coniux fecerunt et Iunio eiusdem liberto\n
EDCS-14400058.translation: Homuncio, son of Tertius Togionis, and Atouta, his daughter, and their freedman Iunius, erected this.
EDCS-14400058.summary: This inscription records Homuncio, son of Tertius Togionis, and Atouta, his daughter, as dedicating a monument for themselves and their freedman Iunius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400059.text_edition: \n Iunius Firmus et Tertia uxor sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14400059.translation: Iunius Firmus and his wife Tertia erected this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14400059.summary: This funerary inscription records Iunius Firmus and his wife Tertia dedicating a monument for themselves during their lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400060.translation: Publius Titius, son of Farsuleius, son of Caius.
EDCS-14400060.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying an individual, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400061.text_edition: \n Diti Smertrio Augusto Marcus Claudius Rufus et Iulia Gemellina aedem fecerunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400061.translation: Marcus Claudius Rufus and Iulia Gemellina willingly and deservedly built this temple to Smertrius Augustus (a local deity).
EDCS-14400061.summary: This is a dedication for the construction of a temple, likely from the Roman Imperial period, funded by Marcus Claudius Rufus and Iulia Gemellina.

EDCS-14400062.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primo Ursini et Seneciae coniugi eius Ursus filius parentibus fecit\n
EDCS-14400062.translation: Ursus, the son, made this for his parents, Primo Ursini and his wife Seneciae.
EDCS-14400062.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a son for his deceased parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400063.text_edition: \n Romanio Ingenuo filio annorum II hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14400063.translation: This monument for Romanio Ingenuus, aged two, will not have a successor through inheritance.
EDCS-14400063.summary: This is a funerary monument for a young child, indicating that the plot will not be inherited, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400064.text_edition: \n Caius Valentinius Crispus vivus fecit sibi et ritiuni coniugi optimae\n
EDCS-14400064.translation: Caius Valentinius Crispus made this for himself and his excellent wife, while alive.
EDCS-14400064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Caius Valentinius Crispus for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400066.summary: This is a very brief epitaph, likely a tombstone inscription for Tacitus Devinatus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400068.text_edition: \n Iovenat Augusto Attia Ingenua votum solvit\n
EDCS-14400068.translation: Attia Ingenua fulfilled her vow to Iovenat Augustus.
EDCS-14400068.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Attia Ingenua to the deity Iovenat Augustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400072.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Adiutoriae Ursi libertae obitae annorum XL et Aufidio Avito militi legionis II Italicae Adiutor et Dionysia soror fratri et sorori optimis\n
EDCS-14400072.translation: For the Divine Shades of Adiutoria, freedwoman of Ursi, deceased aged 40, and Aufidius Avitus, soldier of the II Italica Legion; Adiutor and his sister Dionysia made this for their brother and sister, the best.
EDCS-14400072.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Adiutor and Dionysia for their deceased relatives, including a freedwoman and a soldier, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400075.text_edition: \n Biraconi Adnamati filio et Montiae Buccionis filiae et Masculo filio annorum XXXI\n
EDCS-14400075.translation: For Biraco, son of Adnamatus, and for Montia, daughter of Buccio, and for Masculus, aged 31.
EDCS-14400075.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating three individuals, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400076.translation: Avita, while alive, made this for herself [remains illegible]
EDCS-14400076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph begun by Avita for herself, with the rest of the inscription now illegible, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400079.text_edition: \n Primi nnorum II mensium dierum XVI Caius Antonius Quintianus\n
EDCS-14400079.translation: Caius Antonius Quintianus, aged 2 years, 1 month, and 16 days.
EDCS-14400079.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Caius Antonius Quintianus, likely a child.

EDCS-14400083.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records a dedication to a person named Sabina.

EDCS-14400088.text_edition: \n Atticus Quarti et Melissa Melissonis ab haeredebus sic factum\n
EDCS-14400088.translation: Atticus, from his heirs, and Melissa, from her heirs, had this made.
EDCS-14400088.summary: This inscription indicates that a monument was erected by the heirs of Atticus and Melissa.

EDCS-14400089.text_edition: \n tori et ae uxori to filio |(centurioni) nus\n
EDCS-14400089.translation: ...to his wife and son, a centurion...
EDCS-14400089.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to record a monument dedicated to a wife and son, one of whom was a centurion.

EDCS-14400091.text_edition: \n militi cohortis praetoriae nnorum stipendiorum et Veneria coniux fecerunt\n
EDCS-14400091.translation: ...a soldier of the Praetorian guard, of X years of service, and Veneria his wife made [this].
EDCS-14400091.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by a Praetorian guardsman and his wife, Veneria.

EDCS-14400092.text_edition: \n Quartus Nemetai libertus et Urbana Palumbi liberta vivi fecerunt et filiae Quintae annorum XVIII dis Manibus\n
EDCS-14400092.translation: Quartus, freedman of Nemetus, and Urbana, freedwoman of Palumbus, made [this] while alive, and for their daughters Quintae, aged 18, [dedicated to] the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-14400092.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a freedman and his freedwoman wife for themselves and their daughters, recording ages and invoking the Manes.

EDCS-14400093.text_edition: \n Capitonio Luci Tein L et et \n
EDCS-14400093.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription that appears to be a dedication to a person named Capitonio.

EDCS-14400094.text_edition: \n Elvioni Blendonis filio et Elvissae Agisonis filiae uxori heres fecit\n
EDCS-14400094.translation: For Elvion, son of Blendus, and for his wife Elvissa, daughter of Agiso, the heir made [this].
EDCS-14400094.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by an heir for Elvion and his wife Elvissa.

EDCS-14400095.text_edition: \n Ursuius vivus sibi fecit et iurae uxori \n
EDCS-14400095.translation: Ursuius made [this] for himself while alive, and for his wife Iura.
EDCS-14400095.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument that Ursuius commissioned for himself and his wife during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14400097.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records a dedication to a person named Vocretanus.

EDCS-14400098.text_edition: \n Vocretano Augusto sacrum Iulius Primulus et Vibia Vibi votum solverunt laeti libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400098.translation: Julius Primulus and Vibia Vibi joyfully, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the deified Augustus.
EDCS-14400098.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deified Augustus by Julius Primulus and Vibia Vibi.

EDCS-14400100.text_edition: \n Gracco servo et Iuniae ancillae coniugi carissumae sic \n
EDCS-14400100.translation: To Gracchus, a slave, and to Junia, a freedwoman, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14400100.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring a slave named Gracchus and his wife, Junia.

EDCS-14400101.text_edition: \n Publius Cispius Publi libertus Trouca Lucius Barbius Luci libertus Anchialus sibei et sueis vivi fecerunt Lucius Cispius Luci Barbi et Publi Cispi libertus Suadrus Cispia Barbi et Cispi liberta Exapia\n
EDCS-14400101.translation: Publius Cispius Trouca, freedman of Publius, son of Publius, and Lucius Barbius Anchialus, freedman of Lucius, son of Lucius, made this (monument) while alive for themselves and their families: Lucius Cispius, freedman of Lucius Barbius and Publius Cispius, and Cispa Exapia, freedwoman of Barbius and Cispius.
EDCS-14400101.summary: This is a funerary monument erected during their lifetime by two freedmen, Publius Cispius Trouca and Lucius Barbius Anchialus, for themselves and their families.

EDCS-14400103.text_edition: \n Silvano Adiuta Valeri Attici liberta votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400103.translation: Adiuta, freedwoman of Valerius Atticus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-14400103.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by his freedwoman Adiuta.

EDCS-14400104.text_edition: \n Marti Marcus Lollius Priscus et Memmia Prisca cum suis\n
EDCS-14400104.translation: To Mars: Marcus Lollius Priscus and Memmia Prisca, with their household.
EDCS-14400104.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars by Marcus Lollius Priscus, Memmia Prisca, and their family.

EDCS-14400105.text_edition: \n Sammus Goutonis vivus fecit sibi et Coptiae \n
EDCS-14400105.translation: Sammus, son of Goutonis, made this while alive for himself and Coptia.
EDCS-14400105.summary: This is a funerary monument erected during his lifetime by Sammus for himself and Coptia.

EDCS-14400109.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Venimari filius Fronto miles cohortis Montanorum primae nnorum L stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Tiberius Iulius Modestus patrono et sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14400109.translation: Tiberius Iulius Venimari f. Fronto, a soldier of the first cohort of Montani, aged 50, with 25 years of service, lies here. Tiberius Iulius Modestus erected this tomb for his patron and himself while alive.
EDCS-14400109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier, Tiberius Iulius Fronto, with a separate monument erected by Tiberius Iulius Modestus for his patron and himself.

EDCS-14400110.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning the name Successus.

EDCS-14400111.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription recording that a man erected something during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400114.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning the name Philotimus and Publius.

EDCS-14400116.text_edition: \n Valtidia Qurena sic viva fecit sibi et Valtidio Barduni coniugi karissimo et Ingenuo filio et Secunda Secundi viva fecit sibi et Covnertiano coniugi karissimo\n
EDCS-14400116.translation: Valtidia Qurena made this for herself while alive, and for her dearest husband Valtidius Bardunus, and for her son Ingenuus; and Secunda Secundi made this for herself while alive, and for her dearest husband Covnertianus.
EDCS-14400116.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Valtidia Qurena and Secunda Secundi dedicated a monument for themselves and their deceased family members.

EDCS-14400118.text_edition: \n Hilara Adiutoris filia viva fecit sibi et Batasiae matri et Adiutoriri patri defuncto annorum VL\n
