EDCS-14500118.summary: This inscription records a vow and dedication made by a military officer (beneficiarius) of a specific legion, dated by consuls and a specific date.

EDCS-14500119.translation: They built a temple in honor of Abascantius (for Pascanius). Aquilinus.
EDCS-14500119.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple in honor of Abascantius (or Pascanius) by Aquilinus.

EDCS-14500121.text_edition: \n Aiecii Secundina pro se et suis votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-14500121.translation: Aiecia Secundina, for herself and her family, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14500121.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by a woman named Aiecia Secundina for herself and her family.

EDCS-14500122.text_edition: \n Privatus Cai A servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500122.translation: Privatus, slave of Caius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500122.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by a slave named Privatus.

EDCS-14500123.text_edition: \n Neroni Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico Re \n
EDCS-14500123.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, King.
EDCS-14500123.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Nero, likely from a public monument or a provincial decree, dating to his reign (54-68 AD).

EDCS-14500124.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Tutoris libertus Calventius et Iulia Tiberi liberta Novella uxor fecerunt sibi et Tiberio Iulio Ingenuo filio militi cohortis XII urbanae\n
EDCS-14500124.translation: Tiberius Iulius Tutoris, freedman of Calventius, and Iulia Novella, freedwoman of Tiberius, his wife, made (this) for themselves and for their son, Tiberius Iulius Ingenuus, a soldier in the urban cohort XII.
EDCS-14500124.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication for a family, including a soldier in an urban cohort, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-14500126.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Aurelio Auggustorum liberto Burrano procuratori XX hereditatium praefecto Orte us \n
EDCS-14500126.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Aurelius Burranus, freedman of the Augusti, procurator of the XX hereditatium (inheritance tax office), prefect of Orte.
EDCS-14500126.summary: This is a dedication to the Divine Manes for Lucius Aurelius Burranus, a procuratorial official responsible for inheritance taxes and prefect of the town of Orte, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Eutycheti Augusti nostri dispensatori procuratoris regni Norici Claudia Domna coniugi et Faustina et Romulus filii et vikarii sic eius bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500127.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudia Domna, wife, and Faustina and Romulus, children, and his slaves, made (this) for Eutyches, our Emperor's dispensator (financial officer) and procurator of the province of Noricum, a deserving man.
EDCS-14500127.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Eutyches, an imperial financial official in the province of Noricum, made by his wife and children, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500129.text_edition: \n Titus Accius Marcus et Saturnina Saturnini filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Accio Maximo filio pientissimo militi legionis II Italicae frumentario annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500129.translation: Titus Accius Marcus and Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus, made (this) while alive for themselves and for their son, Accius Maximus, a most dutiful soldier in the frumentarius corps of legion II Italica, aged twenty-two.
EDCS-14500129.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by parents for their son, a frumentarius (supply officer) in the legion II Italica, dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500130.text_edition: \n Tito Aelio Suttiho veterano et Flaviae Acconi Fulvia Mat coniugi karissimo et matri pientissimae et sibi fecit\n
EDCS-14500130.translation: Fulvia Mat made (this) for Titus Aelius Suttiho, a veteran, and for Flavia Acco, her dearest husband, and most dutiful mother, and for herself.
EDCS-14500130.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Fulvia Mat for her husband, a veteran, and her mother, and herself, dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500131.text_edition: \n Aggaeo hexarcho alae celerum viro sagittandi peritissimo vi militum interempto Monna marito amantissimo\n
EDCS-14500131.translation: To Aggaeus, hexarch (a subordinate officer) of the ala celerum (swift cavalry wing), a man most skilled in archery, killed in battle, (made by) Monna, his most loving husband.
EDCS-14500131.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aggaeus, a skilled cavalry officer known for archery, who was killed in battle, made by his husband Monna, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500132.text_edition: \n Quintus Aurelius Macer veteranus ex beneficiario vivus fecit sibi et Ulpiae Romuli filiae Oecciae uxori annorum XXXII\n
EDCS-14500132.translation: Quintus Aurelius Macer, a veteran, formerly an assistant (beneficiarius), made (this) while alive for himself and for Ulpia Romula, his wife, daughter of Oecius, aged thirty-two.
EDCS-14500132.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a veteran soldier for himself and his wife, Ulpia Romula, dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Potitus veteranus alae Augustae vexellarius sic et Surilla Triti filia coniugi karissimae et sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500133.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Potitus, a veteran, vexillarius (standard-bearer) of ala Augusta, and Surilla, daughter of Tritus, his most cherished wife, made (this) for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500133.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a veteran cavalry standard-bearer and his wife for themselves, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Secundiano duplicario legionis II Italicae militi annorum XXX im sic bello desideratus sic Aurelius Senilis patrono fecit\n
EDCS-14500134.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Senilis made (this) for Aurelius Secundianus, a duplicarius (infantryman with double pay) in legion II Italica, a soldier aged thirty, who was lost in war.
EDCS-14500134.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelius Senilis for Aurelius Secundianus, a soldier in legion II Italica who died in battle, dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Secundinus miles legionis II Italicae strator consularis vivus fecit sibi et Arriae Valentinae coniugi karissimae et Valeriae Tutae conobrinae liberi scriben(dum) curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500135.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, soldier of the II Italica Legion, orderly to the consular governor, made this for himself and his dearest wife Arria Valentina and his mother-in-law Valeria Tuta, with his children arranging the inscription. He lived.
EDCS-14500135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier of the II Italica Legion, who lived and made provisions for his burial.

EDCS-14500136.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Tertius et Aurelius Tertianus milites legionis II Italicae @(biti) annorum XL\n
EDCS-14500136.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Tertius and Aurelius Tertianus, soldiers of the II Italica Legion, died aged 40.
EDCS-14500136.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two soldiers of the II Italica Legion, recording their names and ages at death.

EDCS-14500137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebius Secundus et Accepta Cupiti vivi fecerunt sibi et Cassius Ingenuus gener sibi fecit et Baebiae Secundae coniugi karissimae mensium VIII obitae nnorum XVI et Baebio Accepto militi legionis II Italicae piae fidelis @(obito) annorum XXXIII\n
EDCS-14500137.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Baebius Secundus and Accepta Cupiti made this for themselves while alive, and for their son-in-law Cassius Ingenuus, and for his dearest wife Baebia Secunda, who died after 8 months, aged 16, and for Baebius Acceptus, a soldier of the II Italica Legion, dutiful and loyal, who died aged 33.
EDCS-14500137.summary: This is a family funerary epitaph for multiple individuals, including soldiers of the II Italica Legion, with some dying at young ages.

EDCS-14500138.text_edition: \n Caio Cantonio Ucco aedili et Iuliae Bardi filiae Eliomarae uxori et Caio Cantonio Cai filio Condiano et Tiberio Cantonio Cai filio Montano militi cohortis I stipendiorum V et Lucio Cantonio Mu militi cohortis XI praetoriae et Iuliae C vivi fecerunt sibi et suis\n
EDCS-14500138.translation: For Gaius Cantionius Ucco, aedile, and his wife Iulia Barda, daughter Eliomara, and their son Gaius Cantionius Condianus, son of Gaius, and Tiberius Cantionius Montanus, soldier of the I Cohort, with 5 years of service, and Lucius Cantionius Mu, soldier of the XI Praetorian Cohort. (His family) made this for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-14500138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an aedile and his family members, including soldiers serving in praetorian cohorts, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500139.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Ingenuo militi cohortis I Asturum Bellicius Statutus decurio alae I Thracum\n
EDCS-14500139.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Ingenuus, soldier of the I Cohort of Asturians. Bellicius Statutus, decurion of the I Ala of Thracians.
EDCS-14500139.summary: This is an epitaph for a soldier of the I Cohort of Asturians, possibly erected by a fellow officer, a decurion of the I Ala of Thracians.

EDCS-14500140.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Tertius et Pomponia Procula Tiberio Claudio Cupito filio militi legionis XXI vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500140.translation: Tiberius Claudius Tertius and Pomponia Procula made this for their son Tiberius Claudius Cupitus, a soldier of the XXI Legion, while they were alive.
EDCS-14500140.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier of the XXI Legion, made by his parents during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500141.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Egronio S et Atticiae Att Egronius Valentinus et sibi et Lucre coniugi karissimae et Egronio Marci filio fratri militi legionis II Italicae\n
EDCS-14500141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Egronius S. Atticia and Egronius Valentinus made this for themselves, and for their dearest wife Lucre, and for Egronius, son of Marcus, their brother, a soldier of the II Italica Legion.
EDCS-14500141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Egronius S. and his wife Lucre, with provisions for their brother who was a soldier in the II Italica Legion.

EDCS-14500142.text_edition: \n Iulio Aprili beneficiario cohortis I Asturum stipendiorum XXII Cauru Blendonis filia con viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500142.translation: For Iulius Aprilis, a staff officer (beneficiarius) of the I Cohort of Asturians, with 22 years of service. Cauru, daughter of Blendonis, made this for him and for herself, while alive.
EDCS-14500142.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a staff officer of the I Cohort of Asturians, erected by his wife.

EDCS-14500143.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Cai filio Velina Basso et Lucio Iulio Cai filio Velina Praesenti speculatoribus Caesaris Augusti Caius Iulius Bassus pater vivus fecit Turpilia Luci filia Iunia Bassi\n
EDCS-14500143.translation: For Gaius Iulius Velina Bassus, son of Gaius, and Lucius Iulius Velina Praesens, son of Gaius, bodyguards of Emperor Augustus. Gaius Iulius Bassus, their father, made this while alive. Turpilia Iunia Bassi, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-14500143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two sons who served as bodyguards to Emperor Augustus, erected by their father.

EDCS-14500144.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Adgelei filius Buccio miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XL hic situs est a et ciata libertae fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500144.translation: Tiberius Iulius Buccio, son of Adgelei, a soldier of the First Cohort of Montani, with 40 years of service, lies here. ... and Ciata, his freedwoman, made (this).
EDCS-14500144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a veteran soldier of the First Cohort of Montani, erected by his freedwoman.

EDCS-14500145.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Condolli filius Capatius miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXX hic situs est Tiberius Iulius Crigalo Tiberius Iulius Buccio heredes fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500145.translation: Tiberius Iulius Capatius, son of Condillus, a soldier of the first cohort of Montani with 30 years of service, is buried here; Tiberius Iulius Crigalo and Tiberius Iulius Buccio, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-14500145.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Iulius Capatius, a soldier, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-14500146.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Adsedi filius Taulus miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXXVI hic situs est heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500146.translation: Tiberius Iulius Taulus, son of Adsedi, a soldier of the first cohort of Montani with 36 years of service, is buried here; his heir made this.
EDCS-14500146.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Iulius Taulus, a soldier, erected by his heir.

EDCS-14500147.text_edition: \n Lucio Peregrini et Aurelio Insequenti militibus legionis XXII Tertia Castricius et Sossius fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500147.translation: Tertia Castricius and Sossius made this for Lucio Peregrini and Aurelio Insequenti, soldiers of the 22nd Legion.
EDCS-14500147.summary: This is a dedication made by Tertia Castricius and Sossius for two soldiers of the 22nd Legion.

EDCS-14500148.text_edition: \n Marius Ructicni filius miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Montanus heres posuit\n
EDCS-14500148.translation: Marius, son of Ructicnus, a soldier of the first cohort of Montani with 25 years of service, is buried here; Montanus, his heir, erected this.
EDCS-14500148.summary: This is a tombstone for Marius, a soldier, erected by his heir, Montanus.

EDCS-14500149.text_edition: \n in canapa sic legionis interfecto a barbaris Marcia Salbia viva fecit coniugi karissimo et sibi et Aurelia Ursa Aelio Leonati coniugi karissimo @(obito) annorum LXX interfecto a barbaris viva fecit et sipi sic\n
EDCS-14500149.translation: In the ship's stores of the legion, killed by barbarians, Marcia Salbia made this while alive for her dearest husband, and for herself, and Aurelia Ursa for her dearest husband Aelius Leonatus, who died at 70 years old, killed by barbarians, she made this while alive for herself and for herself.
EDCS-14500149.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcia Salbia for her husband and herself, and by Aurelia Ursa for her husband Aelius Leonatus, both husbands killed by barbarians.

EDCS-14500150.text_edition: \n Genio Auggustorum nnostrorum Ulpius Moderatus decurio cohortis I Thracum\n
EDCS-14500150.translation: To the Genius of our Emperors, Ulpius Moderatus, a decurion of the 1st Cohort of Thracians.
EDCS-14500150.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of the Emperors by Ulpius Moderatus, an officer in the 1st Cohort of Thracians.

EDCS-14500151.text_edition: \n Sabinia Helenae sic viva fecit sibi et Aellio Tertio beneficiario coniugi optimo et Casto filio nnorum VIIII\n
EDCS-14500151.translation: Sabinia Helena made this while alive for herself and for her excellent husband Aellius Tertius, a beneficiarius, and for Castus, her son, 9 years old.
EDCS-14500151.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Sabinia Helena for herself, her husband Aellius Tertius, and her son Castus.

EDCS-14500152.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sestia Redita uxor viva fecit sibi et Tadio Victori veterano legionis II Italicae marito optimo Tadiae Victoris filiae annorum X\n
EDCS-14500152.translation: To the Divine Shades, Sestia Redita, his wife, made this while alive for herself and for her excellent husband Tadius Victor, a veteran of the 2nd Italic Legion, and for Thadía, daughter of Victor, 10 years old.
EDCS-14500152.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Sestia Redita for herself, her husband Tadius Victor (a veteran), and her daughter Thadía.

EDCS-14500153.text_edition: \n Sextus Adnamatonis filius et Bonis Anbusuli filius uxori Sextus Antonius Verus evocatus Romae Sporae Sexti filiae heredes vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500153.translation: Sextus, son of Adnamaton, and Bonis, son of Anbusulus, and Sextus Antonius Verus, an evocatus of Rome, made this while alive for their wife Sporae, daughter of Sextus, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500153.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Sextus, Bonis, and Sextus Antonius Verus for their wife Sporae and themselves.

EDCS-14500154.text_edition: \n Memoriae Valeri Claudi Quinti primo pilo legionis II Italicae duci legionis III Italicae duci et praeposito legionis III Augustae viro innocentissimo Iulius Eutychianus et Iulia Auxanon alumni\n
EDCS-14500154.translation: For the memory of Valerius Claudius Quintus, former primus pilus of the 2nd Italic Legion, commander of the 3rd Italic Legion, and prefect of the 3rd Augustan Legion, their most honest husband, Iulius Eutychianus and Iulia Auxanon, his foster children, made this.
EDCS-14500154.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the foster children Iulius Eutychianus and Iulia Auxanon for their foster father, Valerius Claudius Quintus, who held several high military commands.

EDCS-14500155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Priscianus miles legionis II Italicae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Valeriae Surillae coniugi pientissimae\n
EDCS-14500155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Priscianus, a soldier of the II Italica Pia Fidelis legion, made this while alive for himself and for his most devoted wife, Valeria Surilla.
EDCS-14500155.summary: A funerary monument set up by a soldier for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14500156.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Veponius Avitus vivus fecit sibi et Diacoxiae Meitimae coniugi karissimae et Veponio Quartino militi legionis II Italicae piae fidelis @(obito) nnorum XXXV stipendiorum IIII bello Ducco desiderato Ieilira\n
EDCS-14500156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Veponius Avitus made this while alive for himself and for his dearest wife, Diacoxia Meitima, and for Veponius Quartinus, a soldier of the II Italica Pia Fidelis legion, who died at the age of 35, after 4 years of service, and was greatly missed in the Duccan war in Ieilira.
EDCS-14500156.summary: A funerary monument set up by a soldier for himself, his wife, and a fellow soldier who died in battle.

EDCS-14500157.text_edition: \n Caius Vettius Quinti filius Pollia eques legionis VIII Augustae nnorum XLIIX stipendiorum XXVIII idem quaestor veteranorum et Quintus Vettius Quinti filius Pollia frater eques legionis VIII Augustae nnorum XL stipendiorum XX hic situs est Marcus Metilius eques legionis VIII Augustae et Publius Arrius heredes Cai Vetti testamento posuerunt\n
EDCS-14500157.translation: Caius Vettius, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, a cavalryman of the VIII Augusta legion, aged 48, with 28 years of service, and Quintus Vettius, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, his brother, a cavalryman of the VIII Augusta legion, aged 40, with 20 years of service, lie here. Marcus Metilius, a cavalryman of the VIII Augusta legion, and Publius Arrius, his heirs, placed this according to Caius Vettius's will.
EDCS-14500157.summary: A tombstone for two brothers, both cavalrymen in the VIII Augusta legion, erected by their heirs.

EDCS-14500158.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Viatorio IIviro iure dicundo Cl(audii) Vir(uni) --- praefectus cohortis I tribunus legionis V Maced(onicae) --- patri carissimo Viatoriae Taurinae et heredes \n
EDCS-14500158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Viatorius, junior magistrate with judicial power, prefect of the I cohort, tribune of the V Macedonica legion, dearest father. To Viatorius Taurinus and the heirs.
EDCS-14500158.summary: A funerary dedication to a man who held various military and judicial offices, likely by his family.

EDCS-14500159.text_edition: \n Tito Ulpio Insequenti veterano ex beneficiario consularis Titus Silvanius Adiutor heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500159.translation: Titus Ulpius Insequens, a veteran soldier, formerly a beneficiary of the governor, was honored with this monument by his heir, Titus Silvanius Adiutor.
EDCS-14500159.summary: A funerary monument erected by an heir for a veteran soldier.

EDCS-14500160.text_edition: \n nius Proculus |(centurio) legionis II Italicae fratri\n
EDCS-14500160.translation: To Proculus, a centurion of the II Italica legion, his brother.
EDCS-14500160.summary: A funerary dedication from a brother to a centurion of the II Italica legion.

EDCS-14500161.text_edition: \n legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Antoninianae ulia ula \n
EDCS-14500161.translation: [...] of the II Italica Pia Fidelis Antoniniana legion. Iulia Ula.
EDCS-14500161.summary: A fragment of an inscription likely commemorating members of the II Italica Pia Fidelis Antoniniana legion.

EDCS-14500163.text_edition: \n Caio Bottio Mercatori aedilicio Bottia Saxsami sic filia Suaducia uxsor sic et sibi viva fecit et Bottiae Nemetae f(iliae)\n
EDCS-14500163.translation: To Gaius Bottius Mercator, formerly aedile. His daughter Bottia Saxsami, Suaducia his wife, also made this for themselves while alive, and for Bottia Nemeta, daughter of Saxsamus.
EDCS-14500163.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife and daughter for a former aedile and another female family member.

EDCS-14500165.text_edition: \n Titus Tapponius Titi filius Secundus IIvir iure dicundo vivus fecit sibi et \n
EDCS-14500165.translation: Titus Tapponius Secundus, son of Titus, junior magistrate with judicial power, made this while alive for himself and...
EDCS-14500165.summary: A funerary monument set up by a junior magistrate for himself and potentially other family members.

EDCS-14500166.text_edition: \n Caio Tertinio Statuto aedilicio Catronia Severa marito optimo viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500166.translation: To Gaius Tertinius Stratus, formerly aedile. Catronia Severa made this for her most excellent husband while alive, and for herself.
EDCS-14500166.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her husband, a former aedile, and for herself.

EDCS-14500167.text_edition: \n Lucius Tuccius Luci filius Pollia Campanus haruspex et Sollonia Publi filia Sabina ob meritum rei publicae Virunensium dederunt\n
EDCS-14500167.translation: Lucius Tuccius, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, a Campanian haruspex, and Sollonia, daughter of Publius, of the Sabina tribe, dedicated this in recognition of the services of the community of Virunum.
EDCS-14500167.summary: A haruspex and his wife dedicated this monument to the community of Virunum, likely a public dedication of some kind.

EDCS-14500169.text_edition: \n Caius Publicius Viruniensium sic libertus Asiaticus fecit sibi et Lupulae coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-14500169.translation: Gaius Publicius, freedman of Virunum and Asiaticus, made this for himself and for his dearest wife, Lupula.
EDCS-14500169.summary: A freedman of Virunum made this tomb for himself and his wife, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500170.text_edition: \n Caio Acisonio Accepto Copponia Potentina sibi et coniugi et filio annorum VIII\n
EDCS-14500170.translation: Copponia Potentina made this for Gaius Acisonius Acceptus, for herself, her husband, and their son, who was 8 years old.
EDCS-14500170.summary: A woman made this tomb for her husband, son, and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500171.text_edition: \n Adiuto publico servo Paulina coniunx viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500171.translation: Paulina, his wife, made this for Adiutus, a public slave, while she was alive.
EDCS-14500171.summary: A wife made this tomb for her deceased husband, a public slave, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500172.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Candido et Emeritae filiae annorum II Cocceia Modestina et filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500172.translation: Cocceia Modestina and her sons made this for Publius Aelius Candidus and Emerita, their daughter, who was 2 years old.
EDCS-14500172.summary: A mother and her sons made this tomb for her husband and their young daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500173.text_edition: \n Aelio Mattoni Aelia Secundina sibi et coniugi pientissimo viva fecit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500173.translation: Aelia Secundina made this monument for Aelius Mattus, for herself, and for her most devoted husband, while she was alive; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500173.summary: A woman commissioned this monument for herself and her husband, stipulating it would not pass to heirs, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500174.text_edition: \n Albano Quinti Sabini Verani servo et Silvanae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500174.translation: For Albano, slave of Quintus Sabinus Veranius, and for his wife Silvana.
EDCS-14500174.summary: A simple funerary dedication for a slave and his wife, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500175.text_edition: \n Lucio Albio Telesphoro marito et Quietae matri et Spectato defuncto annorum XX et Quieto defuncto annorum XVI fratribus karissimis servis Albi Maximi Victorina et Hygia filiae servae Albi Maximi et sibi vivae fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500175.translation: Victorina and Hygia, slave daughters of Albus Maximus, made this for their dearest brothers Telesphorus and Quietus, who died at ages 20 and 16 respectively, and for their mother Quieta, and for their husband Lucius Albius Telesphorus, while they themselves were alive.
EDCS-14500175.summary: Two slave women dedicated a tomb for their mother, husband, and two deceased brothers, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500176.text_edition: \n Alfiae Publi filiae Sabinae nnorum XVIII Lucius Licinius Luci filius Fabia Syriacus uxori karissimae et Publio Alfio Epitynchano Alfiae Daphnae parentibus piissimis fecit\n
EDCS-14500176.translation: Lucius Licinius, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, called Syriacus, made this for his dearest wife Alfia, daughter of Publius, of the Sabina tribe, who was 18 years old, and for his most devoted parents, Publius Alfius Epitynchanus and Alfia Daphne.
EDCS-14500176.summary: A man dedicated this tomb to his young wife and his parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500178.text_edition: \n Marcus Annius Rufinus vivus fecit sibi et Ingenuae Titi filiae uxori et Rufinae filiae annorum IV\n
EDCS-14500178.translation: Marcus Annius Rufinus, while alive, made this for himself, for his wife Ingenua, daughter of Titus, and for his daughter Rufina, who was 4 years old.
EDCS-14500178.summary: A man commissioned this tomb for himself, his wife, and young daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500179.text_edition: \n Ariomanus Terti filius et Quarta Masculi filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Vibio filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500179.translation: Ariomanus, son of Tertius, and Quarta, daughter of Masculus, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Vibius, aged 25.
EDCS-14500179.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Ariomanus and Quarta for themselves and their deceased son Vibius.

EDCS-14500180.text_edition: \n Quintus Aterius Herma et Ateriae Primigeniae uxori et Ingenuae filiae F F\n
EDCS-14500180.translation: Quintus Aterius Herma and Ateriae Primigeniae, his wife, and Ingenua, their daughter, made this.
EDCS-14500180.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Quintus Aterius Herma for his wife Ateriae Primigeniae and daughter Ingenua.

EDCS-14500181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Primula Iulio Caio coniugi karissimo bene merenti viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500181.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Primula made this while alive for her most beloved and deserving husband, Julius Caius.
EDCS-14500181.summary: Aurelia Primula dedicated this funerary monument to her husband, Julius Caius.

EDCS-14500182.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Sura viva fecit sibi et Aurelio Tricconi coniugi karissimo et Aurelio Urso filio obito nnorum XXXII\n
EDCS-14500182.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Sura made this while alive for herself, for Aurelius Tricconus, her most beloved husband, and for Aurelius Ursu, who died at the age of 32.
EDCS-14500182.summary: Aurelia Sura dedicated this funerary monument for herself, her husband Aurelius Tricconus, and her son Aurelius Ursu.

EDCS-14500183.text_edition: \n Banona nni Sabini viva fecit sibi et Septumo coniugi\n
EDCS-14500183.translation: Banona, wife of Sabinus, made this while alive for herself and for Septumus, her husband.
EDCS-14500183.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Banona for herself and her husband Septumus.

EDCS-14500184.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Barbio Mogetus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-14500185.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Barbiae Atticae obitae nnorum L Barbia Attia coniux matri karissimae\n
EDCS-14500185.translation: To the departed spirits. For Barbia Attica, who died at the age of 50. Barbia Attia, her most beloved wife, [made this].
EDCS-14500185.summary: Barbia Attia dedicated this funerary monument to her wife, Barbia Attica.

EDCS-14500186.text_edition: \n Barbiae Publi filiae Verae uxori Publius Titius Publi libertus Adrotus sibi et suis vivus fecit Lucio Titio Publi filio Clementi annorum XII\n
EDCS-14500186.translation: Publius Titius Adrotus, freedman of Publius, made this while alive for himself, for Vera, wife of Publius, and for Lucius Titius Clementus, son of Publius, aged 12.
EDCS-14500186.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Publius Titius Adrotus for his family, including his wife Vera and son Lucius Titius Clementus.

EDCS-14500187.text_edition: \n Bassus Congeistli filius vivus fecit sibi et Camuliae Quarti filiae coniugi pientissimae et suis\n
EDCS-14500187.translation: Bassus, son of Congeistlus, made this while alive for himself, for Camulia, daughter of Quarti, his most dutiful wife, and for his family.
EDCS-14500187.summary: Bassus dedicated this monument for himself, his wife Camulia, and his family.

EDCS-14500188.text_edition: \n ius Bausonis vivus fecit sibi et Cotoniae niugi optimae et Aino filio annorum II\n
EDCS-14500188.translation: [... for] Bauson, his wife Cotonia, an excellent wife, and Aino, their son, aged 2, made this while alive for themselves.
EDCS-14500188.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Bauson, his wife Cotonia, and their son Aino.

EDCS-14500189.text_edition: \n Bauso Cupiti et Rusticae coniugi viva sic fecit sibi et Candidae filiae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500189.translation: Bauso Cupitius, while still alive, made this for himself and his daughter Candida, aged thirty, and his wife Rustica.
EDCS-14500189.summary: This funerary epitaph records a self-funded tomb for the deceased Bauso Cupitius, his wife Rustica, and daughter Candida, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500190.text_edition: \n Caballo Batavi filio Marcus Ulpius Senecio fratri\n
EDCS-14500190.translation: Marcus Ulpius Senecio, to his brother Caballo, son of Batavus.
EDCS-14500190.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Ulpius Senecio to his deceased brother, Caballo, son of Batavus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500191.text_edition: \n Lucius Celerius Campester et Celeria Primigenia filio mensium VIII\n
EDCS-14500191.translation: Lucius Celerius Campester and Celeria Primigenia, to their son, aged eight months.
EDCS-14500191.summary: This epitaph records a dedication by Lucius Celerius Campester and Celeria Primigenia to their infant son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500192.text_edition: \n Quintus Cervius Faustus et Banona Montani vivi fecerunt sibi et Faustinae filiae nnorum X\n
EDCS-14500192.translation: Quintus Cervius Faustus and Banona, wife of Montanus, while alive, made this for themselves and their daughter Faustina, aged ten.
EDCS-14500192.summary: This epitaph records a self-funded tomb for Quintus Cervius Faustus, his wife Banona, and daughter Faustina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500193.text_edition: \n Camuliano Camuli et Suadra<e> Senuci heres fecit Facundus libertus\n
EDCS-14500193.translation: The heir of Camulianus, son of Camulus, and Suaeta, daughter of Senucius, Facundus the freedman, made this.
EDCS-14500193.summary: This epitaph records that Facundus, a freedman and heir of Camulianus and Suaeta, commissioned the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Charitonis Caesaris servi Epagathiani Flavia Faustina uxor et Flavia Nice filia fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500194.translation: To the departed spirits of Chariton, a slave of Caesar, of Epagathianus. Flavia Faustina, his wife, and Flavia Nice, his daughter, made this.
EDCS-14500194.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Flavia Faustina and Flavia Nice to their deceased relative Chariton, a slave of Caesar, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500195.translation: Cinamen, freedman of Primus, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500195.summary: This epitaph records a self-funded tomb for Cinamen, a freedman of Primus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500196.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Firmaes sic Claudia Maximina et Claudia Firmina matri fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500196.translation: To the departed spirits of Claudia Firma. Claudia Maximina and Claudia Firmina made this for their mother.
EDCS-14500196.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Claudia Maximina and Claudia Firmina to their mother Claudia Firma, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500197.text_edition: \n Titus Clufennius Demetrius vivus fecit sibi et Masculiniae Verinae uxori\n
EDCS-14500197.translation: Titus Clufennius Demetrius made this while alive for himself and his wife Masculinia Verina.
EDCS-14500197.summary: This epitaph records a self-funded tomb for Titus Clufennius Demetrius and his wife Masculinia Verina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500198.text_edition: \n Marco Cocceio Sutuedi filio Quincto et Bacaucuni Togionis filiae vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500198.translation: To Marcus Cocceius Quinctus, son of Sutuedius, and Bacauca, daughter of Togio, they made this while alive.
EDCS-14500198.summary: This epitaph records a self-funded tomb for Marcus Cocceius Quinctus and Bacauca, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500199.text_edition: \n Marcus Cocceius Verecundus vivus fecit sibi et Flaviae Marciae coniugi pientissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500199.translation: Marcus Cocceius Verecundus made this monument for himself and his most dutiful wife Flavia Marcia while he was alive; he will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-14500199.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Marcus Cocceius Verecundus for himself and his wife, stating it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-14500200.text_edition: \n Coma Lottonis filia Atuiae filiae et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500200.translation: Coma, daughter of Lottonis, made this for her daughter Atuia and herself while alive.
EDCS-14500200.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Coma for her daughter Atuia and herself.

EDCS-14500201.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Conconnus Senogi vivus fecit sibi et Boniatae coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-14500201.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Conconnus Senogi made this for himself and his dearest wife Boniata while alive.
EDCS-14500201.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to the Manes by Conconnus Senogi for himself and his wife Boniata.

EDCS-14500202.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Covnerti Ituci AdmaIN Gemellinus sibi faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500202.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Gemellinus had this funerary monument made for himself by his slave Covnertus Ituci.
EDCS-14500202.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Gemellinus, to be made by his slave Covnertus Ituci.

EDCS-14500203.text_edition: \n Covnerto Terti filio Secundina Colae filia marito et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500203.translation: Secundina, daughter of Colae, made this for her husband Covnerto, son of Terti, and for herself while alive.
EDCS-14500203.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Secundina for her husband Covnerto and herself.

EDCS-14500204.text_edition: \n Covnerta Mosgaiti viva fecit sibi et MAT TO SV VON III Massato Babbecio fi<l=I>(iis \n
EDCS-14500204.translation: Covnerta Mosgaiti made this for herself and her three sons, Massato and Babbecius, while alive.
EDCS-14500204.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Covnerta Mosgaiti for herself and her three sons.

EDCS-14500206.summary: A tombstone for Cupitus Masculus Octavius, likely a funerary inscription with a name only.

EDCS-14500207.text_edition: \n Cupitus Atedunae libertus et Litulla Touti filia Genetivo filio nnorum X et sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500207.translation: Cupitus, freedman of Ateduna, and Litulla, daughter of Touti, made this for their son Genetivus, aged 10, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500207.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Cupitus and Litulla for their son Genetivus and themselves.

EDCS-14500208.text_edition: \n Cupito Burrani filio et Iuliae Iuili filiae uxsori sic Speratus filius fecit\n
EDCS-14500208.translation: For Cupito, son of Burranus, and his wife Julia, daughter of Iulius, Speratus, their son, made this.
EDCS-14500208.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Speratus for his parents Cupito and Julia.

EDCS-14500209.text_edition: \n Daphino Gai Iuli Vogitouti liberto et Secundae uxori vivae et Asteri et Frontoni vivo filii vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500209.translation: Daphinus, freedman of Gaius Iulius Vogitoutus, and his wife Secunda, and their children Aster and Fronton, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500209.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Daphinus, his wife Secunda, and their children Aster and Fronton.

EDCS-14500210.text_edition: \n Dresiu Luonis filia viva fecit sibi et Tertioni Mannonis filio coniugi et Cerescenti filio\n
EDCS-14500210.translation: Dresia, daughter of Luonis, while alive, made this for herself, her husband Tertius, son of Mannonis, and her son Cerescens.
EDCS-14500210.summary: A funerary monument erected by a woman for herself and her family members, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500211.text_edition: \n Helvisio Saxami filio et Maximae Spirviconis filiae uxori et Restituto filio annorum XVI Tertia et Helvissa filiae fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500211.translation: Helvisius, son of Saxamus, and Maxima, daughter of Spirvicon, and their son Restitutus, aged sixteen, and daughters Tertia and Helvissa, made this.
EDCS-14500211.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for themselves and their children, including a son who died at age sixteen, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500212.text_edition: \n Non gravis hic texit tumulus te punica virgo Musarum amor et Charitum Erasina voluptas annorum XII\n
EDCS-14500212.translation: This tomb covered you, Phoenician maiden, not heavily; you, beloved of the Muses and joy of the Graces, aged twelve.
EDCS-14500212.summary: An epitaph commemorating a young Phoenician girl, likely a child of an elite family, who died at the age of twelve, possibly from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500213.text_edition: \n Felix Cilumbae filius et Titiu Aterae filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Vercaioni filio\n
EDCS-14500213.translation: Felix, son of Cilumbas, and his wife Titius, daughter of Aterae, while alive, made this for themselves and for their son Vercaio.
EDCS-14500213.summary: A funerary monument erected by a couple for themselves and their son, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500214.text_edition: \n Flavio Aphobo Flavius Zosimus et Flavia Cupita patrono bene merenti fecerunt et sibi libertis libertabusque hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500214.translation: Flavius Zosimus and Flavia Cupita made this for their esteemed patron Flavius A phobus, and for themselves, their freedmen and freedwomen; this monument will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-14500214.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by freedpersons to their patron, with provisions for themselves and their freed slaves, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500215.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Natali et Domitiae Euctae Caetenia Cleonice amicis optimis fecit\n
EDCS-14500215.translation: Caetenia Cleonice made this for her most excellent friends Titus Flavius Natalis and Domitia Eucta.
EDCS-14500215.summary: A dedication of a monument or gift from a woman to her friends, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500216.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Priscus et Spuria Flavina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpiae Sabinae matri pientissimae\n
EDCS-14500216.translation: Titus Flavius Priscus and Spuria Flavina, while alive, made this for themselves and for their most devoted mother Ulpia Sabina.
EDCS-14500216.summary: A funerary monument erected by a couple for themselves and their mother, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500217.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Receptus testamento fieri iussit sibi et Bottiae Bottionis filiae fieri heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500217.translation: Titus Flavius Receptus ordered by his will that this be made for himself, and his heir had it made for Bottia, daughter of Bottio.
EDCS-14500217.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by will for the deceased and his wife, executed by his heir, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500218.translation: Publius Fundanius Hospes, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-14500218.summary: An epitaph identifying an individual as a freedman, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500219.text_edition: \n Fuscae Aiacis libertae Restitutus vivus fecit et sibi et Tacito filio nnrum XX et Sextae filiae annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500219.translation: Restitutus, while alive, made this for Fusca, freedwoman of Aiacis, and for himself, and for his son Tacitus, aged twenty, and for his daughter Sexta, aged twenty-two.
EDCS-14500219.summary: A funerary monument erected by a man for his freedwoman wife and their children, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500220.text_edition: \n Fuscia Citata Exs filia sibi viva fecit \n
EDCS-14500220.translation: Fuscia Citata made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-14500220.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Fuscia Citata for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500221.text_edition: \n Gaia Servanda viva fecit sibi et Valerio Secundino coniugi\n
EDCS-14500221.translation: Gaia Servanda made this for herself while alive, and for her husband Valerius Secundinus.
EDCS-14500221.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaia Servanda for herself and her husband Valerius Secundinus.

EDCS-14500222.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gambugius, son of Albanus, likely for a tombstone.

EDCS-14500223.text_edition: \n Caius Gavillius Peculiaris vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Tertullae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500223.translation: Gaius Gavillius Peculiaris, while alive, made this monument for himself; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500223.summary: This funerary monument was made by Gaius Gavillius Peculiaris for himself, specifying it is not to pass to heirs.

EDCS-14500225.text_edition: \n Iucundo Caucavi liberto et Suadruni Vitalis filiae\n
EDCS-14500225.translation: To Iucundus, freedman of Caucavus, and to Suadruna, daughter of Vitalis.
EDCS-14500225.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or memorial to Iucundus, a freedman, and Suadruna.

EDCS-14500226.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Censoni filio et Iuliae Privatae Sabinae libertae filii fecerunt [] MR\n
EDCS-14500226.translation: The sons made this for Gaius Julius Censoninus, son [of...], and for Iulia Privata Sabina, freedwoman, daughter [of...].
EDCS-14500226.summary: Sons erected this monument for their father, Gaius Julius Censoninus, and their mother, Iulia Privata Sabina.

EDCS-14500227.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Facundus vivus fecit sibi et suis \n
EDCS-14500227.translation: Tiberius Iulius Facundus made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-14500227.summary: This funerary monument was made by Tiberius Iulius Facundus for himself and his relatives during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500228.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus vivus fecit Iuliae Luci filiae Alpinae Tiberio Iulio Luci filio Passeri Lucio Iulio Luci filio Lucullo\n
EDCS-14500228.translation: Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus made this for Iulia Alpina, daughter of Lucius, for Tiberius Iulius Passer, son of Lucius, and for Lucius Iulius Lucullus, son of Lucius, while alive.
EDCS-14500228.summary: Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus created this monument for three individuals, likely family members, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500229.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Hermes et Govruna Tertii filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Adiutori filio et Ingenuae filiae\n
EDCS-14500229.translation: Tiberius Iulius Hermes and his wife Govruna, daughter of Tertius, made this for themselves while alive, and for their son Adiutor and daughter Ingenua.
EDCS-14500229.summary: This funerary monument was created by Tiberius Iulius Hermes and his wife Govruna for themselves and their children.

EDCS-14500230.text_edition: \n Tiberio Iulio Blaesati liberto Hospiti et Clodiae Quinti filiae Severae uxori heres vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500230.translation: The heir, while alive, made this for his freedman Hospes, Tiberius Iulius Blaesatus, and for his wife Clodia Severa, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-14500230.summary: An heir erected this monument for their freedman, Tiberius Iulius Hospes, and his wife Clodia Severa.

EDCS-14500231.text_edition: \n Tiberio Iulio Primigenio et suis Cationi coniugi Attiae matri Quartae sorori heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500231.translation: The heirs erected this for Tiberius Julius Primigenius and his family: Cationia his wife, Attia his mother, and Quarta his sister.
EDCS-14500231.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by the heirs for Tiberius Julius Primigenius, his wife, mother, and sister, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500232.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tiberio Iulio Senecioni @(obito) et Bell Avetae Tiberius Iulius Asprenas et Tiberius Iulius Iulianus parentibus bene merentibus fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500232.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Julius Asprenas and Tiberius Julius Julianus erected this for Tiberius Julius Senecio, deceased, and Bell Aveta, parents well deserving.
EDCS-14500232.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Tiberius Julius Senecio and Bell Aveta, erected by their sons Tiberius Julius Asprenas and Tiberius Julius Julianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500233.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Sossius vivus fecit sibi et Severinae uxori pientissimae\n
EDCS-14500233.translation: Titus Julius Sossius erected this for himself and for Severina his most dutiful wife, during his lifetime.
EDCS-14500233.summary: This inscription records a man, Titus Julius Sossius, erecting a monument for himself and his wife Severina during his own lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500234.text_edition: \n Tiberio Iulio Tiberi liberto Successo et Tertiae coniugi et Invento fratri fideli\n
EDCS-14500234.translation: For Tiberius Julius Successus, freedman of Tiberius, and for Tertia his wife, and for Inventus his faithful brother.
EDCS-14500234.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument to Tiberius Julius Successus, his wife Tertia, and his brother Inventus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500235.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Tertius et Caius Sempronius Albanus faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500235.translation: Marcus Julius Tertius and Caius Sempronius Albanus saw to its construction.
EDCS-14500235.summary: This inscription records the completion of a monument by Marcus Julius Tertius and Caius Sempronius Albanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500236.text_edition: \n Iuliae Tiberi filiae Banonae Marcus Ulpius Attio matri optimae\n
EDCS-14500236.translation: Marcus Ulpius Attius for Julia Banona, daughter of Tiberius, his excellent mother.
EDCS-14500236.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument from Marcus Ulpius Attius to his mother, Julia Banona, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500237.text_edition: \n Iusto Ituli Boniata Cintuli marito viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500237.translation: Cintula erected this for her husband Bonus, son of Itulus, during her lifetime, and for herself.
EDCS-14500237.summary: This funerary inscription records Cintula erecting a monument for her husband Bonus and herself during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500238.text_edition: \n Licinius Tert sibi et Tyronio! Prisco\n
EDCS-14500238.translation: Licinius Tertius for himself and for Tyronius Priscus.
EDCS-14500238.summary: This inscription records Licinius Tertius dedicating a monument for himself and Tyronius Priscus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500239.text_edition: \n Lucconi Terti filio et Aracuni Attunis filiae eeredes sic fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500239.translation: The heirs erected this as follows for Lucconus, son of Tertius, and for Aracunia, daughter of Attunis.
EDCS-14500239.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by the heirs for Lucconus and Aracunia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Masculinius Ianuarius et Caius Sabinius Saturninus faciendum curaverunt et Aureliae Martinae coniugi mihi karissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500240.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Masculinius Ianuarius and Caius Sabinius Saturninus saw to its construction, and to Aurelia Martina, my dearest wife; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500240.summary: This funerary inscription records Caius Masculinius Ianuarius and Caius Sabinius Saturninus overseeing the construction of a monument for themselves and for Aurelia Martina, stipulating it is not to be inherited, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500241.text_edition: \n Caio Masculinio Olympae et Iuniae coniugi Olympia et Tertulla filiae parentibus optimis fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500241.translation: Olympia and Tertulla, daughters, made this for their best parents, Gaius Masculinius Olympa and his wife Iunia.
EDCS-14500241.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by daughters for their parents, Gaius Masculinius Olympa and Iunia.

EDCS-14500242.translation: To Masculus, son of Saturninus, aged 16.
EDCS-14500242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Masculus, son of Saturninus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-14500243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mansuetus, son of Verus.

EDCS-14500245.text_edition: \n Attasaon Maximi servus Tertiae coniugi pientissimae\n
EDCS-14500245.translation: Attasaon, slave of Maximuus, for Tertia, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500245.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Attasaon, a slave, for his devoted wife, Tertia.

EDCS-14500246.text_edition: \n Mestrio Attiano coniugi karissimo et Mestrio Primiano et Mestrio Secundino filiis\n
EDCS-14500246.translation: To Mestrius Attianus, dearest husband, and to Mestrius Primianus and Mestrius Secundinus, sons.
EDCS-14500246.summary: This funerary monument honors Mestrius Attianus, his dearest husband, and their sons, Mestrius Primianus and Mestrius Secundinus.

EDCS-14500247.text_edition: \n Montissius Mogianci libertus sibi et Vibiae Bouterionis filiae coniugi et Cituliae filiae nnum XV vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500247.translation: Montissius Mogianci, freedman, made this for himself, for his wife Vibia, daughter of Bouterio, and for his daughter Citulia, aged 15.
EDCS-14500247.summary: This funerary monument was self-funded by Montissius Mogianci, a freedman, for himself, his wife Vibia, and his daughter Citulia.

EDCS-14500248.text_edition: \n Nemeto Marcrottae filius et Patiaantae Conbogi filiae sibi et filiae vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500248.translation: To Nemetus, son of Marcrotta, and Patiaanta, daughter of Conbogius; he made this for himself and his daughter while living.
EDCS-14500248.summary: This funerary monument was self-funded by Nemetus, son of Marcrotta, for himself and his daughter, with Patiaanta, daughter of Conbogius, also mentioned.

EDCS-14500250.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Octavio Cnaei filio Cantarro Caius Octavius Tattus Lucius Octavius Rufus Octavia Aiu fratris sic et sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500250.translation: Gaius Octavius Tattus, Lucius Octavius Rufus, and Octavia Aiu, siblings of the deceased, made this for Gnaeus Octavius, son of Gnaeus, called Cantarro, and for themselves while living.
EDCS-14500250.summary: This funerary monument was erected by siblings, Gaius Octavius Tattus, Lucius Octavius Rufus, and Octavia Aiu, for their deceased brother Gnaeus Octavius Cantarro and for themselves.

EDCS-14500251.text_edition: \n Caius Pomponius Felix vivus fecit Belatullae Duni filiae uxori et Pomponio Cai filio Martiali\n
EDCS-14500251.translation: Gaius Pomponius Felix made this while living for his wife Belatulla, daughter of Duni, and for his son Pomponius Cai Martialis.
EDCS-14500251.summary: This funerary monument was self-funded by Gaius Pomponius Felix for his wife Belatulla and son Pomponius Cai Martialis.

EDCS-14500252.text_edition: \n Primianus sibi et Valeriae Proculae coniugi karissimae @(obitae) nnorum XXX et Antoniae Ursae coniugibus piis\n
EDCS-14500252.translation: Primianus made this for himself and for Valeria Procula, his dearest wife, deceased at 30 years of age, and for Antonia Ursa, his faithful wife.
EDCS-14500252.summary: This funerary monument was self-funded by Primianus for his dearest wife Valeria Procula and his faithful wife Antonia Ursa.

EDCS-14500253.text_edition: \n Caius Priscius Cai libertus Surio vivus fecit sibi et Septimae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500253.translation: Gaius Priscius Surio, freedman of Caius, made this in his lifetime for himself and his wife Septimia.
EDCS-14500253.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Priscius Surio for himself and his wife during his lifetime, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500254.text_edition: \n Tiberio Publicio Atuno et Publiciae Vindae Privatus filius fecit Caio Publicio Privato Aeliae Quintae coniugi LGENIMIL vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500254.translation: Privatus, son of Tiberius Publicius Atunus and Publicia Vinda, made this for Gaius Publicius Privatus, his wife Aelia Quinte, and his son L. Genimius (illegible). He made this in his lifetime.
EDCS-14500254.summary: A funerary monument erected by Privatus for his parents, his wife, and his son, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500255.text_edition: \n Caius Publicius Optatus vivus fecit sibi et Aiuni \n
EDCS-14500255.translation: Gaius Publicius Optatus made this in his lifetime for himself and Aiunia.
EDCS-14500255.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Publicius Optatus for himself and Aiunia during his lifetime, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500256.text_edition: \n Publicius Quartus Publiciae Cupitae coniugi et Maturo filio\n
EDCS-14500256.translation: Publicius Quartus for Publicia Cupita, his wife, and Maturus, his son.
EDCS-14500256.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Publicius Quartus to his wife Publicia Cupita and son Maturus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500257.text_edition: \n Poblicia Mellita Poblicia Prima Poblicius Quartus de suo fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500257.translation: Poblicia Mellita, Poblicia Prima, and Poblicius Quartus made this at their own expense.
EDCS-14500257.summary: A funerary monument erected by three individuals, Poblicia Mellita, Poblicia Prima, and Poblicius Quartus, at their own expense, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500258.text_edition: \n Publilius Sossius et Publilia Silvana vivi fecerunt sibi et Sossiae filiae iuveni nnorum XX\n
EDCS-14500258.translation: Publilius Sossius and Publilia Silvana made this in their lifetime for themselves and for their daughter Sossia, a young woman of 20 years.
EDCS-14500258.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publilius Sossius and Publilia Silvana for themselves and their deceased daughter Sossia, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500259.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Pusinna, wife of the dedicator, with illegible additional text, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500260.text_edition: \n Quarto et Citatae Privatus filius parentibus fecit et Lupae filiae @(obitae) annorum III\n
EDCS-14500260.translation: Privatus, son of [illegible], made this for his parents [illegible] and for his daughter Lupa, who died at the age of 3.
EDCS-14500260.summary: A funerary monument erected by Privatus for his deceased parents and his young daughter Lupa, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500261.text_edition: \n Quartus Croutae filius et Crispa Eliomari filia uxor sibi et Restitutae filiae annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-14500261.translation: Quartus, son of Crouta, and Crispa, daughter of Eliomarus, his wife, made this for themselves and for their daughter Restituta, aged 18.
EDCS-14500261.summary: A funerary monument erected by Quartus and his wife Crispa for themselves and their deceased daughter Restituta, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500262.text_edition: \n Quarto Iuniani servo Quinta uxor viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500262.translation: Quartus, slave of Iunianus, his wife Quinta, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500262.summary: A funerary monument erected by Quinta, wife of Quartus (a slave of Iunianus), for herself and likely her husband, during her lifetime, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500263.text_edition: \n Quietus Pr Sabinae servus vivus fecit sibi et Crescentinae uxori et Saturnino fratri\n
EDCS-14500263.translation: Quietus, slave of Pr(o)imus Sabinus, made this while alive for himself, for his wife Crescentina, and for his brother Saturninus.
EDCS-14500263.summary: This funerary monument, likely a tombstone or a dedication, was erected by a freedman named Quietus for himself and his family members.

EDCS-14500264.text_edition: \n Quintiliano et Maguriae et Ianuario e<t> Det Peculiari et Sextillae Crispina domina fecit servis bene merentibus\n
EDCS-14500264.translation: The mistress Crispina made this for her deserving slaves, Quintilianus, Maguria, Ianuarius, and Peculiaris.
EDCS-14500264.summary: This is a dedication or funerary monument set up by a mistress, Crispina, for her slaves, indicating their valued service.

EDCS-14500265.text_edition: \n Redsato Matugenti filio et Martiali filio Iulia Cai filia Quinctilla uxor et Caius Iulius Bassus filius vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500265.translation: Iulia Quinctilla, wife of Redsatus Matugenus and daughter of Caius, and her son Gaius Iulius Bassus, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500265.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Iulia Quinctilla and her son Gaius Iulius Bassus for Redsatus Matugenus and Martialis, likely a husband and son.

EDCS-14500266.text_edition: \n Restituta Restituti viva fecit sibi et Iulio Finitiano coniugi carissimo\n
EDCS-14500266.translation: Restituta, daughter of Restitutus, made this while alive for herself and for her dearest husband Iulius Finitianus.
EDCS-14500266.summary: This funerary inscription records Restituta dedicating a monument for herself and her husband, Iulius Finitianus.

EDCS-14500267.text_edition: \n Aelius Romulus veteranus legionis X geminae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et IV \n
EDCS-14500267.translation: Aelius Romulus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis, made this while alive for himself and for IV [persons unknown].
EDCS-14500267.summary: This monument was erected by Aelius Romulus, a veteran of a Roman legion, for himself and four other individuals.

EDCS-14500268.text_edition: \n Rufiae Firminae Secunda Togionis filia viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500268.translation: Rufia Firmina Secunda, daughter of Togio, made this while alive for herself.
EDCS-14500268.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Rufia Firmina Secunda for herself.

EDCS-14500269.text_edition: \n Avaro Rumonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Tantiae Saxami filiae uxori Bruttae filiae\n
EDCS-14500269.translation: Avarus, son of Rumon, made this while alive for himself, for his wife Tantia Saxama, and for his daughter Brutta.
EDCS-14500269.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Avarus for himself, his wife, and his daughter.

EDCS-14500271.text_edition: \n Sabinius Peculiaris vivus fecit sibi et Florentinae coniugi et Serotinae filiae nnorum IIII et Vitali SLIMISORI III\n
EDCS-14500271.translation: Sabinius Peculiaris made this while alive for himself, for his wife Florentina, for his daughter Serotina aged 4, and for Vitalis [name incomplete] aged 3.
EDCS-14500271.summary: This funerary inscription records Sabinius Peculiaris dedicating a monument for himself, his wife, his young daughter, and another child.

EDCS-14500272.text_edition: \n Cnaei filius Sabi Cnaei filius Uti Cnaei filius Rust\n
EDCS-14500272.translation: [Monument for] Sabi, son of Cnaeus, Uti, son of Cnaeus, Rust[icus?], son of Cnaeus.
EDCS-14500272.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary monument listing at least three individuals, all sons of a man named Cnaeus.

EDCS-14500274.text_edition: \n Samuco Masculi filia viva fecit sibi et Iulio Tertiano marito\n
EDCS-14500274.translation: Samuca, daughter of Masculus, made this while alive for herself and for her husband Iulius Tertianus.
EDCS-14500274.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Samuca for herself and her husband, Iulius Tertianus.

EDCS-14500275.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sarturonius Securus et Respectia Festa vivi fecerunt sibi et Senicionio Sarturoni et Vindrunae parentibus et Sarturonio Tertiano fratri\n
EDCS-14500275.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sarturonius Securus and Respectia Festa, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their parents Senicionius Sarturonius and Vindruna, and for their brother Sarturonius Tertianus.
EDCS-14500275.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Sarturonius Securus and Respectia Festa for themselves and their deceased family members.

EDCS-14500276.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Satullus Ursini et Finita Cracci vivi fecerunt sibi et Finitae VI annorum dierum V Musuabrae\n
EDCS-14500276.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Satullus son of Ursinius and Finita daughter of Cracci, while alive, made this for themselves, and for Finita, aged 6 years and 5 days, and for Musuabra.
EDCS-14500276.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Satullus and Finita for themselves and two other individuals, including a child.

EDCS-14500278.text_edition: \n Scipio Sabini Dextri servus vivus fecit sibi et Vicariae coniugi et Exspectato filio annorum VII\n
EDCS-14500278.translation: Scipio, slave of Sabinus Dexter, while alive, made this for himself, and for Vicaria his wife, and for Exspectatus his son, aged 7 years.
EDCS-14500278.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a slave, Scipio, for himself and his family.

EDCS-14500279.text_edition: \n Secundinus Finiti vivus fecit sibi et Petroniae Avitae coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-14500279.translation: Secundinus son of Finitus, while alive, made this for himself and for Petronia Avita, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14500279.summary: A funerary epitaph created by Secundinus for himself and his wife Petronia Avita.

EDCS-14500280.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundinius Ursinus vivus fecit sibi et Betautuni coniugi karissimae et Lasscivae nepti @(obitae) anni I mensium V\n
EDCS-14500280.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Secundinius Ursinus, while alive, made this for himself, and for Betautunia his dearest wife, and for Lassciva his granddaughter, who died aged 1 year and 5 months.
EDCS-14500280.summary: A funerary epitaph established by Secundinius Ursinus for himself, his wife, and his granddaughter.

EDCS-14500281.text_edition: \n Secundus Adiutoris et Melissa Rustici filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500281.translation: Secundus, son of Adiutor, and Melissa, daughter of Rusticus, made this.
EDCS-14500281.summary: A dedication made by Secundus and Melissa, possibly for a parent or a shared monument.

EDCS-14500282.text_edition: \n Maxumo et Secundae filiae uxori lius fecit\n
EDCS-14500282.translation: Maxumus, his wife Secunda, and their daughter [---], made this.
EDCS-14500282.summary: A dedication made by Maxumus and Secunda for themselves and their daughter.

EDCS-14500283.text_edition: \n Secunda Obiliaes liberta viva fecit sibi et Primo marito optimo\n
EDCS-14500283.translation: Secunda, freedwoman of Obilius, while alive, made this for herself and for Primus, her excellent husband.
EDCS-14500283.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Secunda, a freedwoman, for herself and her husband Primus.

EDCS-14500284.text_edition: \n Caius Sestius Hermes sibi et Tertiae uxori et Niceni filiae et Cornelio Arogo\n
EDCS-14500284.translation: Caius Sestius Hermes made this for himself, and for Tertia his wife, and for Nicene his daughter, and for Cornelius Arogo.
EDCS-14500284.summary: A funerary epitaph made by Caius Sestius Hermes for himself and his family members.

EDCS-14500285.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sextiliae Sextili vixit annos XXX menses II dies XI Valentinius Ingenuus coniugi pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-14500285.translation: Sextilia, wife of Sextilius, lived 30 years, 2 months, and 11 days. Valentinius Ingenuus made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500285.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextilia, set up by her husband Valentinius Ingenuus.

EDCS-14500286.text_edition: \n Sexstus sic Auli C Severi libertus et Secundae coniugi vivus fecit sibi et Quarto fratri et Crinuoni servo annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500286.translation: Sextus, freedman of Aulus Cornelius Severus, made this for himself and his wife Secunda while alive, and for his brother Quartus and his slave Crinuo, aged 20.
EDCS-14500286.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Sextus, a freedman, for himself and his family members, including a slave, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500287.text_edition: \n Cosutio Silvano et Quartillae coniugi et Mosicaito filio nnorum VIII\n
EDCS-14500287.translation: For Cosutius Silvanus and his wife Quartilla and their son Mosicaius, aged 8.
EDCS-14500287.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Cosutius Silvanus, his wife, and their young son.

EDCS-14500288.text_edition: \n Sisiae matri Tattu filia viva fecit et Germanae sorori\n
EDCS-14500288.translation: For his mother Sisia, Tattu made this while alive, and for his sister Germana.
EDCS-14500288.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Tattu for his mother and sister during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500289.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Suadra Severi filia sibi viva fecit et Bassiano coniugi et Quartianus filius karissimus obitus\n
EDCS-14500289.translation: To the departed spirits. Suadra, daughter of Severus, made this for herself while alive, and for her husband Bassianus and her dearest son Quartianus.
EDCS-14500289.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Suadra for herself, her husband, and her son, likely indicating a tombstone or similar monument.

EDCS-14500290.text_edition: \n Sumario Limmonis filio et Bussullae Atianti filiae uxori et Tertio Sumari filio annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-14500290.translation: For Sumarius, son of Limmo, and his wife Bussulla, daughter of Atiantus, and for their son Tertius, son of Sumarius, aged 35.
EDCS-14500290.summary: This inscription commemorates three members of a family: a father, mother, and son, with the son's age provided.

EDCS-14500291.text_edition: \n et Ursae nurae vivae et Tertiniae Tertinae nepti @(obitae) annorum \n
EDCS-14500291.translation: And for Ursa, his living mother, and for Tertinia, his granddaughter, deceased, aged [---].
EDCS-14500291.summary: This fragmented inscription records individuals including a mother and a deceased granddaughter, likely part of a larger funerary monument.

EDCS-14500292.text_edition: \n Tertio Caesaris liberto Causia Occi filia uxor viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500292.translation: Causia, daughter of Occius, wife of Tertius, freedman of Caesar, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500292.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Causia, wife of Tertius, a freedman of Caesar, indicating a memorial made during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500293.text_edition: \n Tertius Iuni vivus fecit sibi et Seneciae Covnerti filiae uxori optimae et Iantullo fratri et Secundae sorori hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500293.translation: Tertius made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Senecia, daughter of Covnertus, an excellent wife, and for his brother Iantullus, and for his sister Secunda; he will not follow his heir.
EDCS-14500293.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertius for himself and his family, stipulating that the property will not pass to an heir.

EDCS-14500295.text_edition: \n Caius Titi vivus fecit Men Adiect et Termi\n
EDCS-14500295.translation: Caius, son of Titius, made this for himself, and for Men Adiectus, and for Termius.
EDCS-14500295.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Caius, son of Titius, for himself and two other individuals, likely a tomb marker.

EDCS-14500296.text_edition: \n Titia Spuri filia Tertia Publius Titius Publi libertus Surus vivi fecerunt sibi et suis Titia Publi liberta Noebia uxsor\n
EDCS-14500296.translation: Titia, daughter of Spurius, Tertia, Publius Titius, freedman of Publius, Surus, made this for themselves and their families. Titia, freedwoman of Publius, Noebia, wife.
EDCS-14500296.summary: This inscription records multiple individuals, including freedmen and women, who jointly erected a monument for themselves and their families.

EDCS-14500297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Secundo et Primulae vivae Secundianus parentibus fecit\n
EDCS-14500297.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Secundianus made this for his parents, Valerius Secundus and Primula, while he was alive.
EDCS-14500297.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Valerius Secundus and Primula, dedicated by their son Secundianus.

EDCS-14500298.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibenius Primitivus et Aiucia Primitivi vivi fecerunt sibi et Peuciniae Quintillae matri\n
EDCS-14500298.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vibenius Primitivus and Aiucia, children of Primitivus, made this for themselves and for their mother, Peucinia Quintilla, while they were alive.
EDCS-14500298.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Vibenius Primitivus and Aiucia for themselves and their mother, Peucinia Quintilla.

EDCS-14500299.text_edition: \n Vibeniae Ursae obitae annorum XXIII mensium V dierum III Paternus vivus fecit coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-14500299.translation: For Vibenia Ursa, who died at the age of 23 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Paternus made this for his dearest wife, while he was alive.
EDCS-14500299.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibenia Ursa, who died at 23 years, 5 months, and 3 days, dedicated by her husband Paternus.

EDCS-14500300.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibeni et Surillae Iuniani servis\n
EDCS-14500300.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Vibenius and Surilla, slaves of Iunianus.
EDCS-14500300.summary: A funerary inscription for two slaves, Vibenius and Surilla, belonging to Iunianus.

EDCS-14500301.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibio Ursi filio @(obito) annorum IIII Vibia havia sic nepoti karissimo fecit\n
EDCS-14500301.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Avia made this for her beloved great-nephew, Vibius Ursus, who died at the age of 4 years.
EDCS-14500301.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibius Ursus, who died at age 4, dedicated by his great-aunt Vibia Avia.

EDCS-14500302.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibiae Urbicae @(obitae) Aurelia Tarfna heres viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500302.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelia Tarfna, the heir, made this for Vibia Urnica, while she was alive.
EDCS-14500302.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibia Urnica, dedicated by her heir Aurelia Tarfna.

EDCS-14500303.text_edition: \n Vitalis vivus fecit sibi et Crescentinae coniugi et parentibus Vireduno Virioni Agrippinae Commae filiae\n
EDCS-14500303.translation: Vitalis made this for himself, for his wife Crescentina, and for his parents Viredunus, Virion, and Agrippina, daughter of Comma, while he was alive.
EDCS-14500303.summary: A funerary inscription for Vitalis, his wife Crescentina, and his parents Viredunus and Virion, and Agrippina, daughter of Comma.

EDCS-14500304.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Calendinus et Iulia Valentina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpiae Calendinae filiae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500304.translation: Marcus Ulpius Calendinus and Iulia Valentina made this for themselves and for their daughter Ulpia Calendina, who died at the age of 20 years, while they were alive.
EDCS-14500304.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Calendinus and Iulia Valentina for themselves and their deceased daughter Ulpia Calendina.

EDCS-14500305.text_edition: \n Urbico Buccionis filio et Iuliae Fuscae uxori Iulia Sisia parentibus fecit\n
EDCS-14500305.translation: Iulia Sisia made this for her parents, Urbicus, son of Buccio, and for her wife Iulia Fusca.
EDCS-14500305.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Sisia for her parents, Urbicus and Iulia Fusca.

EDCS-14500306.text_edition: \n Perpetuae Securitati Ursulus Iuliani et Covinaerta Covinerti vivi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500306.translation: Ursulus, son of Iulianus, and Covinaerta, son of Covinertus, made this for Perpetua Securitas, while they were alive.
EDCS-14500306.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Ursulus and Covinaerta for Perpetua Securitas.

EDCS-14500308.text_edition: \n Soliuslo Tourenao patri iea fecit Vibenae matri parentibus pientissumis sic\n
EDCS-14500308.translation: Soliuslo made this for Tourenus, his father, and Vibena, his mother, most dutiful parents.
EDCS-14500308.summary: This is a dedication from a son to his parents, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-14500310.text_edition: \n us tus veteranus Baiae ae coniugi Naevi Q VLCIS N V\n
EDCS-14500310.translation: Us[---]tus, a veteran, and his wife Baiaeae, daughter of Naevius Q. V L CIS N V.
EDCS-14500310.summary: This inscription appears to be part of a tomb or funerary monument for a veteran and his wife.

EDCS-14500316.text_edition: \n ADO E SER C G BSEA AMI fidi <l=E>ibert-- coniugi et sibi fecit \n
EDCS-14500316.translation: Addo, son of SER C G BSEA AMI, made this for his faithful freedman, his wife, and himself.
EDCS-14500316.summary: This funerary inscription records a man setting up a monument for himself, his wife, and his freedman.

EDCS-14500319.text_edition: \n Ancill3 Lu viva fecit sibi et Ur inconparabili coniugi\n
EDCS-14500319.translation: Luciva, the slave, made this for herself and for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-14500319.summary: This epitaph records a female slave making a monument for herself and her husband.

EDCS-14500325.text_edition: \n Lucco Vergillaes et Bacausus Terti filius fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500325.translation: Lucco Vergillaes and Bacausus, son of Tertius, made this.
EDCS-14500325.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Lucco Vergillaes and Bacausus, jointly commissioning a monument.

EDCS-14500326.text_edition: \n Poio Cribonis filius vivus fieri fecit\n
EDCS-14500326.translation: Poio, son of Cribo, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500326.summary: This funerary inscription records Poio, son of Cribo, establishing a monument for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500328.summary: This inscription records the death of Tertius, son of Adsedo, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-14500329.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Tertullus vivus fecit sibi et Maximiae Secundae uxori\n
EDCS-14500329.translation: Publius Aelius Tertullus made this for himself and for his wife Maximia Secunda while alive.
EDCS-14500329.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Aelius Tertullus establishing a monument for himself and his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500330.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Commodi fratris et Commodini nepotis Finitus Claudi Prisci fecit\n
EDCS-14500330.translation: Finitus Claudi Prisci made this for the spirits of his brother Commodus and his grandson Commodinus.
EDCS-14500330.summary: This is a dedication to the divine spirits (Dis Manibus) for the deceased brother and grandson of the dedicator.

EDCS-14500331.text_edition: \n Caius Gallius Cai libertus Tertius vivus fecit sibi et Galliae Cai libertae Suadrae uxori\n
EDCS-14500331.translation: Gaius Gallius Tertius, freedman of Gaius, made this for himself and for his wife Suadra, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-14500331.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Gallius Tertius, a freedman, establishing a monument for himself and his wife, who was also a freedwoman.

EDCS-14500332.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aper Vibeni vivus f<e>cit <F=E>irminae coniugi karissimae et sibi \n
EDCS-14500332.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aper Vibeni made this while alive for his dearest wife Firmina and for himself.
EDCS-14500332.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Aper Vibeni for his wife Firmina and himself, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500333.text_edition: \n Auno Bellici Iuliani servus Bacauso coniugi fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500333.translation: Bacauso made this for his wife and for himself. He was the slave of Aunus Bellicus Iulianus.
EDCS-14500333.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Bacauso, a slave, for his wife and himself, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500334.text_edition: \n Cacusius Adnnami sic filius Exapia Croutae filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Tertio filio\n
EDCS-14500334.translation: Cacusius, son of Adnnamius, and Exapia, daughter of Crouta, his wife, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Tertius.
EDCS-14500334.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Cacusius and his wife Exapia for themselves and their son Tertius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500335.text_edition: \n Attalo Donni filio et Aetae libertae coniugi f<il=H>ii vivi fecere sic \n
EDCS-14500335.translation: Attalus, son of Donnus, and Aeta, his freedwoman wife, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500335.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Attalus and his wife Aeta, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500336.translation: Graccus, son of Civius Iunonius, [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500336.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording a dedication by Graccus, son of Civius Iunonius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500337.text_edition: \n Lucio Dripponio Maximo et Iuniae Cai filiae Bateiae uxori Caio Maximio Cai filio Iuniano decurioni Viruniensium defuncto Romae in legatione nnorum XXX et Dripponiae Maximi filiae Suadrae nnorum XXXVIII\n
EDCS-14500337.translation: For Lucius Dripponius Maximus and his wife Iunia Bateia, daughter of Caius, aged 30, who died in Rome while on legation; and for Caïus Maximius Iunianus, decurion of the Virunienses, aged 30, who died in Rome while on legation; and for Dripponia Suadra, daughter of Maximus, aged 38.
EDCS-14500337.summary: A complex funerary inscription commemorating multiple family members, including a decurion of the Virunienses, dating likely to the late 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500338.text_edition: \n Gemellus Quarti filius et Quintilla Quinti filia vivi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500338.translation: Gemellus, son of Quarti, and Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500338.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Gemellus and Quintilla for themselves, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.
