EDCS-14500339.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rufius Mosgaito et Iulia Ingenua vivi faciendum curaverunt sibi et Eburo filio fecerunt @(obito) annorum XXX et Ingenuae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500339.translation: To the Divine Shades. Rufius Mosgaito and Iulia Ingenua arranged for this to be made while alive for themselves and for their son Eburo, aged 30, and for Ingenua, his wife.
EDCS-14500339.summary: A funerary epitaph arranged by Rufius Mosgaito and Iulia Ingenua for themselves, their son Eburo, and Eburo's wife, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500340.text_edition: \n Marcus Iuventius Vibenus vivus fecit sibi et Cudsoni Senuci filiae uxori optimae et Pastori filio annorum VII\n
EDCS-14500340.translation: Marcus Iuventius Vibenus made this while alive for himself, and for his excellent wife Cudsonia, daughter of Senucius, and for his son Pastor, aged 7.
EDCS-14500340.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Iuventius Vibenus for himself, his wife Cudsonia, and his son Pastor, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500341.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibena Ursini viva fecit sibi et Urso marito carissimo et Vibeno filio @(obito) annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500341.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vibena, wife of Ursinus, made this while alive for herself, for her dearest husband Urso, and for her son Vibenus, aged 25.
EDCS-14500341.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Vibena for herself, her husband Urso, and her son Vibenus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500342.text_edition: \n Termunibus sic Au<g=C>(ustis) sacrum Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus conductor ferrariarum Noricarum et Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus Iunior et Charitonianus filii restituerunt curante Caio Iulio Hermete procuratore \n
EDCS-14500342.translation: Sacred to Terminus Augustus. Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus, manager of the Norican iron mines, and Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus Iunior and Charitonianus, his sons, restored this, under the supervision of Gaius Julius Hermes, procurator.
EDCS-14500342.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a shrine to the god Terminus by the manager of iron mines and his sons, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500343.text_edition: \n Subitio Suri filius testamento fieri iussit sibi et Vercillae Troucilli filiae uxori Castio filius vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500343.translation: Subitius, son of Surus, ordered this to be made in his will for himself and for Vercilla, daughter of Troucillus, his wife; Castius, his son, made it during his lifetime.
EDCS-14500343.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a tomb by a son for his father and mother, as stipulated in the father's will, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500344.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primitiva fecit viva sibi et Augustino coniugi @(obito) annorum LXX\n
EDCS-14500344.translation: To the Divine Manes. Primitiva, who lived 70 years, made this while alive for herself and for Augustinus, her deceased husband.
EDCS-14500344.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman, Primitiva, making arrangements for herself and her deceased husband, Augustinus, during her lifetime, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500345.text_edition: \n Cotulia Masculi et Vibia Vibi viva filia Vibianus fecit\n
EDCS-14500345.translation: Cotulia, daughter of Masculus, and Vibia, daughter of Vibus, (made this) while alive; Vibianus made it.
EDCS-14500345.summary: This inscription records two women, Cotulia and Vibia, and Vibianus making an unspecified dedication or construction during their lifetimes, probably in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500346.text_edition: \n Secundinus Ittes vivus fecit sibi et Vibiae Adiecti et Secundinae filiae annorum XX et Senicioni filio annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500346.translation: Secundinus, son of Ittes, made this while alive for himself, for Vibia, daughter of Adiectus, and for Secundina, his daughter, aged 20, and for Senicio, his son, aged 30.
EDCS-14500346.summary: This funerary inscription records a man, Secundinus, making provisions for himself and his family members, including his wife Vibia and two children, during his lifetime, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500347.text_edition: \n Atero Festi et Cattuni Montani heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500347.translation: The heir of Aterus, son of Festus, and Cattunus, son of Montanus, made this.
EDCS-14500347.summary: This inscription records an heir making a dedication or funerary monument on behalf of two deceased individuals, Aterus and Cattunus, probably in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500348.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Super vivus fecit sibi et Vibiae uxori Classici filiae et Ulpio Vibio et Ulpio Supero filiis militibus cohortis IIII praetoriae\n
EDCS-14500348.translation: Marcus Ulpius Super, alive, made this for himself, for Vibia, daughter of Classicus, his wife, and for his sons Ulpius Vibius and Ulpius Supero, soldiers of the 4th Praetorian cohort.
EDCS-14500348.summary: This funerary inscription records a soldier, Marcus Ulpius Super, making provisions for himself, his wife Vibia, and their two sons who also served in the Praetorian Guard, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500350.text_edition: \n Aurelia Aureliana viva fecit Aurelio Iantullo fratri carissimo obito annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500350.translation: Aurelia Aureliana, alive, made this for Aurelius Iantullus, her dearest brother, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500350.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman, Aurelia Aureliana, making a dedication for her deceased brother, Aurelius Iantullus, during her lifetime, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500351.text_edition: \n Castricius Constitutus vivus fecit sibi et Secundinae Secconis filiae coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-14500351.translation: Castricius Constitutus, alive, made this for himself and for Secundina, daughter of Secconis, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14500351.summary: This funerary inscription records a man, Castricius Constitutus, making provisions for himself and his wife Secundina during his lifetime, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500352.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundina viva fecit sibi et Ingenuo marito et Secundino filio\n
EDCS-14500352.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundina, alive, made this for herself, for Ingenuus, her husband, and for Secundinus, her son.
EDCS-14500352.summary: This funerary inscription records a woman, Secundina, making arrangements for herself, her husband Ingenuus, and her son Secundinus during her lifetime, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500353.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Surianus servus saltuarius Sabini Maximi et Catulla coniux fecerunt vivi\n
EDCS-14500353.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Surianus, a woodsman slave of Sabinus Maximus, and his wife Catulla, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500353.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, was set up by a slave and his wife for themselves while alive.

EDCS-14500357.text_edition: \n Veranus Sammi et Iulia Secundina vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundino filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500357.translation: Veranus and his wife Iulia Secundina made this while alive for themselves and for their son Secundinus, aged 20.
EDCS-14500357.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, records a couple setting up a monument for themselves and their deceased son.

EDCS-14500358.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Rapido Titiae Titi filiae Paulae uxori Titio Praesenti filio Caius Tit<i>us Censor heres utriusque filius fecit\n
EDCS-14500358.translation: Caius Titius Censor, son of Titus and heir of both, made this for his wife Paula, daughter of Titus Titius Rapidus, and for his son Titus Praesens.
EDCS-14500358.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, was commissioned by Caius Titius Censor for his wife and son.

EDCS-14500359.text_edition: \n Tittionius Maturus vivus fecit sibi et Citatiae Citatae coniugi karissimae obitae annorum L\n
EDCS-14500359.translation: Tittionius Maturus made this while alive for himself and for his dearest wife Citatia Citata, who died at the age of 50.
EDCS-14500359.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, records a man setting up a monument for himself and his deceased wife.

EDCS-14500360.text_edition: \n Accepto Secci filio qui SVIN et Bateli Deusonis filiae uxori filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500360.translation: His sons made this for Acceptus, son of Seccus, who was in the legion SVIN, and for his wife Batelia, daughter of Deuso.
EDCS-14500360.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, was erected by sons for their father Acceptus, a soldier, and his wife Batelia.

EDCS-14500362.text_edition: \n Victoriae Priminiae carissimae dulcissimae coniugi Pacatianus p f q cum qua vixit annos piam memoriam fecserit sic et fili- carissim- obit- annorum XV\n
EDCS-14500362.translation: Pacatianus, son of P.f.q., made this in pious memory for his dearest, sweetest wife Victoria Priminia, with whom he lived, and also for his beloved son, who died at the age of 15.
EDCS-14500362.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, commemorates a wife and son, set up by the husband and father.

EDCS-14500363.text_edition: \n Publius Veturius Clarus vivus fecit sibi et Publio Veturio Amphioravo IIIIII patrono et Veturiae Publi filiae Tertullae uxori\n
EDCS-14500363.translation: Publius Veturius Clarus made this while alive for himself, for his patron Publius Veturius Amphioravus, and for his wife Veturia Tertulla, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-14500363.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, records a man setting up a monument for himself, his patron, and his wife.

EDCS-14500364.text_edition: \n Calupa Sabinaes sic serva viva fecit sibi et Upponi coniugi karissimo annorum XXV et Albanae Sabinaes sic ancillae vivae\n
EDCS-14500364.translation: Calupa Sabina, a slave, made this while alive for herself, for her dearest husband Upponius, aged 25, and for Albana Sabina, a slave.
EDCS-14500364.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, was set up by a slave woman for herself, her husband, and another slave woman.

EDCS-14500365.text_edition: \n Caio Acilio Cai filio Aciliano fratres fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500365.translation: His brothers made this for Caius Acilius Acilianus, son of Caius.
EDCS-14500365.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, was erected by brothers in memory of their sibling, Caius Acilius Acilianus.

EDCS-14500366.text_edition: \n Sacretius Speratus et Primitiva coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Adiutorinae nepti bene merenti @(obitae) annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500366.translation: Sacretius Speratus and his wife Primitiva made this while alive for themselves and for their well-deserving granddaughter Adiutorina, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-14500366.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the imperial period, records a couple setting up a monument for themselves and their deceased granddaughter.

EDCS-14500367.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Suro veterano Publius Aelius Gaius patri optimo vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500367.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Aelius Gaius erected this for his father, Publius Aelius Surus, a veteran, while he was alive.
EDCS-14500367.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a veteran, erected by his son while he was alive.

EDCS-14500368.text_edition: \n Caius Annius Lucius vivus fecit sibi et Anniae Sedatinae uxorii sic\n
EDCS-14500368.translation: Gaius Annius Lucius erected this for himself and his wife Annia Sedatina while he was alive.
EDCS-14500368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a man for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14500369.text_edition: \n Caio Cominio Lucano et Attiae Litugenae uxori heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500369.translation: The heir arranged for this to be made for Gaius Cominius Lucanus and his wife Attia Litugena.
EDCS-14500369.summary: This funerary inscription records that an heir commissioned the monument for Gaius Cominius Lucanus and his wife.

EDCS-14500370.text_edition: \n Cottaiio sic Anni Senecae servus vivus fecit sibi et Iustae coniugi optimae\n
EDCS-14500370.translation: The slave of Annius Seneca erected this for himself and his excellent wife Iusta while he was alive.
EDCS-14500370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a slave for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14500371.text_edition: \n Titus Sammius Passer vivus fecit sibi et Eppaeae Lucriae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500371.translation: Titus Sammius Passer erected this for himself and his wife Eppaea Lucria while he was alive.
EDCS-14500371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a man for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14500372.text_edition: \n Cattia Cai filia Serena sibi et Marco Iunio Censorino filio veterano et Iuniae Boudae filiae et Samudae filiae? \n
EDCS-14500372.translation: Cattia Serena, daughter of Gaius, for herself and her son Marcus Iunius Censorinus, a veteran, and her daughter Iunia Bouda, and her daughter Samuda.
EDCS-14500372.summary: This funerary inscription lists the individuals for whom the monument was made, including a veteran son and two daughters.

EDCS-14500373.text_edition: \n Dis deabusque omnibus Claudius Valerius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500373.translation: Claudius Valerius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to all the gods and goddesses.
EDCS-14500373.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow made to all gods and goddesses by Claudius Valerius.

EDCS-14500374.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Acceptus Acceptiani vivus fecit sibi et Acceptiano et Adnamato filiis et Iliae Successi filiae coniugi karissimae obitae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500374.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Acceptus Acceptiani erected this for himself, and for his sons Acceptianus and Adnamatus, and for his wife Ilia Successa, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500374.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a man for himself, his sons, and his deceased wife.

EDCS-14500375.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Volcacius Martialis beneficiarius consularis Valeria Sulpiciana \n
EDCS-14500375.translation: Volcacius Martialis, a beneficiary of the consular governor, [erected this] for Jupiter Best and Greatest. Valeria Sulpiciana [also dedicated this].
EDCS-14500375.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Volcacius Martialis, a military official, possibly with Valeria Sulpiciana.

EDCS-14500376.text_edition: \n Lucio Barbio Vercaio aedilicio et Barbiae Suadullae uxori vivae et Lucio Barbio Verano militi cohortis I praetoriae filii filiaeque fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500376.translation: His sons and daughters erected this for Lucius Barbrius Verca, who held the office of aedile, and for his wife Barbria Suadulla, and for his son Lucius Barbrius Veranus, a soldier in the first praetorian cohort.
EDCS-14500376.summary: This funerary inscription records the monument erected by children for their parents and a brother who was a soldier.

EDCS-14500377.text_edition: \n Caio Crispinio Rufo aedilicio et Baebiae Boniae uxori filii et filiae vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500377.translation: Their children, while alive, made this for Gaius Crispinus Rufus, former aedile, and his wife Baebia Bonia.
EDCS-14500377.summary: A funerary monument erected by children for their parents, dating likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500379.text_edition: \n Disocno Dulsonis filio et Boniatae Vindonis filiae coniugi et Quinto Strittonis filio et Bateiae Disocni filiae coniugi Banio Verecundo bene merentibus\n
EDCS-14500379.translation: To Disocnus, son of Dulso, and to his wife Boniata, daughter of Vindon; and to Quintus, son of Stritto, and to his wife Bateia, daughter of Disocnus; for Banio Verecundus, well deserving.
EDCS-14500379.summary: An epitaph dedicated to several individuals, possibly a family group, with a formula of praise for Banio Verecundus, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-14500380.text_edition: \n Attico Rimi filio fecit Caius Iulius Maxsumus sic hic situs est annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500380.translation: Caius Julius Maxsumus made this for Atticus, son of Rimus; he lies here, aged 20.
EDCS-14500380.summary: A tombstone for Atticus, erected by Caius Julius Maxsumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500381.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetut(ae) Securitati Romanius Iuvenis et ceoniunx Aquilina fecerunt fileae! Iunillae annorum XVII Secundinius avi Iunillae Iucundianus et Mira\n
EDCS-14500381.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead and to eternal Security, Romanus Iuvenis and his wife Aquilina made this for their daughter Iunilla, aged 17. Also made by Iunilla's grandfather Iucundianus (also called Secundinius) and Mira.
EDCS-14500381.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to the underworld gods and to the memory of a young girl, Iunilla, with contributions from her parents and grandfather, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500382.text_edition: \n C Secundinius Secundus aedilis Celeiae sibi et Maternae Vibeni filiae coniugi annorum \n
EDCS-14500382.translation: C. Secundinius Secundus, aedile of Celeia, for himself and for his wife Materna, daughter of Vibenus, aged...
EDCS-14500382.summary: A dedication for a tomb, made by a local official (aedile) for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period in a provincial town.

EDCS-14500383.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tamacus Titucaes sic et Secundina coniunx et Primianus filius et Finita coniunx vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundo filio annorum X et Secundinus filius Ursula coniunx\n
EDCS-14500383.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Tamacus Titucaes, his wife Secundina, their son Primianus, and his wife Finita, made this for themselves while alive, and also for their son Secundinus, aged 10, and his wife Ursula.
EDCS-14500383.summary: A family tomb inscription where multiple generations jointly dedicated the monument for themselves and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500384.text_edition: \n Lucius Turpilius Luci libertus Secundus Peccia Luci filia Ingenua Lucio Turpilio Luci filio Probo Turpiliae Luci filiae Urbanae et sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500384.translation: Lucius Turpilius Secundus, freedman of Lucius, and Peccia Ingenua, daughter of Lucius, made this while alive for Lucius Turpilius Probus, son of Lucius, and for Turpilia Urbana, daughter of Lucius, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500384.summary: A tomb inscription set up by a freedman and his wife for themselves and for members of their patron's family, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500385.translation: To Geminus, son of Vicarius, of the family of Marcellus...
EDCS-14500385.summary: A fragmentary epitaph honoring a man named Geminus, possibly indicating his tribal affiliation or family connection, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500386.text_edition: \n a uxor pientissima XXXI et Ingenuinae\n
EDCS-14500386.translation: ...his very devoted wife, aged 31, and Ingenuina...
EDCS-14500386.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription mentioning a devoted wife and another individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500387.text_edition: \n Vercaius Ucconis filius vivus fecit sibi et Diacuni Suri filiae coniugi et Fuscae filiae annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-14500387.translation: Vercaius, son of Uccon, while alive, made this for himself and for his wife Diacuna, daughter of Surus, and for his daughter Fusca, aged 18.
EDCS-14500387.summary: A funerary monument erected by a man for himself, his wife, and his daughter, likely from a Roman provincial context during the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500388.text_edition: \n Albino Donconis filio et Comae Rustici filiae coniugi fili- fec-\n
EDCS-14500388.translation: To Albinus, son of Doncon, and Coma, daughter of Rusticus, his wife.
EDCS-14500388.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Albinus and Coma, likely a funerary monument or a votive offering.

EDCS-14500389.text_edition: \n Surus Bricionis vivus fecit sibi et Tertiae D filiae uxori et Quarto filio militi cohortis V Breucorum\n
EDCS-14500389.translation: Surus, son of Bricio, made this in his lifetime for himself, his daughter Tertia, and his son Quartus, a soldier in the 5th Cohort of Breuci.
EDCS-14500389.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Surus for his family, including his son serving in the military, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500390.text_edition: \n Reburrus I et Asseda et Gracco atobitis Goutae et Atticin\n
EDCS-14500390.translation: Reburrus, his wife Asseda, and Graccus, sons of Gouta, and Atticinus.
EDCS-14500390.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly setting up a monument or as part of a group dedication, likely ancient.

EDCS-14500393.text_edition: \n Iulius Annamus vivus sibi fecit et Iuliae Secundae uxori et Iuliae Ianuariae filiae\n
EDCS-14500393.translation: Julius Annamus made this in his lifetime for himself, his wife Julia Secunda, and his daughter Julia Ianuaria.
EDCS-14500393.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Julius Annamus for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500394.text_edition: \n Lollio Secundo Fabia et Attiae Secundinae uxori filius fecit et Attio Accepto militi alae Commagenorum\n
EDCS-14500394.translation: His son made this for Lollius Secundus, Fabia, his wife Attia Secundina, and Attius Acceptus, a soldier in the Ala Commagenorum.
EDCS-14500394.summary: This funerary inscription records a son's dedication to his parents and a brother serving in the cavalry, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500395.text_edition: \n Marco Longinio Vero quaestorio et Porciae Cai filiae Ritumarae coniugi filiae fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500395.translation: Marcus Longinius Verus, of quaestorian rank, and Porcia Ritumar, daughter of Caius, his wife, made this.
EDCS-14500395.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Longinius Verus and his wife Porcia Ritumar, likely a funerary monument or a public benefaction.

EDCS-14500396.text_edition: \n Iano Gemino Consutius Ianuarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500396.translation: Consutius Ianuarius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Janus Geminus.
EDCS-14500396.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Janus by Consutius Ianuarius.

EDCS-14500397.text_edition: \n Tert Ateva Sabin et Publio Aelio T II Mi fratri \n
EDCS-14500397.translation: To Tertia Ateva, and to Publius Aelius T. Filius, her brother.
EDCS-14500397.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary dedication from Tertia Ateva to her brother Publius Aelius, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500398.text_edition: \n Silvano Saxano Augusto sacrum Adiutor et Secundinus\n
EDCS-14500398.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Saxanus Augustus. Adiutor and Secundinus [made this].
EDCS-14500398.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Saxanus Augustus by Adiutor and Secundinus, likely a religious monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500399.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Superis et Severi filiorum annorum VIIII et VI Vetucius Tutor pater\n
EDCS-14500399.translation: To the Upper Divine Manes, and to Severus, sons aged 9 and 6. Vetucius Tutor, their father, [made this].
EDCS-14500399.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial set up by Vetucius Tutor for his two young sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500400.text_edition: \n Primus Suronis et Tertiae coniugi vivus fecit sibi\n
EDCS-14500400.translation: Primus made this for himself and his wife Tertia while alive.
EDCS-14500400.summary: A funerary monument or tomb inscription by Primus for himself and his wife Tertia, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500401.text_edition: \n Sirus Tricci filius et Primae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500401.translation: Sirus, son of Triccus, and for his wife Prima.
EDCS-14500401.summary: An epitaph or funerary marker for Sirus, son of Triccus, and his wife Prima, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500402.text_edition: \n Latobio Augusto sacrum Lucius Caesernius Avitus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500402.translation: Sacred to Latobius Augustus. Lucius Caesernius Avitus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500402.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Latobius Augustus by Lucius Caesernius Avitus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500403.text_edition: \n Latobio Augusto sacrum pro salute Nammoniae Sabinae et Iuliae Bassillae Vindonia Vera mater votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500403.translation: Sacred to Latobius Augustus. For the health of Nammonia Sabina and Iulia Bassilla, Vindonia Vera, mother, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14500403.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Latobius Augustus by Vindonia Vera for the well-being of Nammonia Sabina and Iulia Bassilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500404.text_edition: \n Caio Priminio Tertio et Litugenae sibi \n
EDCS-14500404.translation: For Gaius Priminius Tertius and Litugena, for themselves.
EDCS-14500404.summary: An inscription likely for a funerary monument or endowment for Gaius Priminius Tertius and Litugena, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500406.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Accisi filio Cisiaco et Devognatae Ationis filiae uxsori sic Florus et Valerius filii et Brogimara filia vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500406.translation: For Gaius Julius Accisi, son of Cisiacus, and Devognata, daughter of Atio, his wife. Florus and Valerius, sons, and Brogimara, daughter, made this for them while alive.
EDCS-14500406.summary: A funerary inscription set up by the children Florus, Valerius, and Brogimara for their father Gaius Julius Accisi and their mother Devognata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500407.text_edition: \n Gannico Manni filio filii et filiae vivi fecere\n
EDCS-14500407.translation: For Gannicus, son of Mannus. Sons and daughters made this for them while alive.
EDCS-14500407.summary: A funerary inscription erected by the children for Gannicus, son of Mannus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500409.text_edition: \n Vibenus Cousonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Secundae Catulli coniugi annorum LI et Successo filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500409.translation: Vibenus, son of Couson, made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Secunda, daughter of Catullus, aged 51, and for his son Successus, aged 20.
EDCS-14500409.summary: A funerary inscription by Vibenus for himself, his wife Secunda, and his son Successus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500410.text_edition: \n Vibenus Vindonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Secconiae Vetullae coniugi pientissimae annorum et Secconio Vibio filio militi cohortis II praetoriae annorum X et Secconio equis Urso Victori nnum et Seneciano Severo? --- militi? --- Vibeni filio annorum ATVRM ONI \n
EDCS-14500410.translation: Vibenus, son of Vindon, made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Secconia Vetulla, most dutiful, aged [---], and for his son Secconius Vibius, a soldier of the II Praetorian cohort, aged 10, and for Secconius Urso Victor, [---], and for Senecianus Severus?, a soldier?, son of Vibeni, aged [---].
EDCS-14500410.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibenus, his wife Secconia Vetulla, son Secconius Vibius (a soldier), and others, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500411.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Calandina viva fecit sibi et Sacronio Verino coniugi karissimo veterano legionis II Italicae custos armorum obitus annorum L\n
EDCS-14500411.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Calandina made this for herself while alive and for her dearest husband Sacronius Verinus, a veteran of the II Italica legion, custodian of arms, who died aged 50.
EDCS-14500411.summary: A funerary inscription by Iulia Calandina for herself and her husband Sacronius Verinus, a veteran soldier, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500412.text_edition: \n Cotulia Rusci filius vivus fecit sibi et Asediae Maximi filiae coniugi et Restutae filiae annorum XX et Boniae Successi filiae\n
EDCS-14500412.translation: Cotulia, son of Ruscius, made this while alive for himself and his wife Asedia, daughter of Maximinus, and his daughter Restuta, aged 20, and his daughter Bonia, daughter of Successus.
EDCS-14500412.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Cotulia for himself and his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500414.text_edition: \n Secundinus Pallonis filius et Cupita Nemetonis filia et Secundina filia annorum XXV et Nertomariae Avitae nepti annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500414.translation: Secundinus, son of Pallon, and Cupita, daughter of Nemetos, and their daughter Secundina, aged 25, and their granddaughter Nertomariae, aged 30.
EDCS-14500414.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating members of a family, including at least three generations, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500415.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Sextius Masculinus votum solvit\n
EDCS-14500415.translation: Sextius Masculinus fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered God Mithras.
EDCS-14500415.summary: A votive inscription to the god Mithras, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500416.text_edition: \n Metilius Maximinus vir egregius omnibus muneribus functus et Aurelia Vera coniux Metilii Aelianus Dumviranus Lucc3 Simplicianus Metilianus Victorianus Severa filii ex suis posuerunt curante Aristonico fratre \n
EDCS-14500416.translation: Metilius Maximinus, an outstanding man who held all offices, and his wife Aurelia Vera, and his children Aelianus Dumviranus, Luccius Simplicianus, Metilianus, and Victorianus Severa, set up this monument from their own resources, with the care of their brother Aristo.
EDCS-14500416.summary: A funerary monument erected by a family, honoring Metilius Maximinus, who held various public offices, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500417.text_edition: \n Albanus Hespani servus vivus fecit sibi et Surae uxori annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-14500417.translation: Albanus, slave of Hespanius, made this while alive for himself and his wife Sura, aged 35.
EDCS-14500417.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a slave for himself and his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500418.text_edition: \n Maximus Antoniorum libertus et Vercilla vivi fecerunt sibi et Nundinae filiae annorum V et Urso filio annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500418.translation: Maximus, freedman of the Antonii, and Vercilla made this while alive for themselves and their daughter Nundina, aged 5, and their son Urso, aged 30.
EDCS-14500418.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a freedman and his wife for themselves and their children, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500419.text_edition: \n Ulpia Firmina annorum triginta Marcus Ulpius Firminus et Connonia Statuta vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpio Primiano filio annorum XIII\n
EDCS-14500419.translation: Ulpia Firmina, aged thirty, and Marcus Ulpius Firminus and Connonia Statuta made this while alive for themselves and their son Ulpius Primianus, aged 13.
EDCS-14500419.summary: A funerary inscription set up by parents for themselves and their son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500420.text_edition: \n Carvoniae Augustae sacrum pro salute Cnaei Atili Iuliani\n
EDCS-14500420.translation: Sacred to Carvonia Augusta, for the health of Gnaeus Atilius Julianus.
EDCS-14500420.summary: A dedication to the deified Augusta Carvonia for the well-being of Gnaeus Atilius Julianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500421.text_edition: \n fili- Claudia Celeia --- o IIvir- iure dicundo Marci filio Materno --- Trosi- Paul--- et --- Claud-ae Eunae? ---\n
EDCS-14500421.translation: For the son [---] Claudia Celeia [---] duovir with judicial authority, son of Marcus Maternos [---] Trosius Paul--- and the wife [---] Claudius Euna?
EDCS-14500421.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication or a funerary monument for a magistrate and his family, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500422.text_edition: \n Atranti Augustae sacrum Fortunatus Cai Antoni Rufi procuratoris Augusti servus vilicus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500422.translation: Sacred to Augusta Atrebatia. Fortunatus, slave of Caius Antonius Rufus, procurator of Augustus, the steward, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-14500422.summary: A votive inscription by a freedman steward to Augusta Atrebatia and likely an emperor, from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500423.text_edition: \n vantit Regius Regalis Iulia Aquilina ex voto\n
EDCS-14500423.translation: Vantitus Regius Aquilina, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-14500423.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vantitus Regius Aquilina by an unknown person, likely a private individual fulfilling a vow around the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500424.text_edition: \n Hecate Augustae Aurelii Asclepiodotus et Lucius pro salute sua et suorum votum solverunt libentes maximo\n
EDCS-14500424.translation: Aurelius Asclepiodotus and Lucius, fulfilling their vow with great willingness for their own health and that of their families, paid their vows to August Hecate.
EDCS-14500424.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aurelius Asclepiodotus and Lucius to August Hecate for the health of themselves and their families, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500425.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Antonius Iulianus procurator portorii publici X votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500425.translation: Gaius Antonius Julianus, procurator of the public customs revenue of X, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-14500425.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Gaius Antonius Julianus, a procurator of customs duties, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500426.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Eutyches Iuliorum conductorum portorii publici servus |contrascriptor stationis Boiodurensis ex vikario! Benigni vilici stationis Atrantinae aram cum signo Lunae ex voto posuit prosedente Tito Claudio Senillo\n
EDCS-14500426.translation: Eutyches, a slave of the contractors of the public customs revenue, a scribe at the Boiodurum station, from the deputy of Benignus, the supervisor of the Atrantina station, set up an altar with the image of Luna to the Unconquered God Mithras as a vow, with Titus Claudius Senillus presiding.
EDCS-14500426.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar by Eutyches, a slave working in customs administration, to the god Mithras, likely dating to the late Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500427.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum Abascantus Antoni Rufi servus scrutator votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500427.translation: Abascantus, slave of Antonius Rufus, a scrutator, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Father Liber.
EDCS-14500427.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Abascantus, a slave and official, to Father Liber, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500428.text_edition: \n Noreiae Augustae et Honori stationis Atrantinae Bellicus et Eutyches |contrascriptores stationis eiusdem ex voto\n
EDCS-14500428.translation: Bellicus and Eutyches, scribes of the same Atrantina station, as a vow to August Noreia and the Honor of the Atrantina station.
EDCS-14500428.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Bellicus and Eutyches, scribes at the Atrantina station, to Noreia and Honor, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500429.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilari fili qui annos vixit III Secundianus contrascriptor et Rufilla parentes filio faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500429.translation: Secundianus and Rufilla, parents, had this made for their son Hilarius, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-14500429.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Hilarius, erected by his parents Secundianus and Rufilla, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500431.text_edition: \n mus Cai us fecit nae co imi annorum \n
EDCS-14500431.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely recording a construction or dedication by Gaius, with incomplete age information, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500432.text_edition: \n Maronius Marcellinus decurio Celeiae vivus fecit sibi et filio Maronio Martino @(obito) annorum XXX et Maronio Marcelliano et Maronio Marcellino nepoti @(obito) annorum XXXIII\n
EDCS-14500432.translation: Maronius Marcellinus, decurion of Celeia, made this himself while alive for himself and for his son Maronius Martinus, who died at the age of 30, and for Maronius Marcellianus and Maronius Marcellinus, his grandsons, who died at the age of 33.
EDCS-14500432.summary: This inscription records a self-dedicated tomb by Maronius Marcellinus, a decurion, for himself and his deceased son and grandsons, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500433.text_edition: \n Viatorinus annorum L Dincen annorum LXX Avitianus Aviti vivus fecit sibi et suis coniugi Viatorinae et Marcius Secundinus et Maximae uxori annorum XXX Maximianus Maximi annorum X\n
EDCS-14500433.translation: Viatorinus, aged 50, and Dincen, aged 70, Avitianus [son] of Avitus, made this himself while alive for himself and his family: his wife Viatorina, and Marcius Secundinus and Maxima his wife, aged 30, and Maximianus son of Maximus, aged 10.
EDCS-14500433.summary: This inscription records a self-dedicated tomb by Viatorinus and Dincen for themselves and various family members, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500434.translation: To Viator, son of Adiec and Adium.
EDCS-14500434.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Viator, the son of Adiec and Adium, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-14500435.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Vibius Sabinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae annorum LXV P Paulinus vivus\n
EDCS-14500435.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Vibius Sabinus, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, aged 65. P. Paulinus [erected this] while alive.
EDCS-14500435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vibius Sabinus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, set up by P. Paulinus.

EDCS-14500436.text_edition: \n Tutor Ioiti et Piaii Coudo<m=NI>ari Acceptus Pi Vibius Nertomari LX Tertius Vibi filius annorum XXV Exortus Secundinus annorum V Antia Suri annorum XXXV \n
EDCS-14500436.translation: To Tutor, son of Ioitus and Piaius. Coudo [c=NI] ari Acceptus, son of Pi Vibius Nertomarus, aged 60. Tertius Vibi, aged 25. Exortus Secundinus, aged 5. Antia Suri, aged 35.
EDCS-14500436.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly indicating family members or deceased individuals commemorated on a monument, with ages ranging from 5 to 60.

EDCS-14500437.text_edition: \n Seccio Maximi filius annorum IX Anecila OAMLIEA \n
EDCS-14500437.translation: Seccio, son of Maximu, aged 9. Anecila OAMLIEA [---].
EDCS-14500437.summary: This inscription likely commemorates two individuals, Seccio and Anecila, with Seccio's age given as 9.

EDCS-14500438.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sundinus intona vivi fecerunt VDP filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500438.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sundinus and his living parents made [this]. For their son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500438.summary: This is a funerary dedication where living parents set up the monument for their son who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-14500439.text_edition: \n Savo et Adsallutae votum solvit libens merito Secundio\n
EDCS-14500439.translation: Savo and Adsallutae. Secundio willingly and deservedly fulfilled [their] vow.
EDCS-14500439.summary: This inscription records Secundio fulfilling a vow made to the deities Savo and Adsallutae.

EDCS-14500440.text_edition: \n Adsallutae Augustae sacrum Gaius Caecina Faustinus\n
EDCS-14500440.translation: Sacred to Adsallutae Augusta. Gaius Caecina Faustinus [erected this].
EDCS-14500440.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred object or place to the deity Adsallutae Augusta by Gaius Caecina Faustinus.

EDCS-14500441.text_edition: \n Adsallutae Augustae sacrum Ocellio Castrici Marcelli servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500441.translation: Sacred to Adsallutae Augusta. Ocellio, slave of Castricius Marcellus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled [his] vow.
EDCS-14500441.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ocellio, a slave of Castricius Marcellus, to the deity Adsallutae Augusta.

EDCS-14500442.text_edition: \n Neptuno Augusto sacrum Caius Castricius Optatus solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500442.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Caius Castricius Optatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled [his] vow.
EDCS-14500442.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred object or place to Neptune Augustus by Caius Castricius Optatus.

EDCS-14500443.text_edition: \n Savo et Adsallutae sacrum Publius Antonius Secundus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500443.translation: Sacred to Savo and Adsallutae. Publius Antonius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled [his] vow.
EDCS-14500443.summary: This inscription records Publius Antonius Secundus fulfilling a vow made to the deities Savo and Adsallutae.

EDCS-14500444.text_edition: \n Bassus Quincti libertus vivus fecit sibi et Catae uxori annorum LXV \n
EDCS-14500444.translation: Bassus, freedman of Quintus, made this while alive for himself and his wife Cata, aged 65.
EDCS-14500444.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Bassus for himself and his wife Cata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500447.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibius Secundus veteranus fecit vivus sibi et suis annorum C Cerula Tutoris annorum LXXXX Vibia Avita annorum XXII Valeria Marcellina obita annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500447.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibius Secundus, a veteran, made this while alive for himself and his family: Cerula, aged 100, wife of Tutor; Vibia Avita, aged 22; Valeria Marcellina, who died aged 22.
EDCS-14500447.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Vibius Secundus, a veteran, for his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500448.text_edition: \n Publius Albinius Antonius aedilis Claudiae Celeiae et Terentia Finita vivi fecerunt sibi et Publio Albinio Finito filio quaestori Claudiae Celeiae annorum XXXX et Publio Albinio Marcellino filio annorum L et Publio Albinio Marciano nepoti quaestori annorum XXX et Iantullae nurui annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500448.translation: Publius Albinius Antonius, aedile of Claudius Celeia, and Terentia Finita made this while alive for themselves and for Publius Albinius Finitus, their son, quaestor of Claudius Celeia, aged 40, and Publius Albinius Marcellinus, their son, aged 50, and Publius Albinius Marcianus, their grandson, quaestor, aged 30, and Iantulla, their daughter-in-law, aged 20.
EDCS-14500448.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Publius Albinius Antonius and Terentia Finita for themselves and several family members, including a quaestor, indicating a family of some status, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500449.text_edition: \n Barbius Cupitus annorum LX Latina Saturnini annorum LV Marina Barbi annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-14500449.translation: Barbius Cupitus, aged 60; Latina, daughter of Saturninus, aged 55; Marina, daughter of Barbius, aged 40.
EDCS-14500449.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing three individuals with their ages, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500450.text_edition: \n Diastumarus Ibliendi filius annorum C Coma Chilonis filia annorum LXXV Viator filius annorum L Crispus filius annorum XXXXV Soa filia annorum \n
EDCS-14500450.translation: Diastumarus, son of Ibliendus, aged 100; Coma, daughter of Chilo, aged 75; Viator, son, aged 45; Crispus, son, aged 45; Soa, daughter, [age illegible].
EDCS-14500450.summary: This funerary inscription records the names and ages of several individuals, likely from a Roman provincial context, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500451.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Uxellimo Serandius Verinus decurio Celeiae poni iussit Pompeia Ursula eius cum Urso filio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500451.translation: Serandius Verinus, a decurion of Celeia, ordered this to be set up to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Uxellimus; Pompeia Ursula, his wife, together with their son Urso, fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-14500451.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Serandius Verinus and fulfilled by his wife Pompeia Ursula and son Urso, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500452.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis Fructus Quinti Sabini Verani conductoris publici portorii servus vilicus posuit\n
EDCS-14500452.translation: Fructus, a slave and overseer of the tax collector Quintus Sabinus Veranius, set this up to the August Nymphs.
EDCS-14500452.summary: This is a dedication to the August Nymphs by Fructus, a slave and overseer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500453.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis Matius Finitus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500453.translation: Matius Finitus fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly to the August Nymphs.
EDCS-14500453.summary: This is a votive inscription to the August Nymphs by Matius Finitus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500454.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum Caius Veponius Phoebus et Felix eius votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14500454.translation: Caius Veponius Phoebus and his wife Felix fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly to the August Nymphs.
EDCS-14500454.summary: This is a votive inscription to the August Nymphs by Caius Veponius Phoebus and his wife Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500456.text_edition: \n Caius Baebius Acceptus annorum LXX Quarta Damionis filia nnorum L heres fecit et Caio Baebio Vitellio filio\n
EDCS-14500456.translation: Caius Baebius Acceptus, aged 70, and his wife Quarta, daughter of Damio, aged 50, made this for themselves and for their son Caius Baebius Vitellius.
EDCS-14500456.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Baebius Acceptus and his wife Quarta for themselves and their son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500457.text_edition: \n Donata liberta Titi Postumi viva fecit sibi et \n
EDCS-14500457.translation: Donata, freedwoman of Titus Postumius, made this while alive for herself.
EDCS-14500457.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a freedwoman for herself, likely during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500458.text_edition: \n Cupitus Aviti filius vivus fecit sibi et Boniatae Secundi filiae annorum LXV hic sita est Antonio filio annorum XX Castus Cupiti annorum LXXV Iustus Cupiti annorum LXXV\n
EDCS-14500458.translation: Cupitus, son of Avitus, made this while alive for himself and for Bonista, daughter of Secundus, aged 65, who is buried here, and for Antonio, aged 20, and Castus Cupiti, aged 75, and Iustus Cupiti, aged 75.
EDCS-14500458.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased and those for whom the monument was erected by Cupitus, son of Avitus, who also made it for himself while alive.

EDCS-14500459.text_edition: \n Saturio Decimi Castrici Sabini servus vivus fecit sibi et Septumae sic coniugi nnorum LX\n
EDCS-14500459.translation: Saturio, slave of Decimus Castricius Sabinus, made this while alive for himself and for Septuma, his wife, aged 60.
EDCS-14500459.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased and his wife, for whom the monument was erected by Saturio, a slave, while alive.

EDCS-14500460.text_edition: \n Seubrinubi Proba viva fecit et Surato filio annorum XXI LL II \n
EDCS-14500460.translation: Seubrinubi Proba made this while alive for herself and for her son Suratus, aged 21.
EDCS-14500460.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased and her son, for whom the monument was erected by Seubrinubi Proba while alive.

EDCS-14500461.text_edition: \n Imperatore Antonino IIII et Balbino consulibus Celeiae Augustae Publius Aelius Verinus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500461.translation: During the consulship of Emperor Antoninus IIII and Balbinus, Publius Aelius Verinus, a *beneficiarius consularis*, willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, as was fitting.
EDCS-14500461.summary: This is a dedication recording a vow fulfilled by a *beneficiarius consularis* during the consulship of Antoninus IIII and Balbinus.

EDCS-14500462.text_edition: \n Dis deabusque omnibus Bellia Sorana votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500462.translation: For all the gods and goddesses, Bellia Sorana willingly fulfilled her vow, as was fitting.
EDCS-14500462.summary: This is a dedication recording a vow willingly fulfilled by Bellia Sorana to all gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14500463.text_edition: \n Fortunae curatio oppidana de sua pecunia faciundum sic curavit Caius Attius [Caius SI------\n
EDCS-14500463.translation: The town officials had the care of Fortune and had it made at their own expense; Caius Attius saw to it, Caius [Son of...]...
EDCS-14500463.summary: This is a public works record concerning the care of Fortune, funded by town officials, with Caius Attius overseeing the project.

EDCS-14500464.text_edition: \n Fortunae Stabili pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severii sic Pertinacis et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum [et Publi Septimi Getae] Ulpius Valerianus |(centurio) legionis X geminae\n
EDCS-14500464.translation: For Fortunatus Stabilius, for the safety of our Lords Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustorum [and Publius Septimius Geta], Ulpius Valerianus, a centurion of the tenth Gemina legion.
EDCS-14500464.summary: This is a dedication to Fortunatus Stabilius for the safety of the emperors, set up by Ulpius Valerianus, a centurion of the tenth Gemina legion, likely during the reign of Severus, Caracalla, and Geta.

EDCS-14500465.text_edition: \n Genio Anigemio cultores eius votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14500465.translation: Its worshippers willingly fulfilled their vow to the Genius of Anigemium, as was fitting.
EDCS-14500465.summary: This is a dedication recording a vow willingly fulfilled by worshippers to the Genius of Anigemium.

EDCS-14500466.text_edition: \n Genio Augusti et Laribus Publius Ursinius Maturus et Cassia Censorina\n
EDCS-14500466.translation: Publius Ursinius Maturus and Cassia Censorina [dedicated this] to the Genius of Augustus and the Lares.
EDCS-14500466.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of Augustus and the Lares by Publius Ursinius Maturus and Cassia Censorina.

EDCS-14500469.text_edition: \n Iovi Depulsori sacrum Aurelius Paterclus beneficiarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500469.translation: Sacred to Jupiter the Repeller; Aurelius Paterclus, a *beneficiarius*, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500469.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Depulsor by Aurelius Paterclus, a soldier serving as a *beneficiarius*, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500470.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Adnamius Flavinus beneficiarius Ulpi Victoris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500470.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Adnamius Flavinus, *beneficiarius* of Ulpius Victor, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500470.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Adnamius Flavinus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Ulpius Victor, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500471.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Adnamius Flavinus beneficiarius Useni Secundi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500471.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Adnamius Flavinus, *beneficiarius* of Usenius Secundus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500471.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Adnamius Flavinus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Usenius Secundus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500472.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Antonius Maximus beneficiarius Quinti Caecili Redditi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500472.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Antonius Maximus, *beneficiarius* of Quintus Caecilius Reddiditus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500472.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antonius Maximus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Quintus Caecilius Reddiditus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500473.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Anonius Valens beneficiarius Flavi Titiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500473.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Caius Anonius Valens, *beneficiarius* of Flavius Titianus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500473.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Anonius Valens, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Flavius Titianus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500474.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Augustanus beneficiarius Gai Rasini Silonis procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500474.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Augustanus, *beneficiarius* of Gaius Rasinius Silon, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500474.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Augustanus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Gaius Rasinius Silon, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500475.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Kaninius sic Lucanus beneficiarius Usieni Secundi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito Tertullo et Sacerdote consulibus\n
EDCS-14500475.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Quintus Kaninius, known as Lucanus, *beneficiarius* of Usenius Secundus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos.
EDCS-14500475.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Kaninius Lucanus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Usenius Secundus, dated to the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500476.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Quintus Crescentius Marcellus beneficiarius Quinti Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500476.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Quintus Crescentius Marcellus, *beneficiarius* of Quintus Lisinius Sabinus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500476.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Crescentius Marcellus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Quintus Lisinius Sabinus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500477.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Dubitatus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500477.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest; Titus Flavius Dubitatus, *beneficiarius* of Lisinius Sabinus, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500477.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Dubitatus, a *beneficiarius* under the procurator Lisinius Sabinus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500478.text_edition: \n Caius Rufius Apolaustus et Iulia Successa vivi sibi fecerunt Iulia Firmina annorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-14500478.translation: Caius Rufius Apolaustus and Iulia Successa made this for themselves while alive; Iulia Firmina, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-14500478.summary: This inscription records a family burial plot made for Caius Rufius Apolaustus and Iulia Successa, and also notes the burial of Iulia Firmina, aged 20, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500479.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Fuscinius Catullus beneficiarius Ulpi Victoris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500479.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Fuscinius Catullus, aide to Ulpius Victor, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500479.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Gaius Fuscinius Catullus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500480.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gemellius Adiutor beneficiarius Drusi Proculi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500480.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gemellius Adiutor, aide to Drusus Proculus, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500480.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Gemellius Adiutor, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500482.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucilius Finitus beneficiarius Flavi Titiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500482.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucilius Finitus, aide to Flavius Titianus, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500482.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Lucilius Finitus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500483.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Masculinius Successus beneficiarius Cai Antisti Auspicis procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500483.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Masculinius Successus, aide to Gaius Antistius Auspex, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500483.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Masculinius Successus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500484.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Messius Frontinus beneficiarius Cai Censori Nigri procuratoris Augusti\n
EDCS-14500484.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Messius Frontinus, aide to Gaius Censor Niger, imperial procurator.
EDCS-14500484.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Lucius Messius Frontinus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE; the vow fulfillment is not explicitly stated but implied.

EDCS-14500485.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Mustius Tettianus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500485.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Mustius Tettianus, aide to Lisinius Sabinus, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500485.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Gaius Mustius Tettianus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500486.text_edition: \n Eponae Augustae sacrum Caius Mustius Tettianus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500486.translation: Sacred to Augusta Epona, Gaius Mustius Tettianus, aide to Lisinius Sabinus, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500486.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Epona by Gaius Mustius Tettianus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500487.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo onius Primus beneficiarius Plauti Caesiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500487.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Onius Primus, aide to Plautius Caesianus, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500487.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Onius Primus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500488.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Sextius Pullaenius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito [Imperatore Commodo VII] et Pertinace consulibus\n
EDCS-14500488.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Quintus Sextius Pullaenius, aide of the consular legate of the II Italian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Emperor Commodus for the 7th time and Pertinax.
EDCS-14500488.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Quintus Sextius Pullaenius, an aide to a consular legate of the II Italian Legion, dated to the consulship of Commodus and Pertinax (192 CE).

EDCS-14500489.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Surus beneficiarius Memmi Apollinaris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500489.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Surus, aide to Memmius Apollinaris, imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500489.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Surus, an aide to an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500490.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Acilianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500490.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Acilianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italian Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500490.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter by Marcus Ulpius Acilianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica.

EDCS-14500491.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Crescens beneficiarius Cai Censori Nigri procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500491.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Crescens, a beneficiarius of Caius Censorius Niger, procurator of the Emperor, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500491.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter by Marcus Ulpius Crescens, a beneficiarius of a procurator Augusti.

EDCS-14500492.translation: To Caecilius Iuventianus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-14500492.summary: A dedication to Caecilius Iuventianus, a procurator Augusti.

EDCS-14500493.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pos(uerunt) Caius Bellicius Ingenuus IIvir Claudiae Celeiae et Aurelia Aurelia coniunx pro salute sua suorumque omnium\n
EDCS-14500493.translation: Gaius Bellicius Ingenuus, as duumvir of Claudia Celeia, and his wife Aurelia Aurelia, set this up for their own health and that of all their family.
EDCS-14500493.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Gaius Bellicius Ingenuus and his wife for their family's well-being, likely dating to his tenure as duumvir.

EDCS-14500494.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calcinius Tertianus conductor publici portorii tribunus militum legionis XX Valeriae Victricis cum Petronia Tertia uxore pro se et filiis votum solvit\n
EDCS-14500494.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Calcinius Tertianus, contractor of the public customs revenue and tribune of the soldiers of the Twentieth Legion Valerian and Victorious, with his wife Petronia Tertia, for himself and his sons, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500494.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter by Gaius Calcinius Tertianus, a publican and military tribune, and his wife.

EDCS-14500495.text_edition: \n Pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Arubiano et Celeiae sanctae Vibius Cassius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito Laeto II et Ceriale consulibus\n
EDCS-14500495.translation: For the health of our lord Emperor Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, to Jupiter Best and Greatest the Protector, and to the holy Arubianus and Celeia, Vibius Cassius Victorinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italian Legion, pious and loyal, Antoninian, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, under the consulship of Laetus and Cerialis.
EDCS-14500495.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter, Arubianus, and Celeia, dedicated by Vibius Cassius Victorinus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, for the emperor's health during the consulship of Laetus and Cerialis.

EDCS-14500496.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali et dis deabusque omnibus Titus Mattius Hecato pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500496.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Culminalis, and to all the gods and goddesses, Titus Mattius Hecato, for his own health and that of his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500496.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Culminalis and other deities by Titus Mattius Hecato.

EDCS-14500497.text_edition: \n [Pro salute Auggustorum] nnostrorum Iovi Optimo Maximo et Celeiae sanctae Caius Licinius Bellicianus beneficiarius legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Gentiano et Basso consulibus Idibus Decembribus\n
EDCS-14500497.translation: For the health of our Emperors, to Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the holy Celeia, Gaius Licinius Bellicianus, a beneficiarius of the Second Italian Legion, pious and loyal, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, on the Ides of December, during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus.
EDCS-14500497.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter and Celeia, dedicated by Gaius Licinius Bellicianus, a beneficiarius of Legio II Italica, for the emperor's health during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus.

EDCS-14500498.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Celeiae et Noreiae Sanctae Rufius Senilis beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500498.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the holy Celeia and Noreia, Rufius Senilis, a beneficiarius consularis, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500498.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter, Celeia, and Noreia by Rufius Senilis, a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-14500499.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabusque omnibus Marcus Aurelius Iustus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Praesente et Extricato consulibus\n
EDCS-14500499.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to all the gods and goddesses, Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italian Legion, pious and loyal, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-14500499.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter and all deities by Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.

EDCS-14500501.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et cultoribusltoribus Caius Rufius aram cum? scr(ipturam) donum dedit Sentonius Spectatus Petronius Marcus Mamilius Respectinus Antonius Saturninus Helvius Iustus Turravius Castor Auro Iantulli Boto Noni Verus Saturnini Sentonius Albucius Pompeius Surus Publicius Callistus Mamilius Crescens Petronius Celer Rufius Herma Claudius Maximus Restutus Saturnini Iantullus Orgetei Maximianus Severi \n
EDCS-14500501.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Rufius dedicated this altar with an inscription as a gift; Sentonius Spectatus, Petronius Marcus, Mamilius Respectinus, Antonius Saturninus, Helvius Iustus, Turravius Castor, Auro Iantulli, Boto Noni, Verus Saturnini, Sentonius Albucius, Pompeius Surus, Publicius Callistus, Mamilius Crescens, Petronius Celer, Rufius Herma, Claudius Maximus, Restutus Saturnini, Iantullus Orgetei, Maximianus Severi.
EDCS-14500501.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Rufius, followed by a list of donors and subscribers, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500503.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Eponae et Celeiae sanctae Marcus S I\n
EDCS-14500503.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the holy Epona and Celeia, Marcus S [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500503.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Epona, and Celeia, made by Marcus S., likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500505.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae Blaundiae Cnaeus Pompeius Iustinus decurio et IIvir Claudiae Celeiae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500505.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Iustinus, decurion and duovir of Claudia Celeia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Great Mother of the Gods, Blaundia.
EDCS-14500505.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gnaeus Pompeius Iustinus to the Great Mother of the Gods (Magna Mater), Blaundia, while he held civic offices in Claudia Celeia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500507.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum et cultoribus eius Iulius Lucifer titulum cum scriptura testamento dedit Viator Gaetuli Avitus Magni Spectatus Sexti Secundinus Secundi Finitus Magni Crescens Nertomari Maximus Quinti Calvenius Martialis Aemilius Spectatus Iulius Maximianus Calvenius Avitus Avitus Secundi Maximus Viatoris Maximus Terti Iulius Secundi Atilius Firmus Cassius Senilis Secundinus Maximi Saturninus Viatoris Praesens Respecti\n
EDCS-14500507.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus and his worshippers. Iulius Lucifer dedicated this inscription by his will; Viator Gaetuli, Avitus Magni, Spectatus Sexti, Secundinus Secundi, Finitus Magni, Maximus Quinti, Calvenius Martialis, Aemilius Spectatus, Iulius Maximianus, Calvenius Avitus, Avitus Secundi, Maximus Viatoris, Maximus Terti, Iulius Secundi, Atilius Firmus, Cassius Senilis, Secundinus Maximi, Saturninus Viatoris Praesens Respecti [dedicated].
EDCS-14500507.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury Augustus and his worshippers, made by Iulius Lucifer through his will, followed by a list of subscribers, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500511.text_edition: \n ProPro Quinti Rufi Atticiani Quintus Rufius Hiero votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500511.translation: Quintus Rufius Hiero willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for Quintus Rufus Atticianus.
EDCS-14500511.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Rufius Hiero on behalf of Quintus Rufus Atticianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500513.text_edition: \n Imperatori Vespasiano Caesari Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori XX consuli VIIII patri patriae Caius Domitius Florus testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-14500513.translation: Gaius Domitius Florus ordered this to be made by his will for the Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power X, imperator XX, consul VIIII, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-14500513.summary: This inscription records a testamentary commission by Gaius Domitius Florus for a monument or dedication to Emperor Vespasian, likely from the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-14500514.text_edition: \n Imperatori divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Caesari Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V Caius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus praefectus cohortis VI Raetorum tribunus militum legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis codicillo fieri iussit\n
EDCS-14500514.translation: Gaius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus, prefect of the VI cohort of Raeti, military tribune of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, by codicil ordered this to be made for the Emperor, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul V.
EDCS-14500514.summary: This inscription records a testamentary commission by Gaius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus, a military officer, for Emperor Trajan, likely from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500521.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Constantio clementissimo adque victori Augusto Martinianus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Norici mediterranei devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-14500521.translation: Martiniana, a most accomplished man, governor of the province of Noricum mediterraneum, devoted to the divinity and majesty of our lord Flavius Constantius, most clement and victorious Augustus.
EDCS-14500521.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Martinianus, governor of Noricum mediterraneum, to the emperor Flavius Constantius, likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-14500522.summary: This very short inscription records a dedication by 'Victor the triumphant', its context and date are uncertain.

EDCS-14500523.text_edition: \n Tito Vario Titi filio Clementi Claudia Celeia procuratori Augusti provinciarum Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto alae Britannicae miliariae praefecto auxiliariorum tempore expeditionis in Tingitaniam missorum praefecto equitum alae II Pannoniorum tribuno legionis XXX Ulpiae praefecto cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae Valerius Urbanus Licinius Secundinus decuriones alares provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis\n
EDCS-14500523.translation: Valerius Urbanus and Licinius Secundinus, decurions of the cavalry units of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, [dedicated this] for Titus Varius, son of Titus, Clemens, Claudia Celeia, procurator of the provinces of Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia, prefect of the ala Britannica miliaria, prefect of auxiliary troops sent during the expedition to Tingitana, prefect of cavalry of the II Pannoniorum ala, tribune of the XXX Ulpia legion, prefect of the II Gallorum Macedonica cohort.
EDCS-14500523.summary: This inscription records a dedication by two decurions for Titus Varius Clemens, listing his extensive military and administrative career, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500524.text_edition: \n Tito Vario Clementi procuratori provinciarum Belgicae Germaniae superioris Germaniae inferioris Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto equitum alae nnae miliariae praefecto auxiliorum in Mauretaniam Tingitanam ex Hispania missorum praefecto equitum alae II nnum tribuno militum legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis praefecto cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae cives Romani ex Italia et aliis provinciis in Raetia consistentes\n
EDCS-14500524.translation: To Titus Varius Clemens, procurator of the provinces of Belgica, Upper Germany, Lower Germany, Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia, prefect of the equestrian ala nna milliaria, prefect of auxiliary troops dispatched to Mauretania Tingitana from Hispania, prefect of the equestrian ala II of Germans, tribune of the XXX Ulpia Victrix legion, prefect of the II Gallorum Macedonica cohort, for Roman citizens residing in Raetia from Italy and other provinces.
EDCS-14500524.summary: This inscription lists the extensive administrative and military career of Titus Varius Clemens, likely erected by Roman citizens under his jurisdiction in Raetia.

EDCS-14500527.text_edition: \n Tito Vario Clementi ab epistulis Augustorum procuratori provinciarum Belgicae et utriusque Germaniae Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto equitum alae Britannicae miliariae praefecto auxiliorum in Mauretaniam Tingitanam ex Hispania missorum praefecto equitum alae II Pannoniorum tribuno legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis praefecto praef(ecto) cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae civitas Treverorum praesidi optimo\n
EDCS-14500527.translation: To Titus Varius Clemens, ab epistulis Augustorum, procurator of the provinces of Belgica, both Germanies, Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia, prefect of the equestrian ala Britannica milliaria, prefect of auxiliary troops dispatched to Mauretania Tingitana from Hispania, prefect of the equestrian ala II Pannoniorum, tribune of the XXX Ulpia Victrix legion, prefect of the II Gallorum Macedonica cohort, to the most excellent governor, from the citizenship of the Treveri.
EDCS-14500527.summary: This inscription honors Titus Varius Clemens, detailing his various high-ranking administrative and military posts, and is dedicated by the Treveri, likely during his tenure as governor.

EDCS-14500528.text_edition: \n nus Cato us Cornelius Saturninus S patroni Causarum provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis\n
EDCS-14500528.translation: To Marcus Cato and Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, patrons of the causes of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-14500528.summary: This inscription dedicates the monument to Marcus Cato and Marcus Cornelius Saturninus, who served as patrons for legal cases in Mauretania Caesariensis.

EDCS-14500529.text_edition: \n Autonius RSC Velina Aquileia et Antonius Respectus amico optimo\n
EDCS-14500529.translation: From Autonius RSC of the Velina tribe, from Aquileia, and Antonius Respectus, to their most excellent friend.
EDCS-14500529.summary: This inscription is a dedication from two friends, Autonius and Antonius Respectus, to a third, highly esteemed friend.

EDCS-14500530.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Iustino militi legionis II Italicae @(obito) in expeditione Daccisca sic annorum XXIII Aurelius Verinus veteranus et Messia Quartina parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500530.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Verinus, a veteran, and Messia Quartina, his parents, made this for Aurelius Iustinus, a soldier of the II Italica legion, who died on the Dacian expedition, aged twenty-three.
EDCS-14500530.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by parents for their son, Aurelius Iustinus, a soldier who died on campaign in Dacia.

EDCS-14500531.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Saturnino veterano @(obito) annorum XLV et Aureliae Secundinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XXXV et Aurelio Secundino fratri @(obito) annorum VII Decimia Quaeta sic avia Aurelius Crescentinus parentibus carissimis\n
EDCS-14500531.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Decimia Quaeta, his grandmother, had this made for her dearest parents, Aurelius Saturninus, a veteran, aged forty-five, and Aurelia Secundina, his wife, who died at thirty-five, and Aurelius Secundinus, their brother, who died at seven.
EDCS-14500531.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a grandmother for her son, his wife, and their young son, all deceased.

EDCS-14500532.text_edition: \n Lucius Braetius Luci filius Publilia Verona veteranus legionis VIII Augustae nnorum LXX hic situs est f f Magiona Verulli filia annorum LXXV hic sita est f f\n
EDCS-14500532.translation: Lucius Braetius, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, a veteran of the VIII Augusta legion, aged seventy, lies here. Magiona Verulli, daughter of Verullus, aged seventy-five, lies here. Both were made by their daughters.
EDCS-14500532.summary: This is a tombstone for a veteran of the VIII Augusta legion and his wife, with both individuals and their respective daughters, who erected the monument, identified.

EDCS-14500533.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cupitius Cupitianus |(obitus) annorum LX et Baebia Maximina coniux |(obita) annorum L Flavius Decoratianus beneficiarius et Cupitia Iuliana coniux vivi parentibus carissimis faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500533.translation: Flavius Decoratianus, a *beneficiarius*, and his wife Cupitia Iuliana, had this made for their dearest parents, Cupitius Cupitianus, who died at sixty, and Baebia Maximina, his wife, who died at fifty.
EDCS-14500533.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a son and his wife for their deceased parents, with one of the sons holding a military position.

EDCS-14500534.text_edition: \n Ennio Secundiano equiti cohortis VII praetoriae annorum XXV et Quinto Ennio Apollonio annorum LX et Valeriae Maximillae annorum L\n
