EDCS-14500534.translation: To Ennius Secundianus, a cavalryman of the VII Praetorian cohort, aged twenty-five, and to Quintus Ennius Apollonius, aged sixty, and to Valeria Maximilla, aged fifty.
EDCS-14500534.summary: This inscription records the deaths and likely honors three individuals: a Praetorian guardsman and two older people, possibly family members.

EDCS-14500535.text_edition: \n Gaviae Publi filiae Virunae Lucius Dindius Respectus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae Publius Dindius Speratus |(centurio) speculatorum Augusti filii matri indulgentissimae et sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500535.translation: Lucius Dindius Respectus, *centurion* of the XIII Gemina legion, and Publius Dindius Speratus, *centurion* of the *speculatorum Augusti*, made this for their most loving mother, Gavia Viruna, daughter of Publius, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500535.summary: This funerary monument was erected by two sons, both centurions, for their mother and also for themselves, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500536.text_edition: \n et Caio Iunio Nicandro filio nnorum XXXVIII Mattiae Publi filiae Verinae nnorum XXIIII et Caio Iunio Isaeo veterano ex decurione alae I Commagenorum annorum LX et Ulpiae Afrodisiae annorum XXV mensis I\n
EDCS-14500536.translation: To Gaius Iunius Nicander, son, aged 38, to Mattia, daughter of Publius Verina, aged 24, and to Gaius Iunius Isaeus, veteran ex-decurion of the I Ala of Commageni, aged 60, and to Ulpia Aphrodisia, aged 25 years and 1 month.
EDCS-14500536.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Iunius Nicander, Mattia, Gaius Iunius Isaeus, and Ulpia Aphrodisia, with the ages of the deceased provided.

EDCS-14500537.text_edition: \n Caio Atilio Secundiano aedili Claudiae Celeiae annorum LVIIII et Veponiae Bellicinae eius Atilius Secundus parentibus et Calvisiae Tutorinae coniugi pudicissimae annorum XXXII et Atiliae Secundinae filiae stolatae annorum \n
EDCS-14500537.translation: To Gaius Atilius Secundianus, aedile, to Claudia Celeia, aged 59, and to Veponia Bellicina, his daughter, Atilius Secundus made this for his parents, and to Calvisia Tutorina, his wife, most virtuous, aged 32, and to Atilia Secundina, his daughter, clad in the stola, aged [---].
EDCS-14500537.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Atilius Secundus for his parents, his wife Claudia Celeia, his daughter Veponia Bellicina, and his daughter Atilia Secundina, noting their ages and his wife's virtue.

EDCS-14500538.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decimo Castricio Vero Antonio Avito decurioni Claudiae Celeiae annorum XXVI Iulia Quinti filia Vera mater viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500538.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Decimus Castricius Verus, also known as Antonius Avitus, decurion of Claudia Celeia, aged 26. Iulia Vera, daughter of Quintus, his living mother, made this, and for herself.
EDCS-14500538.summary: This funerary inscription honors Decimus Castricius Verus (Antonius Avitus), a decurion, and was erected by his mother, Iulia Vera, who also dedicated it for herself.

EDCS-14500539.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius municipii Celeiae libertus Favor vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Pusillae coniugi suae et suis\n
EDCS-14500539.translation: Tiberius Claudius Favor, freedman of the municipality of Celeia, made this while alive for himself, and for his wife Iulia Pusilla, and for his family.
EDCS-14500539.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by the freedman Tiberius Claudius Favor for himself, his wife, and his family.

EDCS-14500540.text_edition: \n Claudiae Secundae nnum XXXII contubernali hic sitae et Caio Cornelio Felici et vivis et sibi Cupitus Celeianorum f(aciendum) curavit\n
EDCS-14500540.translation: To Claudia Secunda, aged 32, his companion, buried here, and to Gaius Cornelius Felix, both living and for himself, Cupitus of the people of Celeia arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-14500540.summary: This funerary inscription was commissioned by Cupitus for his companion Claudia Secunda and for Gaius Cornelius Felix, indicating they were alive when it was made.

EDCS-14500541.text_edition: \n Enniae Marci filiae Verae decreto decurionum publice ob singularem eius pudicitiam Masculinius Surus \n
EDCS-14500541.translation: By decree of the town council, publicly, for Ennia Vera, daughter of Marcu, because of her singular modesty. Masculinius Surus [made this].
EDCS-14500541.summary: This inscription records that Masculinius Surus publicly honored Ennia Vera, daughter of Marcu, with a decree of the town council due to her exceptional modesty.

EDCS-14500542.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Valeria Aurelio Adiutori civi Afro negotianti obito annorum XXXV coniugi karissimo titulum posuit\n
EDCS-14500542.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Valeria placed this monument for Aurelius Adiutor, citizen of Africa, a merchant, who died at the age of 35, her dearest husband.
EDCS-14500542.summary: This funerary inscription was placed by Flavia Valeria for her husband, Aurelius Adiutor, an African merchant who died at 35.

EDCS-14500543.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Euhodiae Fructi uxori annorum XL domo Epheso Insequens servus fecit\n
EDCS-14500543.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Insequens, a slave, made this for Euhodia, wife of Fructus, aged 40, from Ephesus.
EDCS-14500543.summary: This funerary inscription was made by the slave Insequens for his wife Euhodia, aged 40, who was from Ephesus.

EDCS-14500544.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Vepo donatus civitate Romana viritim et inmunitate ab divo Augusto vivos sic fecit sibi et Boniatae Antoni filiae coniugi et suis\n
EDCS-14500544.translation: Gaius Iulius Vepo, granted Roman citizenship and immunity by the deified Augustus, made this while alive for himself, and for Boniata, daughter of Antonius, his wife, and for his family.
EDCS-14500544.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Gaius Iulius Vepo, who received Roman citizenship and immunity from Augustus, for himself, his wife, and his family.

EDCS-14500545.text_edition: \n Maximo IIviro iure dicundo Iulia Tiberi filia Maximina marito\n
EDCS-14500545.translation: To Maximus, holder of the II virate with judicial power. His wife, Iulia Maximina, daughter of Tiberius, [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-14500545.summary: This inscription records that Iulia Maximina, daughter of Tiberius, set up this monument for her husband, Maximus, who held the office of II vir iure dicundo.

EDCS-14500546.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mattio Adiecto interfectus a Mattzaris annorum XL Antonia Quincta coniugi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500546.translation: To the departed spirits. Mattius Adiectus, killed by the Mattzaris, aged 40. Antonia Quincta, his wife, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500546.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mattius Adiectus, noting his age at death and that he was killed by the Mattzaris, with his surviving wife erecting the monument.

EDCS-14500547.text_edition: \n Sextus Publicius Fronto nnrum LX Batro Celeianorum nnrum LXX Servatus Celeianorum nnrum XXXV Insequens Celeianorum nnrum LXX hic siti\n
EDCS-14500547.translation: Sextus Publicius Fronto, aged 60. Batro Celeianus, aged 70. Servatus Celeianus, aged 35. Insequens Celeianus, aged 70. Buried here.
EDCS-14500547.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing the names and ages of four individuals, presumably buried together in this location.

EDCS-14500548.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a decurio named Terentius and a place or person named Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14500549.text_edition: \n Terenti Proculi LILS optimi IIviri Claudiae Celeiae \n
EDCS-14500549.translation: To Terentius Proculus, son of L.ilius, a most excellent duovir of Claudia Celeia.
EDCS-14500549.summary: This is an honorary inscription honoring Terentius Proculus, a high-ranking official (duovir) in the community of Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14500550.text_edition: \n Marcus Vibius Dioscorus Iulia Concordia annorum LXXX et Tae\n
EDCS-14500550.translation: Marcus Vibius Dioscorus, and Iulia Concordia, aged 80, and Tae...
EDCS-14500550.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the names and ages of individuals, possibly commemorating a married couple or family members.

EDCS-14500551.summary: This is a short inscription, likely a dedication or funerary marker, to an individual named Antonius Torquatus Secundinus.

EDCS-14500552.text_edition: \n Antoniae Marullae annorum III Antonius Felix et Speratia Iuliana parentes filiae fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500552.translation: To Antonia Marulla, aged 3. Antonius Felix and Speratia Iuliana, parents, made this for their daughter.
EDCS-14500552.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, Antonia Marulla, erected by her grieving parents.

EDCS-14500553.text_edition: \n Caio Atilio Secundino vixit annos LXII obito annorum VII filio pientissimo libens merito vivus curavit\n
EDCS-14500553.translation: To Gaius Atilius Secundinus, who lived 62 years. To his most devoted son, who died at 7 years old. He provided this willingly and deservedly while alive.
EDCS-14500553.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring both a father (Gaius Atilius Secundinus) and his young son, erected by the father for himself and his son.

EDCS-14500554.text_edition: \n Aouius Ittonis libertus annorum C et Boniata coniux annorum LX Saturninus filius Ittonis libertus Genialis Ittonis libertus annorum L Finita Ittonis liberta annorum XL Saturnina Saturnini filia annorum \n
EDCS-14500554.translation: Aouius Ittonis, freedman, aged 100. And Boniata, wife, aged 60. Saturninus, freedman of Ittonis, Genialis, freedman of Ittonis, aged 50. Finita, freedwoman of Ittonis, aged 40. Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus, aged...
EDCS-14500554.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing several family members, including freedmen and freedwomen, and their ages, indicating a burial plot for a household.

EDCS-14500555.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Attio Ursulo annorum LXX et Ursino filio annorum XXIII Tertia coniux viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500555.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Attius Ursulus, aged 70, and to Ursino, his son, aged 23. Tertia, his wife, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500555.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Attius Ursulus and his son Ursino, erected by the surviving wife and mother.

EDCS-14500556.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Gaudentius filius Aeli Graeci matri vivae et Aurelio Sereniano fratri obito annorum XVI fecit\n
EDCS-14500556.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Gaudentius, son of Aelius Graecus, made this for his mother while she was alive and for his brother Aurelius Serenianus, who died at sixteen years of age.
EDCS-14500556.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Aurelius Gaudentius to his mother and deceased brother.

EDCS-14500557.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Iustinus obitus annorum LX Iustus filius et Respecta uxor vivi posuerunt\n
EDCS-14500557.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Iustinus, who died at sixty years of age. His son Iustus and his wife Respecta, while alive, set this up.
EDCS-14500557.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Iustus and his wife Respecta for Aurelius Iustinus, who died at sixty.

EDCS-14500558.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Secundinus et Aurelia Valentina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Aurelius Secundianus filius obitus annorum VII crudeles parentes facere curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500558.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Secundinus and his wife Aurelia Valentina, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their son Aurelius Secundianus, who died at seven years of age, their cruel parents arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-14500558.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Aurelius Secundinus and Aurelia Valentina for themselves and their son Aurelius Secundianus, who died at seven.

EDCS-14500559.text_edition: \n Campanus Atebodui filius vivus fecit sibi et Quartae coniugi et suis\n
EDCS-14500559.translation: Campanus, son of Atebodus, while alive, made this for himself, and for his wife Quarta, and for his family.
EDCS-14500559.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Campanus for himself, his wife Quarta, and their family.

EDCS-14500560.text_edition: \n Titi Carmaei Finiti annorum LXXX Sorniae Secundae annorum Tito Carmaeo Avito annorum XX Sornia Maxima parentibus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500560.translation: Sornia Maxima arranged for the making of this for her parents: Finitus, aged eighty, and Sornia Secunda; and for Titus Carmaeus Avitus, aged twenty.
EDCS-14500560.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Sornia Maxima for her deceased parents Finitus and Sornia Secunda, and for Titus Carmaeus Avitus.

EDCS-14500561.text_edition: \n Lucius Decius Luci filius Ianuarius nnorum L hic situs est Cassia Viatoris filia annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500561.translation: Lucius Decius Ianuarius, son of Lucius, aged fifty, lies here. Cassia, daughter of Viatoris, aged sixty.
EDCS-14500561.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Lucius Decius Ianuarius and Cassia.

EDCS-14500562.text_edition: \n annorum LV hic situs est vivus fecit O Diastulli N annorum LX nta filiae annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500562.translation: Aged fifty-five, he lies here. He made it while alive for O. Diastulli, aged sixty, and his daughter N...nta, aged twenty-five.
EDCS-14500562.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by an unnamed man, aged fifty-five, for O. Diastulli and his daughter N...nta.

EDCS-14500564.text_edition: \n Tiberius Exsoratuys sic et Ianuariae Ianuari vixit annos XXX et Atilio Victori annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500564.translation: Tiberius Exsoratuys lies here, and for Ianuaria, wife of Ianuarius, who lived thirty years, and for Attilius Victor, aged twenty-five.
EDCS-14500564.summary: This inscription records the burial of Tiberius Exsoratuys, Ianuaria, and Attilius Victor.

EDCS-14500565.text_edition: \n Finitus Maximi filius vivus fecit sibi et \n
EDCS-14500565.translation: Finitus, son of Maxumus, while alive, made this for himself and...
EDCS-14500565.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Finitus for himself, but it is incomplete.

EDCS-14500566.text_edition: \n Liberalis et Curena Quinti Marii Iuliani heredes vivi fecerunt sibi et Crescentino filio annorum XI et Iulianae nepoti annorum XV et Salutari filio annorum \n
EDCS-14500566.translation: Liberalis and Curena, heirs of Quintus Marius Iulianus, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their son Crescentinus, aged eleven, and for their granddaughter Iuliana, aged fifteen, and for their son Salutaris, aged...
EDCS-14500566.summary: This funerary epitaph was commissioned by Liberalis and Curena for themselves and their relatives, including children and grandchildren.

EDCS-14500567.text_edition: \n Magena Magemari viva fecit sibi et Primulae Primi filiae annorum V\n
EDCS-14500567.translation: Magena, daughter of Magemarus, made this for herself while alive and for Primula, daughter of Primus, aged 5.
EDCS-14500567.summary: A funerary monument erected by Magena for herself and her daughter Primula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500568.text_edition: \n Marcellinus Aviti vivus fecit sibi et suis Aureliae Bassinae coniugi karissimae! obitae annorum LXXX Aureliae Pothentinae filiae karissimae! obitae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500568.translation: Marcellinus, son of Avitus, made this for himself while alive and for his dearest wife Aurelia Bassina, who died at the age of 80, and for his dearest daughter Aurelia Pothentina, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500568.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Marcellinus for himself, his wife Aurelia Bassina, and his daughter Aurelia Pothentina, indicating a well-to-do family.

EDCS-14500569.text_edition: \n Mariccae Atemeri filiae nnorum LXXX Caius Cassius Civis heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500569.translation: Caius Cassius Civis, the heir, made this for Maricca, daughter of Atemirus, aged 80.
EDCS-14500569.summary: A funerary monument erected by the heir Caius Cassius Civis for Maricca, likely a freedwoman or client.

EDCS-14500570.text_edition: \n Publius Petronius Publi filius Velina Priscus fecit sibi et Atiliae Nigellae coniugi Publio Petronio Severo filio annorum X Petroniae Priscae filiae annorum \n
EDCS-14500570.translation: Publius Petronius Priscus, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, made this for himself and for his wife Atilia Nigella, his son Publius Petronius Severus, aged 10, and his daughter Petronia Prisca.
EDCS-14500570.summary: A family funerary monument erected by Publius Petronius Priscus for himself, his wife, and his children.

EDCS-14500571.text_edition: \n Petronio Publi filio Accepto etmae Almiae Publi filio Ved annorum LXVI sibi IB Marco Petronio II Censorino fratri rarissimo\n
EDCS-14500571.translation: For Petronius Acceptus, son of Publius, and her [wife] Almia, daughter of Publius, aged 66, and for himself, and for his very rare brother Marcus Petronius Censorinus, son of Publius.
EDCS-14500571.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Petronius Acceptus, his wife Almia, and his brother Marcus Petronius Censorinus, suggesting a distinguished family.

EDCS-14500572.text_edition: \n Petronia Publi filia Maximilla hic quiescit Lucius Petronius Luci libertus RSA Petronius Petronius Luci libertus annorum XXII \n
EDCS-14500572.translation: Petronia Maximilla, daughter of Publius, rests here. Lucius Petronius, freedman of Lucius, [erected this for] RSA Petronius, freedman of Lucius, aged 22.
EDCS-14500572.summary: A tombstone for Petronia Maximilla and Lucius Petronius, son of Lucius, with the latter likely erected by a patron or relative.

EDCS-14500573.text_edition: \n Pompeius Agilis vivus fecit sibi et Pompeiae Primigeniae coniugi nnorum XXXV et Pompeiae Spectatae filiae nnorum XVII et Couriae filiae nnorum XVI et Sextiae Suputae annorum XXX et Vitali fili-- annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500573.translation: Pompeius Agilis made this for himself while alive and for his wife Pompeia Primigenia, aged 35, and his daughter Pompeia Spectata, aged 17, and his daughter Couria, aged 16, and Sextia Suputa, aged 30, and Vitalis his son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500573.summary: A comprehensive funerary monument set up by Pompeius Agilis for himself and multiple family members, including his wife, daughters, and son.

EDCS-14500574.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pomponio Fortunato nnorum XXX EP CNA EN annorum XL Aelia Candida fecit sibi et coniugi pientissimo\n
EDCS-14500574.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelia Candida made this for herself and for her most pious husband, Pomponius Fortunatus, aged 30, and [for another person, names missing] aged 40.
EDCS-14500574.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Pomponius Fortunatus and his wife by Aelia Candida, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500575.text_edition: \n Quintus Publicius Lucifer testamento fieri iussit Cominiae Maximinae uxori optimae annorum XXX et sibi et suis Cominio Maximo filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500575.translation: Quintus Publicius Lucifer ordered this to be made by his will for his excellent wife Cominia Maxima, aged 30, and for himself, and for his son Cominius Maximus, aged 20.
EDCS-14500575.summary: A funerary monument established by testament by Quintus Publicius Lucifer for his wife, son, and himself.

EDCS-14500576.text_edition: \n Quartus Sirae vivus fecit sibi et Licoviae Ingenuae uxori annorum L et Leucimarae filiae annorum X Finito filio annorum VI Licovio Sexto annorum XX Ausco Muscionis filia annorum XXX Dubnae filiae annorum XXXX Marcus Licovius Barus annorum Marcus Licovius Boniatus nnorum L Vetulla Bucia Urbani filia annorum L Marcus Licovius Ursus annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500576.translation: Quartus Sirae made this for himself while alive and for his wife Licovia Ingenua, aged 50, and his daughter Leucimara, aged 10, his son Finitus, aged 6, Licovius Sextus, aged 20, Auscus Muscionis, daughter of [mother's name], aged 30, Dubna, daughter of [father's name], aged 40, Marcus Licovius Barus, aged [age missing], Marcus Licovius Boniatus, aged 50, Vetulla Bucia, daughter of Urbanus, aged 50, Marcus Licovius Ursus, aged 20.
EDCS-14500576.summary: An extensive funerary monument erected by Quartus Sirae for a large extended family, including his wife, children, and possibly other relatives or household members.

EDCS-14500577.text_edition: \n Respectus Titi Postumi Severi et Titus Postumius Titi libertus Provincialis vivi fecerunt sibi et Domestico et Ionicae parentibus\n
EDCS-14500577.translation: Respectus and Titus Postumius Provincialis, freedman of Titus Postumius Severus, made this while alive for themselves and for Domestica and Ionica, their parents.
EDCS-14500577.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by two men, Respectus and Titus Postumius Provincialis, for themselves and their parents.

EDCS-14500578.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Respectus Vari Luciferi vivus fecit sibi et Fortunatae uxori carissimae annorum XXX et Respectae filiae annorum VI et Ursinae filiae annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500578.translation: To the Divine Shades. Respectus, freedman of Lucifer, made this while alive for himself and for Fortunata, his dearest wife, aged 30, and for Respecta, his daughter, aged 6, and for Ursa, his daughter, aged 22.
EDCS-14500578.summary: This funerary epitaph records a man named Respectus who dedicated a monument for himself, his wife, and two daughters.

EDCS-14500579.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sabina Quarti viva fecit sibi et Sabino filio pientissimo annorum XXX et posuit Litugenae filiae annorum XL\n
EDCS-14500579.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sabina, wife of Quartus, made this while alive for herself and for Sabinus, her most dutiful son, aged 30, and placed this for Litugena, her daughter, aged 40.
EDCS-14500579.summary: This funerary epitaph records a woman named Sabina who dedicated a monument for herself, her son, and her daughter.

EDCS-14500580.text_edition: \n arsi Tiberi filio nnorum LII Secundus Marcelli filius vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500580.translation: To Tiberius, aged 52. Secundus, son of Marcellus, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500580.summary: This funerary epitaph records Secundus, son of Marcellus, who dedicated a monument for Tiberius.

EDCS-14500581.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundinus Secundi et Attia Ursula vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundo filio annorum VII\n
EDCS-14500581.translation: To the Divine Shades. Secundinus and Attia Ursula made this while alive for themselves and for Secundus, their son, aged 7.
EDCS-14500581.summary: This funerary epitaph records Secundinus and Attia Ursula who dedicated a monument for themselves and their young son.

EDCS-14500582.text_edition: \n Secundus Magimari filius vivus fecit sibi et suis et Magimaro Auctomari et Cupitae Atevorti filiae parentibus et Exorato nepoti annorum II\n
EDCS-14500582.translation: Secundus, son of Magimarus, made this while alive for himself and his family, and for his parents Magimarus and Cupita, and for Exoratus, his grandson, aged 2.
EDCS-14500582.summary: This funerary epitaph records Secundus, son of Magimarus, dedicating a monument for himself, his parents, and his grandson.

EDCS-14500584.text_edition: \n Belliciani vivus fecit sibi et Statiliae Cervae coniugi optimae obitae annorum XL Tertullinae filiae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500584.translation: Bellicianus made this while alive for himself and for Statilia Cerva, his excellent wife, deceased, aged 40, and for Tertullina, his daughter, aged 30.
EDCS-14500584.summary: This funerary epitaph records Bellicianus dedicating a monument for himself, his wife, and his daughter.

EDCS-14500585.text_edition: \n Lucius Trosius Propincus et Iantulla coniunx vivi fecerunt sibi et Iuveni filio annorum VIII\n
EDCS-14500585.translation: Lucius Trosius Propincus and his wife Iantulla made this while alive for themselves and for Iuvenis, their son, aged 8.
EDCS-14500585.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Trosius Propincus and his wife Iantulla dedicating a monument for themselves and their young son.

EDCS-14500586.text_edition: \n Titus Vedius Atressus vivus fecit sibi et Corneliae Publi libertae Valentinae coniugi annorum XXX et Secundinae filiae nnorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500586.translation: Titus Vedius Atressus made this while alive for himself and for Cornelia Valentina, freedwoman of Publius, his wife, aged 30, and for Secundina, his daughter, aged 30.
EDCS-14500586.summary: This funerary epitaph records Titus Vedius Atressus dedicating a monument for himself, his wife, and his daughter.

EDCS-14500587.translation: Gaius Vedius, son of Publius Valens...
EDCS-14500587.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph fragment that names Gaius Vedius, son of Publius Valens.

EDCS-14500592.text_edition: \n Caius Rufius Cai libertus Symphonus et Rufia Primula et \n
EDCS-14500592.translation: Gaius Rufius Symphonus, freedman of Rufus, and Rufia Primula.
EDCS-14500592.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription identifying two individuals, Gaius Rufius Symphonus and Rufia Primula, likely a couple or family members.

EDCS-14500593.text_edition: \n Celeianorum liberto annorum LX et llae uxori parentibus et lo filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500593.translation: To a freedman of the Celeianorum, aged 60, and his wife, and to their parents and son, aged 25.
EDCS-14500593.summary: This text appears to be part of a funerary monument dedicated to a freedman, his wife, and son, with a mention of parents.

EDCS-14500594.text_edition: \n Veriniae Aureliae coniugi carissimae stolatae feminae natione Dardana quae vixit annis XXXII Aurelius Valentinus vir egregius maritus vivus posuit\n
EDCS-14500594.translation: To Verinia Aurelia, dearest wife, a woman of rank, of the Dardani tribe, who lived 32 years; erected by her husband, Aurelius Valentinus, while he was still alive.
EDCS-14500594.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Verinia Aurelia, erected by her husband Aurelius Valentinus, providing her age, tribal origin, and her husband's status.

EDCS-14500596.text_edition: \n Severa Apronis filia viva fecit sibi et Boniatae filiae annorum XX Surio Genialis filius annorum L et Briga Aviti filia annorum XL et Ianuarius Genialis et Saturia coniux Severus Surio annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500596.translation: Severa, daughter of Apronis, erected this while alive for herself and for her daughter Boniata, aged 20, Surius Genialis, aged 50, and Briga, daughter of Avitus, aged 40, and Ianuarius Genialis and his wife Saturia, and Severus, son of Surius, aged 60.
EDCS-14500596.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Severa for herself and multiple family members, listing their names, ages, and relationships.

EDCS-14500597.text_edition: \n us Atto filio annorum XX et Nonnae Tatuci filiae uxori annorum C Successus filius annorum L\n
EDCS-14500597.translation: To Atto, son of us, aged 20, and Nonna, daughter of Tatucius, his wife, aged 100, and Successus, son, aged 50.
EDCS-14500597.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Atto, his wife Nonna, and their son Successus, providing their ages.

EDCS-14500598.text_edition: \n Trogimarus Maeni filius annorum LX Quadratus Trogimari filius annorum LXX et Secundinus annorum XX Sabianus Trogimara Cai Severa Quarti Firmus \n
EDCS-14500598.translation: Trogimarus, son of Maenius, aged 60; Quadratus, son of Trogimarus, aged 70; and Secundinus, aged 20; Sabianus; Trogimara; Gaius Severus; Firmus.
EDCS-14500598.summary: This text lists several individuals, likely members of a family, with some ages provided, possibly for a funerary monument.

EDCS-14500600.text_edition: \n Messor Caturei annorum L et Dumba Silvani filia annorum XXXV heres faciendum curavit Loucita Messori filia annorum XXX Restumarus filius annorum XIV Armianus\n
EDCS-14500600.translation: Messor, son of Catureius, aged 50, and Dumba, daughter of Silvanus, aged 35, had this built by their heir. Loucita, daughter of Messor, aged 30. Restumarus, son, aged 14. Armianus.
EDCS-14500600.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by an heir for Messor, his wife Dumba, and their children Loucita and Restumarus, noting their ages.

EDCS-14500601.text_edition: \n Silvanus Iantumari filius et Successa coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et suis Iunia filia annorum X\n
EDCS-14500601.translation: Silvanus, son of Iantumarus, and his wife Successa, erected this while alive for themselves and their relatives, and their daughter Iunia, aged 10.
EDCS-14500601.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Silvanus and his wife Successa for themselves, their relatives, and their daughter Iunia, mentioning her age.

EDCS-14500602.text_edition: \n Ingenuus Assedomari f(ilius) annorum LII Quinta Camuinui annorum \n
EDCS-14500602.translation: Ingenuus, son of Assedomarus, aged 52, and Quinta, [daughter] of Camuinus, aged [---].
EDCS-14500602.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of Ingenuus and Quinta.

EDCS-14500608.text_edition: \n Iustio Iusto Tutina anus obitus annorum LX IFR annorum LX Iustius Tutorinus annorum XL\n
EDCS-14500608.translation: To Iustius, son of Iustus, [a woman] named Tutina, deceased, aged 60; [---] FR, aged 60; Iustius Tutorinus, aged 40.
EDCS-14500608.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths and ages of at least three individuals: Tutina, an unnamed person, and Iustius Tutorinus.

EDCS-14500609.text_edition: \n AI Masculus AI Avita C PIA\n
EDCS-14500609.translation: Aulus Masculus and Aulus Avita for their daughter Pia.
EDCS-14500609.summary: This is a dedication made by Aulus Masculus and Aulus Avita for their daughter Pia, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-14500612.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto pro salute Quinti Mai Ce\n
EDCS-14500612.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the health of Quintus Maius.
EDCS-14500612.summary: This is a votive offering to Mars Augustus, dedicated for the health of Quintus Maius, possibly on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-14500613.text_edition: \n o Augusto Marcus Messius IIvir iure dicundo pro salute Spectatae eorum et Sequ\n
EDCS-14500613.translation: To Augustus, Marcus Messius, duovir with judicial authority, for the health of his wife Spectata and Sequ...
EDCS-14500613.summary: This is a dedication to Augustus by Marcus Messius, a local magistrate, for the health of his wife and possibly another person, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500614.text_edition: \n ia VercET Deusa liberta arcam I fecit patronae suae\n
EDCS-14500614.translation: Deusa, freedwoman of Vercetus, made this tomb for her patroness.
EDCS-14500614.summary: This inscription records a tomb built by a freedwoman named Deusa for her patroness, Vercetia.

EDCS-14500615.text_edition: \n Honoratiano XXVIII et Honoratae nepti II o Honoratiano \n
EDCS-14500615.translation: For Honoratianus, aged 28, and Honorata, his granddaughter.
EDCS-14500615.summary: This is likely an epitaph for Honoratianus, who died at 28, and his granddaughter Honorata, indicating a family tomb or memorial.

EDCS-14500616.text_edition: \n IL Honorati annorum coniux viv--- fecit sibi Ronus filius \n
EDCS-14500616.translation: The wife of Honoratus, lived... made [this] for herself, Ronus the son.
EDCS-14500616.summary: This inscription records that the wife of Honoratus commissioned a monument for herself, with her son Ronus mentioned as the builder or dedicator.

EDCS-14500618.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Augusto et ceteris dis deabusque omnibus inmortalibus Ulpius Martinus pro se suisque liberis ex voto posuit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500618.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mars Augustus, and all the other immortal gods and goddesses, Ulpius Martinus erected [this] for himself and his children, having vowed it and fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-14500618.summary: This is a religious dedication by Ulpius Martinus to Jupiter, Mars, and other deities, fulfilling a vow made for himself and his family.

EDCS-14500619.text_edition: \n Cai Iuli Attiani negotiatoris veteranorum R\n
EDCS-14500619.translation: Of Gaius Julius Attianus, a merchant of veterans...
EDCS-14500619.summary: This inscription likely refers to Gaius Julius Attianus, a merchant associated with veterans, possibly part of a funerary monument or an official record.

EDCS-14500620.text_edition: \n Caius Iustius Proculus aedilis Fl(aviae) Solvae Orionia Sexti filia Maxima \n
EDCS-14500620.translation: Gaius Iustius Proculus, aedile, [and] Flavia Solva, daughter of Orionia, Maxima...
EDCS-14500620.summary: This inscription lists civic officials and a woman, Flavia Solva, possibly related to a public building or a funerary monument.

EDCS-14500621.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Primigenius votum solvit libens merito Titi Atti libertus\n
EDCS-14500621.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Primigenius, freedman of Titus Attius, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-14500621.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury, dedicated by Primigenius, a freedman, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14500622.text_edition: \n Sennus Sacri filius hic situs est et Cona Modesti filia coniugi filii et filiae vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500622.translation: Sennus, son of Sacrus, is buried here, and Cona, daughter of Modestus. His living children made this for their parents and themselves.
EDCS-14500622.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sennus and Cona, dedicated by their children, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500623.text_edition: \n Eponae Augustae sacrum Volusius Equester votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500623.translation: Volusius Equester willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Epona Augusta.
EDCS-14500623.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Epona Augusta by Volusius Equester, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500624.text_edition: \n Firmus Sabini filius vivus fecit sibi et \n
EDCS-14500624.translation: Firmus, son of Sabinus, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14500624.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Firmus, son of Sabinus, who dedicated it for himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500625.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustae sacrum pro salute Valeriae Marcellae pater votum solvit\n
EDCS-14500625.translation: For the Augustan wet nurses. Valeriae Marcellae's father fulfilled his vow for her health.
EDCS-14500625.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nutrices Augustae (Augustan wet nurses) by a father for his daughter Valeria Marcella's health, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500626.text_edition: \n Secundinus qui Spittuli et Aurelia Gemella vivi fecerunt sibi et Respecto filio annorum XVI\n
EDCS-14500626.translation: Secundinus, who lived in Spittuli, and Aurelia Gemella made this for themselves and their son Respectus, aged sixteen.
EDCS-14500626.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundinus, Aurelia Gemella, and their son Respectus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500627.text_edition: \n Finitus Titionis filius vivus fecit sibi et \n
EDCS-14500627.translation: Finitus, son of Titius, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14500627.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Finitus, son of Titius, who dedicated it for himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500628.text_edition: \n Marcus Porcius Verus procurator Augusti me posuit\n
EDCS-14500628.translation: Marcus Porcius Verus, procurator of the Emperor, erected me.
EDCS-14500628.summary: This is an inscription by Marcus Porcius Verus, a Roman imperial official, likely erected during his term of office in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500629.text_edition: \n B Me vivus fecit sibi et filio annorum X Valeria Restituta \n
EDCS-14500629.translation: I, B[---] Me, made this for myself while alive and for my son, aged ten. Valeria Restituta.
EDCS-14500629.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Valeria Restituta for herself and her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500630.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Attius Vitalis et Senius Iuvenis curam agentes civitasque Iulius Cogitatus et Cunaito Titiani inius Ursinus et Polius Ianuarius estus et Surillio Appiani tinus et Priscianus Salvi Vibianus et Ursinus Vitalis Aquilinus et Caesernius Iulianus i Sammio et Hermadio ii Valentinus Inv et Iulius Primitivus Iulii Ursinus Secundi et Mogio Verani Uranius Domesticus et Aurelius Martius I Appuleius Ursus et Aurelius Secundinus II Malius Decianus et\n
EDCS-14500630.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Attius Vitalis and Senius Iuvenis, acting as curators, and the community, Julius Cogitatus and Cunaito, son of Titianus, Ursinus and Polius Ianuarius, Es[tus?] and Surillius, son of Appianus, Tinus and Priscianus, son of Salvius, Vibianus and Ursinus Vitalis Aquilinus, and Caesernius Iulianus, [for?] Sammio and Hermadio, Valentinus, son of Inv[ictus?], and Julius Primitivus, sons of Julius Ursinus Secundus, and Mogius Veranius Uranius Domesticus, and Aurelius Martius, son of Appuleius Ursus, and Aurelius Secundinus, sons of Malius Decianus, and...
EDCS-14500630.summary: This is a long, complex inscription recording contributions or responsibilities related to public works or a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, involving numerous individuals and possibly a civic community, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500631.text_edition: \n Marti Latobio Marmogio Toutati Sinati Mogetio Caius Valerius Valerinus ex voto\n
EDCS-14500631.translation: To Mars Latobius, Marmogius, Toutati, Sinatus, Mogetius. Caius Valerius Valerinus, in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-14500631.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to various deities, including Mars Latobius, by Caius Valerius Valerinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500632.text_edition: \n Marti Latobio Quintus Morsius Quinti filius Titianus votum solvit\n
EDCS-14500632.translation: Quintus Morsius, son of Quintus Titianus, fulfilled his vow to Mars Latobius.
EDCS-14500632.summary: This inscription records an individual's fulfillment of a vow to the god Mars Latobius.

EDCS-14500633.translation: Aurelius Diso set up this vow.
EDCS-14500633.summary: This inscription records an individual, Aurelius Diso, setting up a votive offering.

EDCS-14500634.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo proconsuli patri patriae tribunicia potestate Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio Marcus Tullius Maximus ob honorem decurionatus\n
EDCS-14500634.translation: Marcus Tullius Maximus, in honor of his decurionate, [dedicated this] to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland, holder of tribunician power, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-14500634.summary: This inscription records an individual's dedication made in honor of his decurionate to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500635.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo proconsuli patri patriae tribunicia potestate Parthico Britannico maximo Gaius Castius Avitus decurio et IIvir Solvae ob honorem decurionatus ita ut promisit plene obtulit\n
EDCS-14500635.translation: Gaius Castius Avitus, decurio and IIvir of Solva, fully offered what he promised in honor of his decurionate to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland, holder of tribunician power, Parthicus, Britannicus Maximus.
EDCS-14500635.summary: This inscription records a local official, Gaius Castius Avitus, fulfilling a promise made in honor of his decurionate to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500636.translation: The council of Solva [dedicated this] to the deified Iovius Maximianus.
EDCS-14500636.summary: This inscription records the council of Solva dedicating a monument to the deified Emperor Maximian.

EDCS-14500637.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino maximo beatissimo ac supra omnes retro principes piissimo et victoriosissimo semper Augusto bono rei publicae nato Fabius Claudius vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Norici mediterranei devotus numini maiestatique eius semper TOIS \n
EDCS-14500637.translation: Fabius Claudius, most perfect official and governor of the province of Noricum Mediterraneum, devoted to the divine majesty and power of our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most great, most blessed, most pious, and most victorious Augustus above all past princes, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-14500637.summary: This inscription records a provincial governor, Fabius Claudius, expressing devotion to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14500638.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari [Marco Aur][elio Antonino] Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli ordo Solvensium\n
EDCS-14500638.translation: The council of Solva [dedicated this] to the Emperor Caesar [Marcus Aurelius Antoninus] Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Fatherland, holder of tribunician power, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-14500638.summary: This inscription records the council of Solva dedicating a monument to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500639.text_edition: \n Marco Gavio Maximo praefecto praetorio Lucius Cammius Secundinus primus pilus praefectus legionis X geminae procurator Augusti amico\n
EDCS-14500639.translation: Lucius Cammius Secundinus, primus pilus, commander of the Legio X Gemina, procurator of Augustus, to his friend Marcus Gavius Maximus, prefect of the praetorium.
EDCS-14500639.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Cammius Secundinus to his friend Marcus Gavius Maximus, a high-ranking official, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500640.text_edition: \n Aelio Leucamulo veterano annorum LXX et Iuliae Iustinae filiae annorum XXVII Caius Kanulanius Eumitus vivus fecit sibi et Nansiniae Verecundae coniugi et Caio Kanulanio Nepoti filio militi alae III Thracum annorum XXV stipendiorum VI loco et impensa Anni Festi\n
EDCS-14500640.translation: Caius Kanulanius Eumitus made this tomb while alive for himself and for Nansinia Verecunda, his wife, and for his son Caius Kanulanius Nepos, a soldier of the Ala III Thracum, aged 25 with 6 years of service, and for Aelius Leucamulus, a veteran aged 70, and for his daughter Iulia Iustina, aged 27, at the place and expense of Annius Festus.
EDCS-14500640.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a man, Caius Kanulanius Eumitus, dedicating a tomb for his family and including a veteran and a soldier, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500641.text_edition: \n Marco Annio annorum LX Annia Mar coniugi et Marco Annio Marcello filio annorum XXV et Marco Annio Marcelliano |(centurioni) cohortis I Asturum annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500641.translation: For Marcus Annius, aged 60, Annia Mar for her husband, and for their son Marcus Annius Marcellus, aged 25, and for Marcus Annius Marcellianus, centurion of the Cohors I Asturum, aged 30.
EDCS-14500641.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a family's burial arrangements for Marcus Annius, his son, and grandson who was a centurion, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-14500642.text_edition: \n annorum XXV et Marcellino filio annorum XVI\n
EDCS-14500642.translation: aged 25 years and to Marcellinus, my son, aged 16 years.
EDCS-14500642.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, likely for a funerary monument, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500643.text_edition: \n Tito Attio Cai filio Tutori praefecto alae I Batavorum miliariae praefecto alae I Tungrorum Frontonianae tribuno militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis praefecto cohortis I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum decurioni Flavia Solva Sedatius Quietus\n
EDCS-14500643.translation: to Titus Attius Tutor, son of Caius, Prefect of the I Batavorum milliaria cavalry wing, Prefect of the I Frontoniana Tungrorum cavalry wing, Tribune of soldiers of the II Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, Prefect of the I Baetasiorum cohort of Roman citizens, Decurion of Flavia Solva, Sedatius Quietus.
EDCS-14500643.summary: This inscription lists the extensive military and civic career of Sedatius Quietus, a Decurion from Flavia Solva, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500645.text_edition: \n Quinto Carminio Latino et Carminio Cupito optioni legionis I Minerviae Catulla avunculo et fratri faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500645.translation: Catulla had this made for Quintus Carminius Latinus and Carminius Cupitus, optiones in the I Minervia legion, as uncle and brother.
EDCS-14500645.summary: This inscription records a woman, Catulla, commissioning a monument for two men, Quintus Carminius Latinus and Carminius Cupitus, who served as optiones, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500646.text_edition: \n Titus Cassius Secundus |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris donis donatus corona murali phaleris torquibus armillis testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Marci Saxi Primi heres faciendum curavit locus sepulturae datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-14500646.translation: Titus Cassius Secundus, a centurion of the XV Apollinaris legion, honored with a mural crown, phalerae, torques, and armillae, ordered this to be made in his will; his heir had it made according to the arbitration of Marcus Saxus Primus; a burial place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-14500646.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Titus Cassius Secundus, a decorated centurion, detailing his honors and the arrangements for his burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500647.text_edition: \n Caio Ciamillio Prisco veterano annorum LXXX et Vindillae Crielonis filiae coniugi annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500647.translation: to Gaius Ciamillius Priscus, a veteran, aged 80 years, and to Vindilla, daughter of Crielon, his wife, aged 60 years.
EDCS-14500647.summary: This inscription records the ages of a veteran and his wife, likely for a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500648.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Secundino IIviro iure dicundo Artoriae Cai filiae Florae Marco Claudio Tiberi filio Lucullo |(centurioni) legionis XIIII |(centurioni) legionis XIII Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Secundo Valeria Crispa fecit\n
EDCS-14500648.translation: Valeria Crispa made this for Tiberius Claudius Secundinus, son of Tiberius, IIvir iure dicundo of Artoria, and for Flora, daughter of Caius Artorius, and for Marcus Claudius Lucullus, son of Tiberius, centurion of the XIIII Gemina legion, centurion of the XIII Gemina legion, and for Tiberius Claudius Secundus, son of Tiberius.
EDCS-14500648.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Valeria Crispa to her husband Tiberius Claudius Secundinus, their daughter Flora, and two sons who served as centurions, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500649.text_edition: \n Caio Donnio Attico veterano cohortis V praetoriae @(obito) annorum LXI Cerviae Ingenuae coniugi vivae fili fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500649.translation: Cervia Ingenua made this for her husband Gaius Donnius Atticus, a veteran of the V Praetoria cohort, who died aged 61 years, and for herself, while alive.
EDCS-14500649.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a funerary monument by Cervia Ingenua for her deceased veteran husband and for herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500650.text_edition: \n Caius Iunius Provincialis vivus fecit sibi et Crispinae Finiti filiae uxori optimae et Quintinio Catullo filio optimo militi cohortis X urbanae p r annorum et Quintiniae Catullae annorum? VEN\n
EDCS-14500650.translation: Gaius Iunius Provincialis made this for himself, while alive, and for his excellent wife Crispina, daughter of Finitus, and for his excellent son Quintinius Catullus, a soldier of the X Urbana cohort, aged [complete age] years, and for Quintinia Catulla, aged?
EDCS-14500650.summary: This inscription is a self-dedicatory funerary monument for Gaius Iunius Provincialis, his wife, and son, and possibly a daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500651.text_edition: \n Macerio evocato legionis et Maceriae et Macerio O\n
EDCS-14500651.translation: Macerio, an evocatus of the legion, and Maceria, and Macerio.
EDCS-14500651.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or funerary notice for Macerio, an evocatus, and two other individuals named Maceria and Macerio, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500652.text_edition: \n e coniugi Memmio o filio alae I Augustae Thracum annorum XXVI\n
EDCS-14500652.translation: to [his] wife Memmia, and to [his] son, of the I Augusta Thracum cavalry wing, aged 26 years.
EDCS-14500652.summary: This inscription records the ages of a son and possibly his mother, who were associated with the I Augusta Thracum cavalry wing, likely for a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500655.text_edition: \n Claudius Crispinus et Censorina Cetti filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Acceptiano annorum XVIII et Crispino Crispini filio aedili Flavia Solva annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500655.translation: Claudius Crispinus and Censorina, daughter of Cettus, made this (monument) while alive for themselves and for Acceptianus, aged 18, and for Crispinius, son of Crispinus, an aedile, and for Flavia Solva, aged 25.
EDCS-14500655.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Claudius Crispinus and Censorina for themselves and other family members, including aediles, likely in the Roman colony of Flavia Solva.

EDCS-14500656.text_edition: \n Lucius Gautius Finitus aedilis Solvae vivus fecit sibi et Firmidiae Mussae uxori optimae annorum L et Finitae filiae annorum V\n
EDCS-14500656.translation: Lucius Gautius Finitus, an aedile of Solva, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Firmidia Musa, an excellent woman aged 50, and for his daughter Finita, aged 5.
EDCS-14500656.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Gautius Finitus, an aedile of Solva, for himself and his wife and daughter.

EDCS-14500657.text_edition: \n Iulius Sabinus aedilis Solvae Iuliae Secundinae uxori optimae et sibi et Iulio Sabiniano filio ---? an(norum) XXX\n
EDCS-14500657.translation: Iulius Sabinus, an aedile of Solva, (made this) for his wife Iulia Secundina, an excellent woman, and for himself, and for his son Iulius Sabinianus, aged 30.
EDCS-14500657.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulius Sabinus, an aedile of Solva, for his wife, himself, and his son.

EDCS-14500659.text_edition: \n Pollybio sic Solvensium et Velleciae matri\n
EDCS-14500659.translation: For Pollybius, a citizen of Solva, and for Vellecia, his mother.
EDCS-14500659.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Pollybius, identified as a citizen of Solva, and his mother Vellecia.

EDCS-14500662.text_edition: \n Adiatullus Vepotali filius Tatucae Vervici filiae uxori et Rumae filiae annorum XXV et filiis sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-14500662.translation: Adiatullus, son of Vepotalus, made this for his wife Tatuca, daughter of Vervicus, and for Roma, his daughter, aged 25, and for his sons, for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500662.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Adiatullus for his wife, daughter, sons, and himself.

EDCS-14500663.text_edition: \n Marco Annio Marci liberto Decumo annorum LXX Sacra Sauronis filia coniugi et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500663.translation: To Decumus, freedman of Marcus, son of Marcus, aged 70. Sacra, daughter of Sauron, made this for her husband and herself while alive.
EDCS-14500663.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Sacra for her husband Decumus and herself.

EDCS-14500665.text_edition: \n Sextus Baebius Pudens vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Festae coniugi nnorum XXV et Iuliae Verecundae matri\n
EDCS-14500665.translation: Sextus Baebius Pudens made this while alive for himself and for his wife Iulia Festa, aged 25, and for his mother Iulia Verecunda.
EDCS-14500665.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Sextus Baebius Pudens for himself, his wife, and his mother.

EDCS-14500666.text_edition: \n Bonioni Atti filio annorum LXXV et Baebiae Suadullae \n
EDCS-14500666.translation: For Bonionius, son of Attius, aged 75, and for Baebia Suadulla.
EDCS-14500666.summary: This inscription records the names of Bonionius and Baebia Suadulla, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-14500667.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Togio vivus fecit sibi et Bisugnatae defunctae nnorum LXV\n
EDCS-14500667.translation: Lucius Cassius Togius made this while alive for himself and for Bisugnata, deceased, aged 65.
EDCS-14500667.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Cassius Togius for himself and his deceased wife Bisugnata.

EDCS-14500668.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cassius Profuturus vivus fecit sibi et Iucundae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XV\n
EDCS-14500668.translation: To the departed spirits. Cassius Profuturus made this while alive for himself and for his wife Iucunda, who died at the age of 15.
EDCS-14500668.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated to the spirits of the dead, was erected by Cassius Profuturus for himself and his wife Iucunda.

EDCS-14500669.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Restutus et Cassia Quarta coniux vivi fecerunt sibi <e>t Cassius! Ingenuus sic et Cassius! Restutianus sic filiis\n
EDCS-14500669.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Restutus and his wife Cassia Quarta, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their sons Cassius Ingenuus and Cassius Restutianus.
EDCS-14500669.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Claudius Restutus and his wife for themselves and their sons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500670.text_edition: \n Claudiae Tiberi filiae Iucundae annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-14500670.translation: To Claudia Iucunda, daughter of Tiberius, aged 23.
EDCS-14500670.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Iucunda, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500672.text_edition: \n EN CN II F Dubnae coniugi optimae annorum XL Candius frater fecit \n
EDCS-14500672.translation: To Dubna, his excellent wife, aged 40. Her brother Candius made this.
EDCS-14500672.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Candius to his wife Dubna, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500673.text_edition: \n Finito Lappi annorum LX et Salviae Iantumari filiae coniugi optimae filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500673.translation: To Finitus Lappi, aged 60, and to Salvia, daughter of Iantumarus, his excellent wife. Their sons made this.
EDCS-14500673.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by sons for their father Finitus Lappi and their mother Salvia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500674.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus securitati perpetuae Iulius Secundinus vivus fecit sibi et Prisciae Albinae coniugi et Romaniae Catullinae coniugi annorum XXV et Iuliae Secundinae sorori et Auraelio Secundino filio\n
EDCS-14500674.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For everlasting peace. Julius Secundinus, while alive, made this for himself, and for his wife Priscia Albina, and for his wife Romania Catullina, aged 25, and for his sister Iulia Secundina, and for his son Aurelius Secundinus.
EDCS-14500674.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Julius Secundinus for himself and multiple family members, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500675.text_edition: \n Marcus Iunius Marci libertus Cerialis et Iunia Aphrodisia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Iuniae Ingenuae filiae annorum III et M(arco) Iunio S\n
EDCS-14500675.translation: Marcus Iunius Cerialis, freedman of Marcus, and his wife Iunia Aphrodisia, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their daughter Iunia Ingenua, aged 3, and for Marcus Iunius S...
EDCS-14500675.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Marcus Iunius Cerialis and his wife for themselves and their daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500677.text_edition: \n Publius Laelius Publi libertus Heracla vivus fecit sibi et Samudae Musonis filiae coniugi pientissimae annorum LV et Laeliae Publi filiae Secundinae annorum XXX et Laeliae Bononiae nepti annorum\n
EDCS-14500677.translation: Publius Laelius Heracla, freedman of Publius, while alive, made this for himself, and for Samuda, daughter of Muson, his most devoted wife, aged 55, and for Laelia Secundina, daughter of Publius, aged 30, and for Laelia Bononia, his granddaughter, aged...
EDCS-14500677.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Publius Laelius Heracla for himself and several female family members, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500679.text_edition: \n Marcus Laetilius Laetiliae libertus Paccius vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Successae coniugi optimae et Scipioni filio annorum VI et Iustae filiae annorum VI et Iustinae filiae annorum VI et Laetilio Iuveni filio annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500679.translation: Marcus Laetilius Paccius, freedman of Laetilia, while alive, made this for himself, and for his excellent wife Iulia Successa, and for his son Scipio, aged 6, and for his daughter Iusta, aged 6, and for his daughter Iustina, aged 6, and for his son Laetilius Iuvenis, aged 30.
EDCS-14500679.summary: This is a funerary epitaph made by Marcus Laetilius Paccius for himself and his wife and five children, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500680.text_edition: \n Lucconi Suri filio annorum LXX et Dubnae Lucconis filio annorum XL etani filiae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500680.translation: To Lucconus, son of Surus, aged 70, and to Dubna, son of Lucconus, aged 40, and to his wife...
EDCS-14500680.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph possibly recording multiple individuals and their ages, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500681.text_edition: \n filio annorum XX et Mascillioni filio annorum XVAN et Coviomaro \n
EDCS-14500681.translation: ...son, aged 20, and Mascillion, son, aged 15, and to Coviomarus...
EDCS-14500681.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly funerary, listing individuals and their ages, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500682.text_edition: \n Masculus Deusi filius vivus fecit sibi et Pettuni Secundi filiae coniugi annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500682.translation: Masculus, son of Deus, made this while alive for himself and for his wife, Pettunia Secunda, aged 60.
EDCS-14500682.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pettunia Secunda, made by her husband Masculus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500684.text_edition: \n Lucio Memmio Luci liberto Liberali et Sammae coniugi patronus fecit\n
EDCS-14500684.translation: The patron made this for his freedman Lucius Memmius Liberalis and his wife Samma.
EDCS-14500684.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Memmius Liberalis and his wife Samma, dedicated by their patron, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500685.text_edition: \n Caio Mimisio Cai liberto Ianuario Caio Mimisio Draconiae annorum X Mosicu Adsedili fecit\n
EDCS-14500685.translation: Caius Mimisius Ianuarius, freedman of Caius Mimisius, aged 10, and Mosicu Adsedili made this for Caius Mimisius Draconia.
EDCS-14500685.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Mimisius Draconia, a child, made by his father Caius Mimisius Ianuarius and possibly his mother Mosicu Adsedili, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500686.text_edition: \n Marci filius Fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Turboniae Nigrinae uxori et Finitae filiae defunctae annis III o Rufi Valenti ann- mensibus II diebus IIio Rufi filio annis VII mensibus III diebus XVI\n
EDCS-14500686.translation: Fidelis, son of Marcus, made this while alive for himself, for his wife Turbonia Nigrina, for his deceased daughter Finita, aged 3 years, 2 months, and 2 days, and for Rufi Valenti, his deceased son, aged 7 years, 3 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-14500686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of a daughter and a son, made by their father Fidelis for his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500688.text_edition: \n Quintus Pompeius Eugamus et Pompeia Venusta vivi fecerunt sibi et Pompeiae Ingenuae filiae defunctae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500688.translation: Quintus Pompeius Eugamus and Pompeia Venusta made this while alive for themselves and for their deceased daughter Pompeia Ingenua, aged 20.
EDCS-14500688.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Ingenua, made by her parents Quintus Pompeius Eugamus and Pompeia Venusta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500689.text_edition: \n Quinto Pompeio Quinti liberto Eutycho annorum LX et filiis Annia Quinta uxsor sic viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500689.translation: Annia Quinta, his wife, made this while alive for Quintus Pompeius Eutyches, freedman of Quintus Pompeius, aged 60, and for his sons and for herself.
EDCS-14500689.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Pompeius Eutyches and his sons, made by his wife Annia Quinta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500690.text_edition: \n Quinto Pompeio Quinti filio Iuliano annorum LV et \n
EDCS-14500690.translation: For Quintus Pompeius Iulianus, son of Quintus Pompeius, aged 55, and...
EDCS-14500690.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph recording the death of Quintus Pompeius Iulianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500692.text_edition: \n Tito Raecio Narcisso annorum L Claudia Cara viva fecit sibi et coniugi optimo\n
EDCS-14500692.translation: Claudia Cara made this while alive for Titus Raecius Narcissus, aged 50, and for her excellent husband.
EDCS-14500692.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Raecius Narcissus and his wife, made by Claudia Cara, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500693.text_edition: \n ae Rufi filiae Albae Caius Memmius Cavarinus matri\n
EDCS-14500693.translation: Caius Memmius Cavarinus to his mother Alba, daughter of Rufi.
EDCS-14500693.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph from Caius Memmius Cavarinus to his mother Alba, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500694.text_edition: \n Lucio Secundinio Suriano annorum LI Socer annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500694.translation: For Lucius Secundinius Surianus, aged 51, and his father-in-law, aged 30.
EDCS-14500694.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Lucius Secundinius Surianus and his father-in-law, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500695.text_edition: \n Secundino Suri filio annorum LX et Tottiae filiae coniugi annorum XXX f e s\n
EDCS-14500695.translation: To Secundinus, son of Surus, aged 60, and to Tottia, daughter of [---], aged 30, wife.
EDCS-14500695.summary: This epitaph records the ages of Secundinus and his wife Tottia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500697.summary: This epitaph records the age of Spectatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500698.text_edition: \n Tertius vivus fecit sibi et Ateboduae Quarti filiae coniugi nnorum XXXV et Secundo filio annorum V\n
EDCS-14500698.translation: Tertius made this, alive, for himself and for Atebodua, daughter of Quartus, wife, aged 35, and for Secundus, son, aged 5.
EDCS-14500698.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tertius for himself, his wife Atebodua, and their son Secundus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500699.text_edition: \n Tertio Quarti filio annorum LV et Avitae Secundini filiae coniugi Tertinus filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500699.translation: Tertinus, son, had this made for Tertius, son of Quartus, aged 55, and for Avita, daughter of Secundinus, wife.
EDCS-14500699.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Tertinus for his father Tertius and his mother Avita, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500701.text_edition: \n Titiae Tiberi Cotuconi annorum CXX et Iuliae Luci filiae Myrinae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500701.translation: To Titia, daughter of Tiberius Cotuconus, aged 120, and to Julia Myrina, daughter of Lucius, aged 30.
EDCS-14500701.summary: This epitaph records the ages of Titia and Julia Myrina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period, notable for the exceptionally high age recorded for Titia.

EDCS-14500702.text_edition: \n Caius Trebonius M vivus fecit Quartae Uperaci filiae et Treboniae Vfiliae annorum XXXX et Treboni\n
EDCS-14500702.translation: Gaius Trebonius M, alive, made this for Quarta Uperaci, daughter, and for Trebonia, daughter, aged 40, and for Trebo[nius?].
EDCS-14500702.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Trebonius M for his daughter Quarta Uperaci, another daughter Trebonia, and possibly a son Trebonius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500703.text_edition: \n Marcus Turbonius et Turbonia Suadra vivi fecerunt sibi et Turbonio Successo filio annorum V et Sexto genero annorum XXX et Secundinae filiae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500703.translation: Marcus Turbonius and Turbonia Suadra, alive, made this for themselves and for Turbonius Successus, son, aged 5, and for Sextus, son-in-law, aged 30, and for Secundina, daughter, aged 20.
EDCS-14500703.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcus Turbonius and Turbonia Suadra for themselves, their son Turbonius Successus, their daughter Secundina, and their son-in-law Sextus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500704.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibius Catussa vivus fecit sibi et Marcellinae uxori annorum XXXII et Vibiae Finitae matri carissimae\n
EDCS-14500704.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vibius Catussa, alive, made this for himself and for Marcellina, wife, aged 32, and for Vibia Finita, dearest mother.
EDCS-14500704.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Vibius Catussa for himself, his wife Marcellina, and his mother Vibia Finita, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500705.summary: This incomplete inscription likely records the names of Gaius Volusius Vibius and Quartina, possibly a married couple, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500707.summary: This fragment records a freedman named -ratus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500708.translation: Publius? son of Quintus? son of Quintus? R. (initials)
EDCS-14500708.summary: This inscription consists of only three Roman initials, possibly for a name, with no further context provided.

EDCS-14500709.text_edition: \n Occia Iuli filius Saitae libertus et Ingenuae Malei filiae coniugi annorum XXXX Occianus parentibus et Emeritae sorori pientissimae et Ingenuinae sorori \n
EDCS-14500709.translation: Occianus, freedman of Occia, daughter of Iulus, and of Saitas, and husband of Ingenua, daughter of Maleus, aged 40, made (this) for his parents and for Emerita, most dutiful sister, and for Ingenuina, sister.
EDCS-14500709.summary: This funerary inscription records Occianus dedicating a monument to his parents, sisters, and wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500710.summary: This brief inscription records Divico dedicating a monument to Marcus Claudius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500711.summary: This fragmented inscription records Verina, a freedwoman of Julius, likely a tombstone or dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500715.text_edition: \n Publius Albius Calandinus vivus fecit sibi et Silviae Ursulae coniugi annorum XL\n
EDCS-14500715.translation: Publius Albius Calandinus, while alive, made (this) for himself and for his wife Silvia Ursula, aged 40.
EDCS-14500715.summary: This is a self-dedicated funerary monument made by Publius Albius Calandinus for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500716.text_edition: \n Atilius Autoscutti filius Brigia Brigi filia Latinae filiae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500716.translation: Atilius, son of Autoscutti, and Brigia, daughter of Briga, and Latina, daughter (of?), aged 20.
EDCS-14500716.summary: This inscription appears to record family members, possibly deceased, including Atilius, Brigia, and Latina, with Latina's age given, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500717.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Secundinus veteranus legionis X geminae piae fidelis Antoninianae vivus fecit sibi et Aureliae Sabinae coniugi annorum et Aurelio filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500717.translation: Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis Antoniniana, while alive, made (this) for himself and for Aurelia Sabina, his wife, aged [..] and for his son Aurelius, aged 20.
EDCS-14500717.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion, dedicating a monument for himself, his wife, and his son, likely dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-14500718.text_edition: \n Saturninius Verinus et Aurelia Secundina vivi fecerunt sibi et Aurelio filio \n
EDCS-14500718.translation: Saturninius Verinus and Aurelia Secundina, while alive, made (this) for themselves and for their son Aurelius.
EDCS-14500718.summary: This funerary inscription records Saturninius Verinus and Aurelia Secundina dedicating a monument for themselves and their son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500719.text_edition: \n Senaca sic Palumbi et Materna coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Palumbo patri annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500719.translation: Senaca and Materna, wife of Palumbus, while alive, made (this) for themselves and for Palumbus, their father, aged 60.
EDCS-14500719.summary: This funerary inscription records Senaca and Materna dedicating a monument for themselves and Materna's father, Palumbus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500720.text_edition: \n Caio Atucio Albano veterano ex praetorio et Aeliae Sabinae uxori optimae et Atuciae Aventinae filiae annorum XXXIII Exupereus milis sic legionis secundae Italicae obitus XXVIII\n
EDCS-14500720.translation: For Gaius Atucius Albanus, veteran from the Praetorian Guard, and for Aelia Sabina, his excellent wife, and for Atucia Aventina, his daughter, aged 33. Exupereus, soldier of the Second Italian Legion, died at 28.
EDCS-14500720.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument dedicated to Gaius Atucius Albanus, his wife Aelia Sabina, and daughter Atucia Aventina, with a separate notice for the death of Exupereus, a soldier, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500721.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dating to the Imperial period, dedicating the monument to Atilia.

EDCS-14500722.text_edition: \n Vibius Vindi filius vivus fecit sibi et Dubitatae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500722.translation: Vibius, son of Vindi, made this while alive for himself and his wife Dubitata.
EDCS-14500722.summary: This funerary inscription records Vibius, son of Vindi, making a monument for himself and his wife Dubitata, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500725.text_edition: \n Boius Boniati et Maximae Dievionis filiae coniugi optimae annorum LX et Comato Boi filio annorum XL et Rustiae Tertullae coniugi et Kalendinus Celati filius miles legionis II adiutricis annorum XVI\n
EDCS-14500725.translation: Boius for his most excellent wife Maxima, daughter of Dievionis, aged 60, and for his son Comatus, aged 40, and for Rustia Tertulla his wife, and for Kalendinus, son of Celatus, a soldier of the Legio II Adjutrix, aged 16.
EDCS-14500725.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial arrangements made by Boius for his family members, including his wife Maxima, son Comatus, another wife Rustia Tertulla, and a soldier named Kalendinus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500726.text_edition: \n Burrus Suri filius et Suaducia Boniati filia coniux\n
EDCS-14500726.translation: Burrus, son of Surus, and Suaducia, daughter of Boniatus, his wife.
EDCS-14500726.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Burrus, son of Surus, and his wife Suaducia, daughter of Boniatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500727.text_edition: \n Lucius Domitius Secundinus et Derva Malai filia coniux sibi et Iuniano filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-14500727.translation: Lucius Domitius Secundinus and his wife Derva, daughter of Malai, for themselves and for their son Iunianus, aged 25.
EDCS-14500727.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Domitius Secundinus and his wife Derva making arrangements for themselves and their son Iunianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500728.text_edition: \n Gemellus Marconis filius et Adnama coniux et Marcellinus filius et Vitellia coniux optima vivi fecerunt sibi Ursacinae\n
EDCS-14500728.translation: Gemellus, son of Marconis, and his wife Adnama, and Marcellinus, and his wife Vitellia, most excellent, made this while alive for themselves and for Ursacina.
EDCS-14500728.summary: This funerary inscription records Gemellus, son of Marconis, his wife Adnama, Marcellinus, and his wife Vitellia making provisions for themselves and Ursacina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500729.text_edition: \n Saturninus Aubnissi filius vivus fecit sibi et Suaduciae Vanni filiae coniugi et fili- annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500729.translation: Saturninus, son of Aubnissis, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Suaducia, daughter of Vanni, and for his son aged 22.
EDCS-14500729.summary: This funerary inscription records Saturninus, son of Aubnissis, making provisions for himself, his wife Suaducia, and his son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500730.text_edition: \n Vercaius Saitulli filius vivus fecit sibi et Caixuni Quarti filiae annorum\n
EDCS-14500730.translation: Vercaius, son of Saitullus, made this for himself and for his wife Caixun, daughter of Quarti, aged...
EDCS-14500730.summary: This funerary inscription records Vercaius, son of Saitullus, making provisions for himself and his wife Caixun, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500731.text_edition: \n Avitus Optati filius defunctus annorum LV et Covrunae Ianuari filiae coniugi optimae et Marco Ulpio IAII et DiCmaEEsae annorum XI et Hostiliia VENEINA coniux II et Marco Ulpio Acilio filio annorum XXII\n
EDCS-14500731.translation: Avitus, son of Optatus, deceased, aged 55, and for his most excellent wife Covruna, daughter of Ianuarius, and for Marcus Ulpius IAII and DiCmaEEsae, aged 11, and for Hostilia VENEINA, his second wife, and for Marcus Ulpius Acilius, son, aged 22.
EDCS-14500731.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial arrangements for Avitus, his wives Covruna and Hostilia, and his children, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500732.text_edition: \n Comatus Atianti filius fecit sibi et Suntrae Darseti filiae coniugi et Adnamato filio annorum LX et Suadullae Sammi filiae Cenicelloni filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500732.translation: Comatus, son of Atiantus, made this for himself and for his wife Suntra, daughter of Darsetus, and for his son Adnamatus, aged 60, and for Suadulla, daughter of Sammi, and for Cenicellus, son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500732.summary: This funerary inscription records Comatus, son of Atiantus, making provisions for his family, including his wife Suntra, son Adnamatus, and others, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500733.text_edition: \n Caius Sempronius Summinus vivus fecit sibi et Musae uxori et Primo filio annorum VIIII\n
EDCS-14500733.translation: Gaius Sempronius Summinus, while alive, made this for himself, his wife Musa, and his son Primus, aged nine.
EDCS-14500733.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Sempronius Summinus for himself and his family.

EDCS-14500734.text_edition: \n Velleco Cacurdae filius et Deusa Ituli filia coniux et Angulato filio annorum X\n
EDCS-14500734.translation: Vellecus, son of Cacurdus, and his wife Deusa, daughter of Itulus, and their son Angulatus, aged ten.
EDCS-14500734.summary: A funerary monument for Vellecus, his wife Deusa, and their son Angulatus, likely commissioned by a family member.

EDCS-14500735.text_edition: \n Cirpo Seni filius et Pameta Deusonis filia coniux filiis filiabusque fecerunt et Autoscuttae nnorum XX et Prisco filio annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500735.translation: Cirpus, son of Senis, and his wife Pameta, daughter of Deusonis, and their children made this for themselves and for Autoscutta, aged twenty, and for their son Priscus, aged sixty.
EDCS-14500735.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Cirpus and his wife Pameta for their children and other family members.

EDCS-14500736.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Saturnus domu Ulpia Poetovione vivus fecit sibi et Pomponiae Secundinae coniugi et Insequenti filio annorum XV\n
EDCS-14500736.translation: Marcus Ulpius Saturninus, from the Ulpian tribe of Poetovio, while alive, made this for himself, his wife Pomponia Secundina, and his son Insequens, aged fifteen.
EDCS-14500736.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Ulpius Saturninus for himself and his family.

EDCS-14500738.text_edition: \n Nammonius Mussa et Kalandina coniux vivi et Saturninus Saturionis vivus fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500738.translation: Nammonius Mussa and his wife Kalandina, and Saturninus, son of Saturio, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14500738.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Nammonius Mussa, his wife Kalandina, and Saturninus, son of Saturio.

EDCS-14500739.text_edition: \n Caius Firminius Primus veteranus legionis II adiutricis et Covnerta Adabi filia coniux optima Caius Firminius Castricius aedilis Solvae vivus fecit sibi et Seiae Urbici filiae Litugenae uxori optimae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500739.translation: Gaius Firminius Primus, a veteran of Legio II Adjutrix, and his excellent wife Covnerta, daughter of Adabus, and Gaius Firminius Castricius, aedile of Solva, while alive, made this for himself, his excellent wife Seia Urbici, a native of Litugena, aged twenty.
EDCS-14500739.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Firminius Primus, his wife Covnerta, and Gaius Firminius Castricius, an official in Solva, for Castricius and his wife Seia Urbici.

EDCS-14500741.translation: To the departed spirits of Mansuetus, whose [lacuna] ...
EDCS-14500741.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Mansuetus, with missing information about the dedicator and relationship.

EDCS-14500742.text_edition: \n Pompeiae Theodorae annorum XXX parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500742.translation: For Pompeia Theodora, aged thirty, her parents made this.
EDCS-14500742.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeia Theodora, aged thirty, erected by her parents.

EDCS-14500744.text_edition: \n Nammonia Materiu viva fecit sibi et Gaio Sempronio Secundino marito decurioni Solvae et Gaio Sempronio Secundino filio librario consularis annorum XVIIII\n
EDCS-14500744.translation: Nammonia Materia, while alive, made this for herself, her husband Gaius Sempronius Secundinus, a decurion of Solva, and her son Gaius Sempronius Secundinus, a consular scribe, aged seventeen.
EDCS-14500744.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Nammonia Materia for herself, her husband who was a decurion, and her son who was a scribe.

EDCS-14500745.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Licini Trionis et Sabiniae Severinae coniugis Licini\n
EDCS-14500745.translation: To the departed spirits of Gaius Licinius Triones and his wife Sabinia Severina.
EDCS-14500745.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Licinius Triones and his wife Sabinia Severina.

EDCS-14500746.text_edition: \n Lucius Cantius Secundus vivus fecit sibi et Cantiae Iuni filiae Boniae uxori et Cantiae Luci filiae Boniatae\n
EDCS-14500746.translation: Lucius Cantius Secundus, while alive, made this for himself, his wife Cantia Bonia, daughter of Lucius Junius, and his daughter Cantia Boniatis, daughter of Lucius.
