EDCS-14500746.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Lucius Cantius Secundus for himself and his wife and daughter, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500747.text_edition: \n Insequens Senilis et Censoria Tervina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ingenuae Terti filiae Insequentis matri et Maximiano Maximi filio vitrico\n
EDCS-14500747.translation: Insequens Senilis and Censoria Tervina, alive, made this for themselves, their daughter Ingenua, mother of Insequens, and their stepson Maximian, son of Maximus.
EDCS-14500747.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Insequens Senilis and Censoria Tervina for themselves, their daughter, and stepson, probably from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500748.text_edition: \n Iunius Vercai filius vivus fecit sibi et Bugiae Secundi filiae coniugi annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500748.translation: Iunius, son of Vercai, while alive, made this for himself and his wife Bugia, daughter of Secundus, aged 30.
EDCS-14500748.summary: This funerary inscription records Iunius, son of Vercai, dedicating a monument for himself and his wife Bugia, aged 30, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500749.text_edition: \n Speratus Sironis et Sporilla Commodi liberta vivi fecerunt sibi\n
EDCS-14500749.translation: Speratus, son of Siron, and Sporilla, freedwoman of Commodus, alive, made this for themselves.
EDCS-14500749.summary: This monument was built by Speratus and Sporilla for themselves, probably a married couple, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500750.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Hostilia Cai filia Crispa \n
EDCS-14500750.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, and Minerva. Hostilia, daughter of Caius Crispus.
EDCS-14500750.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Capitoline Triad by Hostilia, daughter of Caius Crispus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500751.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano Lucius Campanius Celer sacerdos urbis Romae aeternae et Iulia Honorata coniux pro se et suis votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14500751.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Arubianus. Lucius Campanius Celer, priest of the eternal city of Rome, and his wife Iulia Honorata fulfilled their vow for themselves and their family with willing hearts and deservedly.
EDCS-14500751.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Campanius Celer, priest of Rome, and his wife Iulia Honorata, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500752.text_edition: \n Iuniano Iuni filio annorum XXXV Saxia Marci filia Amm coniugi et sibi\n
EDCS-14500752.translation: To Iunianus, son of Junius, aged 35. To his wife Saxia Amm, daughter of Marcus, and to himself.
EDCS-14500752.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iunianus, aged 35, and his wife Saxia Amm, for whom the monument was made, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500753.text_edition: \n Vercaius Titiae vivus fecit sibi et Boniae coniugi annorum L et Secundino Adiutoris filio annorum \n
EDCS-14500753.translation: Vercaius Titiae, alive, made this for himself, his wife Bonia, aged 50, and for Secundinus, son of Adiutor, aged [...]
EDCS-14500753.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Vercaius Titiae for himself, his wife Bonia (aged 50), and their son Secundinus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500754.text_edition: \n Accepto Atti Tutoris annorum XC et Mogiae Accepti filiae annorum L Fabro filius parentibus fecit\n
EDCS-14500754.translation: Fabro, son of Acceptus Tutor, aged 90, and Mogia, daughter of Acceptus, aged 50. He made this for his parents.
EDCS-14500754.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Fabro for his parents, Acceptus Tutor (aged 90) and Mogia (aged 50), likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500755.text_edition: \n Saturnus annorum LI et Vibia coniux et Elvima filia annorum XXX et Surio vivus filius Ianuarius annorum L\n
EDCS-14500755.translation: Saturnus, aged 51, and his wife Vibia, and their daughter Elvima, aged 30, and their son Surius Ianuarius, aged 50, while alive.
EDCS-14500755.summary: This funerary monument was created by Saturnus, his wife Vibia, their daughter Elvima (aged 30), and son Surius Ianuarius (aged 50), likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500756.text_edition: \n Caius Attius Propinqui libertus Senno annorum LX et Elvia Oclatius et Finita coniux vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500756.translation: Caius Attius Senno, freedman of Propinquus, aged 60, and Elvia, wife of Oclatius, and Finita, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500756.summary: This funerary monument, made by living individuals for themselves and possibly others, records the names and age of Caius Attius Senno and Elvia.

EDCS-14500757.text_edition: \n Sabinus Masculi et Candida Potentis vivi fecerunt sibi et Nigellioni filio militi legionis II Italicae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500757.translation: Sabinus, son of Masculus, and Candida, daughter of Potens, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Nigellio, a soldier in the II Italica Legion, aged 30.
EDCS-14500757.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sabinus and Candida for themselves and their deceased son Nigellio, a soldier in the II Italica Legion.

EDCS-14500758.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Salvianus veteranus legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae ex stratore consularis et Aurelia Martia coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Marco Aurelio Ursi<ci=G>no filio militi praetoriano cohortis IIII praetoriae stipendiorum III @(obito) annorum XX \n
EDCS-14500758.translation: Marcus Aurelius Salvianus, a veteran of the II Italica Legion, pious and faithful, Severian, from the rank of consular attendant, and Aurelia Martia, his wife, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Marcus Aurelius Ursicinus, a soldier in the Praetorian cohort IIII, with 3 years of service, who died aged 20.
EDCS-14500758.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Marcus Aurelius Salvianus, a veteran soldier and former attendant, and his wife Aurelia Martia, for themselves and their son Marcus Aurelius Ursicinus, a Praetorian guardsman.

EDCS-14500759.text_edition: \n Hostilius Tunger et Ingenua Vicari filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Tacito filio militi cohortis VII praetoriae annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500759.translation: Hostilius Tunger and Ingenua, daughter of Vicarius, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Tacitus, a soldier in cohort VII Praetoria, aged 30.
EDCS-14500759.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Hostilius Tunger and Ingenua for themselves and their son Tacitus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-14500760.text_edition: \n Candido Cassi filio annorum L et Candidae Ucci filiae coniugi filius fecit et Candidiano filio annorum XXI\n
EDCS-14500760.translation: His son made this for Candido, son of Cassius, aged 50, and for his wife Candida, daughter of Uccius, and for his son Candidianus, aged 21.
EDCS-14500760.summary: This epitaph records the dedication by a son for his father Candido, his mother Candida, and his brother Candidianus.

EDCS-14500762.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Dii libertae Quintae Iulius Dii libertus Amianthus et Iulia Dii liberta Amanda parentes vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500762.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Iulia Quinta, freedwoman of Diius. Iulius Amianthus, freedman of Diius, and Iulia Amanda, freedwoman of Diius, parents, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500762.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by parents, Iulius Amianthus and Iulia Amanda, to their deceased daughter Iulia Quinta.

EDCS-14500764.text_edition: \n Marcus Mogius Valentinus Vibi filius et Mogia Iustina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Mogio Urso filio militi cohortis I Brittonum annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500764.translation: Marcus Mogius Valentinus, son of Vibius, and his wife Mogia Iustina made this while alive for themselves and for their son Mogius Urso, a soldier in cohort I Brittonum, aged 30.
EDCS-14500764.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Marcus Mogius Valentinus and his wife Mogia Iustina for themselves and their son Mogius Urso, a soldier in the I Brittonum cohort.

EDCS-14500765.text_edition: \n Caius Vitalius Vitulus Tertiniae Sabinae coniugi annorum XXXII\n
EDCS-14500765.translation: For Caius Vitalius Vitulus, aged 32, and for his wife Tertinia Sabina, aged 32.
EDCS-14500765.summary: This inscription records the ages of Caius Vitalius Vitulus and his wife Tertinia Sabina.

EDCS-14500766.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco T filio Iucundo IIviro iure dicundo Flaviae Solvae et Atiliaeae parentibus I \n
EDCS-14500766.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Marcus T. Iucundus, former duovir with judicial authority, and his parents Flavia Solva and Atilia. [Made by] Iucundus.
EDCS-14500766.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus T. Iucundus, a former duovir, and his parents, and was likely erected by his son.

EDCS-14500767.translation: Passerinus made this while alive for himself and.
EDCS-14500767.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records Passerinus's act of setting up a monument for himself and at least one other person.

EDCS-14500768.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Menelao Lucia fil annorum? \n
EDCS-14500768.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Menelaus, son of Lucia, [who died aged...] years.
EDCS-14500768.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Menelaus.

EDCS-14500769.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Debulsori sic Caius Iulius Probus miles legionis X geminae Severianae votum solvit libens laetus merito Maximo II et Agricola consulibus\n
EDCS-14500769.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Deliverer, Gaius Julius Probus, a soldier of the tenth Gemini Severiana legion, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Maximus II and Agricola were consuls.
EDCS-14500769.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier of the Tenth Legion, dating to the consulship of Maximus II and Agricola.

EDCS-14500770.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Hercules Augustus.

EDCS-14500771.text_edition: \n Caius Atilius po annorum LXX et Melissaconiux Caius Atilius Emeritus parentibus fecit sibi et Atiliae ae uxori et ae filiae annorum III Emeritus supra scriptus decurio municipii Claudi Teurniae\n
EDCS-14500771.translation: Gaius Atilius Emeritus made this for his parents, Gaius Atilius [---], aged 70, and Melissaconia, and for himself, and for his wife Atilia, and for their daughter, aged 3. Gaius Atilius Emeritus, mentioned above, was a decurion of the municipium of Claudius Teurnia.
EDCS-14500771.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Atilius Emeritus for his parents, himself, his wife, and daughter, identifying him as a decurion.

EDCS-14500772.text_edition: \n Iuniano Burrani filio annorum L Sucela Ucconis filia marito et Terentino et Iulianae Tertini filiae nepti\n
EDCS-14500772.translation: To Iunianus, son of Burranus, aged 50. Sucela, daughter of Ucconis, [set this up] for her husband and for Terentius and for Iuliana, daughter of Tertinus, his granddaughter.
EDCS-14500772.summary: This funerary inscription honors Iunianus, his wife Sucela, and their granddaughter Iuliana.

EDCS-14500773.text_edition: \n Caius Sabinius Primigenius et Sabinia Magiona Cai Sabini filia Veci Cinti filio nnorum XXII et sibi et Sucellae nepti annorum XII\n
EDCS-14500773.translation: Gaius Sabinius Primigenius and Sabinia Magiona, daughter of Gaius Sabinius, [set this up] for Vecius Cinti, aged 22, and for themselves, and for Sucella, granddaughter, aged 12.
EDCS-14500773.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Vecius Cinti, Sabinia Magiona, and Sucella, with Gaius Sabinius Primigenius also being commemorated.

EDCS-14500774.text_edition: \n Sato Togionis filio et Bussullae Saggonis filiae coniugi heres fecit\n
EDCS-14500774.translation: For Sato, son of Togio, and Bussulla, daughter of Saggon, his wife, the heir made this.
EDCS-14500774.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by an heir for Sato and his wife Bussulla.

EDCS-14500776.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vicario Suri liberto et Profuturae coniugi Septumi sic et Secundinae libertae Viatorina @(obita) annorum XXXXV et Libertio vivus fecit sibi et coniugi filiis\n
EDCS-14500776.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Viatorina, who died at 45 years of age, a freedwoman of Vicarius Suri, and Profutura, his wife, and Secundina, a freedwoman. Viatorina made this, living, for herself, her wife Profutura, and her children.
EDCS-14500776.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Viatorina and Profutura, with the inscription being made by Viatorina for herself and her family.

EDCS-14500777.text_edition: \n Vibius Helvisiani et Attia Brigantia coniux annorum XXXX et Boniata coniux et Vibiano filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500777.translation: To Vibius, son of Helvisianus, and Attia Brigantia, his wife, aged 40, and Boniata, his wife, and Vibianus, his son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500777.summary: This funerary inscription honors Vibius and his wives and son.

EDCS-14500778.text_edition: \n Buccioni Ressimari filio et Taparuni Senici filiae coniugi et Vindoni filio annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-14500778.translation: To Buccio, son of Ressimar, and Taparuna, daughter of Senicus, his wife, and Vindo, their son, aged 40.
EDCS-14500778.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Buccio, his wife Taparuna, and their son Vindo.

EDCS-14500779.text_edition: \n Propeoni Ottonis filio et Togiae Cassi filiae coniugi et Aelio Parato\n
EDCS-14500779.translation: To Propeonia, daughter of Otto, and to her wife Togia, daughter of Cassius, and to Aelius Paratus.
EDCS-14500779.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Propeonia, Togia, and Aelius Paratus, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-14500781.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus perpetuae securitati Aurelius Saturio veteranus cohortis V Breucorum vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Faustinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500781.translation: To the departed spirits, for eternal security. Aurelius Saturio, a veteran of the Vth Cohort of Breuci, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Claudia Faustina, who died at the age of 60.
EDCS-14500781.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by a veteran for himself and his deceased wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500782.text_edition: \n Saturio Talionis vivus fecit sibi et Banonae Bardi annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500782.translation: Saturio, son of Talio, made this while alive for himself and for Banona, daughter of Bardo, aged 60.
EDCS-14500782.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Saturio for himself and his deceased wife Banona, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500783.text_edition: \n Pluncus Victoris et Caletiu Adnamati vivi fecerunt sibi et Banonae filiae annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-14500783.translation: Pluncus, son of Victor, and Caletius, son of Adnamatus, made this while alive for themselves and for their daughter Banona, aged 18.
EDCS-14500783.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by two individuals for themselves and their deceased daughter Banona, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500785.text_edition: \n Aurelio Candido militi legionis II Augustae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500785.translation: To Aurelius Candidus, a soldier of the II Augusta legion, aged 20.
EDCS-14500785.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young soldier of the II Augusta legion, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500786.text_edition: \n Quintianus Cittonis filius et Nigella liberta Boudes vivi fecerunt sibi et Adnamato filio annorum VI\n
EDCS-14500786.translation: Quintianus, son of Citton, and Nigella, freedwoman of Boudes, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Adnamatus, aged 6.
EDCS-14500786.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by a couple for themselves and their deceased young son, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500787.text_edition: \n Muntissius Privati annorum XXXX et Quinta Aspronis viva fecit sibi et marito\n
EDCS-14500787.translation: Muntissius, son of Privatus, aged 40, and Quinta, daughter of Aspronius, made this while alive for herself and her husband.
EDCS-14500787.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by a woman for herself and her husband, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500788.text_edition: \n Quarta Camulae liberta fecit sibi et Totto filio annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500788.translation: Quarta, freedwoman of Camula, made this for herself and for her son Totto, aged 20.
EDCS-14500788.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by a freedwoman for herself and her deceased son, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500789.text_edition: \n Quintus Septueius Adiectus sibi et Samicantuni Goutonis filiae uxori vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500789.translation: Quintus Septueius Adiectus made this for himself and for his wife Samicantune, daughter of Gouto.
EDCS-14500789.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Quintus Septueius Adiectus for himself and his wife, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500790.text_edition: \n Serotinus et Verecunda Cai Anni Rufini liberta vivi fecerunt sibi et Quintae filiae annorum XIII\n
EDCS-14500790.translation: Serotinus and Verecunda, freedwoman of Caius Annius Rufus, made this while alive for themselves and for their daughter Quita, aged 13.
EDCS-14500790.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by a couple for themselves and their deceased young daughter, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14500791.text_edition: \n Vibia Terti filia Gracilio Secundino marito et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500791.translation: Vibia Tertia, daughter of Gracilius, made this for her husband Secundinus and herself while alive.
EDCS-14500791.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Vibia Tertia for herself and her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500792.text_edition: \n Cribuionis filius annorum LX hic situs est et Blendo coniux annorum LXXX\n
EDCS-14500792.translation: Cribuio, son of [unknown], aged 60, lies here, and his wife Blenda, aged 80.
EDCS-14500792.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cribuio and his wife Blenda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500794.text_edition: \n Totulo Mufonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Giamillae Necorni filiae coniugi annorum LX\n
EDCS-14500794.translation: Totulus, son of Mufonis, made this while alive for himself and his wife Giamilla, daughter of Necornius, aged 60.
EDCS-14500794.summary: This funerary monument was set up by Totulus for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500795.text_edition: \n C Tertullinus et D Tertulla coniux et Tertino filio et Kalandino filio militi legionis X et Crispa et Digna\n
EDCS-14500795.translation: C. Tertullinus, and his wife D. Tertulla, and their son Tertinus, and their son Kalandinus, a soldier in the 10th Legion, and Crispa, and Digna.
EDCS-14500795.summary: This inscription lists family members, including a soldier, and likely served as a funerary or commemorative marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500796.text_edition: \n Nammo Cabalionis filio annorum LX et Soliae Loturi filiae annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-14500796.translation: Nammo, son of Cabalio, aged 60, and Solia, daughter of Loturus, aged 40.
EDCS-14500796.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nammo and Solia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500797.text_edition: \n Marcus Annius Romanius Successa Atuali filia coniux Annio Saturnino filio\n
EDCS-14500797.translation: Marcus Annius Romanius, and his wife Successa, daughter of Atualis, for Annio Saturninus, their son.
EDCS-14500797.summary: This inscription records family members, likely for a funerary or commemorative purpose, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500798.text_edition: \n Masculus Ituli filius sibi et Sabinae Quinti filiae coniugi testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-14500798.translation: Masculus, son of Itulus, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and his wife Sabina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-14500798.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Masculus through his will for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500799.text_edition: \n Cucio Romulo Quarto KA F I\n
EDCS-14500799.translation: To Cucius Romulus, son of Quartus, KA F I.
EDCS-14500799.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cucius Romulus, dating to the Roman Imperial period; the abbreviation 'KA F I' is unclear.

EDCS-14500800.summary: This is an incomplete funerary or dedicatory inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500801.text_edition: \n Adiutor Romani filius vivus fecit sibi et Saturninae Massae filiae coniugi annorum L et Attico filio et Luciae Veturiae Pontentinae coniugi pientissimae annorum XL\n
EDCS-14500801.translation: Adiutor, son of Romanus, made this while alive for himself and his wife Saturnina, daughter of Massa, aged 50, and for his son Atticus, and for Lucia Veturia Pontentina, his most devoted wife, aged 40.
EDCS-14500801.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Adiutor for himself, his two wives Saturnina and Lucia, and his son Atticus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500802.text_edition: \n Quintus Capitonius Potens vivus fecit sibi et Belliciae Spectatae coniugi optimae annorum XXXVII et Capitoniae Veneriae coniugi\n
EDCS-14500802.translation: Quintus Capitonius Potens made this, while alive, for himself and his excellent wife Bellicia Spectata, aged 37, and for his wife Capitoina Venere.
EDCS-14500802.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Capitonius Potens, his wife Bellicia Spectata, and another wife Capitoina Venere, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500803.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus pro salute sua et Baebiae Proculeiae coniugi votum solvit\n
EDCS-14500803.translation: Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Repeller, for his own safety and that of his wife Baebia Proculeia.
EDCS-14500803.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus for his well-being and that of his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500804.text_edition: \n Calventius Masculi filius vivus fecit sibi et Avetae Adiutoris filiae coniugi annorum L\n
EDCS-14500804.translation: Calventius, son of Masculus, made this, while alive, for himself and for Aveta, daughter of Adiutor, his wife, aged 50.
EDCS-14500804.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calventius and his wife Aveta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500805.text_edition: \n Urbano Ressimari filio annorum LXX et Resillae Adnamati filiae coniugi annorum LX et Iantumaro filio annorum XXX et iae filiae annorum XVIII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500805.translation: The heirs arranged for this to be made for Urbano, son of Ressimar, aged 70, and for Resilla, daughter of Adnamatus, his wife, aged 60, and for their son Iantumaro, aged 30, and for their daughter, aged 18.
EDCS-14500805.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by heirs for a family, including parents, son, and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500806.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Maximianus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Respectae coniugi vivae et Tito Flavio Maximo militi legionis XIIII geminae filio annorum XXVII\n
EDCS-14500806.translation: Titus Flavius Maximianus made this, while alive, for himself and his wife Iulia Respecta, who is also alive, and for his son Titus Flavius Maximus, a soldier in the Legio XIIII Gemina, aged 27.
EDCS-14500806.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Maximianus, his wife Iulia Respecta, and their son Titus Flavius Maximus, a soldier, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500807.text_edition: \n Maleius Atressi filius annorum LXXX Blassia Quinti filia Brutta uxor annorum filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500807.translation: Maleius, son of Atressus, aged 80, and Blassia Brutta, daughter of Quintus, his wife, aged [..], arranged for this to be made for their son.
EDCS-14500807.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by a couple for their son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500808.text_edition: \n Giamillius Senilis vivus fecit sibi et B Valentinae et Giamillio Priscino filio et Avitae Iustini et Giamillio Valentino filio et Giamilliae Valentinae parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500808.translation: Giamillius Senilis made this, while alive, for himself and for B. Valentina, and for his son Giamillius Priscinus, and for Avita, daughter of Iustinus, and for Giamillius Valentinus, his son, and for Giamillia Valentina. The parents made this.
EDCS-14500808.summary: This funerary epitaph was made by parents for their children and possibly a relative, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500810.text_edition: \n Secundinus C annorum LX et Iulia Litugena et Secundinae filiae annorum\n
EDCS-14500810.translation: Secundinus C., aged 60, and Iulia Litugena, and for their daughter Secundina, aged [..].
EDCS-14500810.summary: This funerary epitaph records a man, a woman, and their daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500811.text_edition: \n S SERQVIA coniux Iuniae et Iuliae filiae filiis nepotibus\n
EDCS-14500811.translation: Serqvia, wife of Iunius, and for their daughter Iulia, and for her sons and grandsons.
EDCS-14500811.summary: This funerary epitaph records Serqvia, her daughter Iulia, and her descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500812.text_edition: \n Caio Vindio Crescenti et Claudiae Crispinae et Vindio Verino filio militi cohortis urbanae annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-14500812.translation: For Gaius Vindius Crescens, and for Claudia Crispa, and for their son Vindius Verinus, a soldier in the urban cohort, aged 23.
EDCS-14500812.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a family, including a soldier of the urban cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500814.text_edition: \n Ittu Peculiaris annorum LXXX et Successae coniugi annorum LXXX Vindio Ittunis filius vivus fecit sibi et Emeritae coniugi et Dubitato filio an(ni) I\n
EDCS-14500814.translation: Ittu Peculiaris, aged 80, and his wife Successa, aged 80, were honored by Vindius Ittunis, their son, who erected this monument for himself, his wife Emerita, and his son Dubitatus, aged 1.
EDCS-14500814.summary: This funerary monument records the ages and familial relationships of Ittu Peculiaris, Successa, Vindius Ittunis, Emerita, and Dubitatus, with Vindius Ittunis as the dedicator.

EDCS-14500815.text_edition: \n Valerius Togionis filius Vibennae coniugi et Valerianus filius annorum XVI\n
EDCS-14500815.translation: Valerius, son of Togio, for his wife Vibenna, and for his son Valerianus, aged 16.
EDCS-14500815.summary: This funerary inscription records the names and ages of Valerius, his wife Vibenna, and his son Valerianus, with Valerius as the dedicator.

EDCS-14500817.text_edition: \n mocca Adiutoris filius et Tacita Proi annorum LX coniux Arsto filius et Aelia Iuliana coniux Aelia Iulia filia et Aelius Iulianus et Claudia Iustina coniux Ursulus Marcelli annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500817.translation: Mocca, son of Adiutor, and his wife Tacita Proi, aged 60; Arstus, their son, and his wife Aelia Iuliana; Aelius Iulianus; and Claudia Iustina, wife of Ursulus Marcellus, aged 30.
EDCS-14500817.summary: This funerary inscription lists multiple family members including Mocca, Tacita Proi, Arstus, Aelia Iuliana, Aelius Iulianus, Claudia Iustina, and Ursulus Marcellus, along with their ages and relationships.

EDCS-14500818.text_edition: \n Lucius Laetilius Castus vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Primigeniae coniugi annorum XXXX et Anniae Valent[inae ------\n
EDCS-14500818.translation: Lucius Laetilius Castus, while alive, erected this for himself, his wife Claudia Primigenia, aged 40, and for Annia Valentina...
EDCS-14500818.summary: This monument was erected by Lucius Laetilius Castus for himself, his wife Claudia Primigenia, and Annia Valentina, with the dedicator being alive at the time of erection.

EDCS-14500819.text_edition: \n militi legionis X? g(eminae)? p(iae)? f(idelis) et Tiberio Claudio Blando filio annorum XXX et Tiberio Claudio Tertio militi legionis X geminae piae fidelis beneficario conssulari annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500819.translation: ...a soldier of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, and for his son Tiberius Claudius Blanus, aged 30, and Tiberius Claudius Tertius, a soldier of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, a beneficarius consularis, aged 30.
EDCS-14500819.summary: This inscription records two soldiers of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, Tiberius Claudius Blanus and Tiberius Claudius Tertius, along with the latter's rank, and their ages.

EDCS-14500821.text_edition: \n Finitus Accepti annorum L Cirpia Elvima coniux viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500821.translation: Finitus Accepti, aged 50; his wife Cirpia Elvima, while alive, erected this for herself and for him.
EDCS-14500821.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cirpia Elvima for her deceased husband Finitus Accepti and for herself, indicating she was alive at the time of erection.

EDCS-14500822.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Avitus et Caixu Rantilli filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Tiberio Iulio Adnnamo annorum IX\n
EDCS-14500822.translation: Tiberius Iulius Avitus and Caixu, daughter of Rantillus, while alive, erected this for themselves and for Tiberius Iulius Adnnamos, aged 9.
EDCS-14500822.summary: This monument was erected by Tiberius Iulius Avitus and Caixu for themselves and for Tiberius Iulius Adnnamos, with the dedicators being alive at the time of erection.

EDCS-14500824.text_edition: \n Caio Sacretio Spectatino IIviro iure dicundo Flaviae Solvae annorum L et Secundiniae Severinae coniugi filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500824.translation: Flavia Solva, aged 50, and her wife Secundinia Severina were honored by their sons, who arranged for this to be made, by Gaius Sacretinius Spectatinus, a duovir iure dicundo.
EDCS-14500824.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by sons for their mother Flavia Solva and her wife Secundinia Severina, with Gaius Sacretinius Spectatinus holding the office of duovir iure dicundo.

EDCS-14500826.text_edition: \n Calventius Iutumari filius obitus annorum LV Iantumara coniux eius Severinus filius eius Ursa filia\n
EDCS-14500826.translation: Calventius, son of Iutumara, died aged 55. His wife Iantumara, his son Severinus, and his daughter Ursa are recorded.
EDCS-14500826.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and age of Calventius, son of Iutumara, and lists his surviving wife Iantumara, son Severinus, and daughter Ursa.

EDCS-14500827.text_edition: \n Atitto Atevali filius nnorum LXXV hic situs est Uttu Helvissonis filia uxor Mommus Atittonis filius Conginna Quordaionis filia filius nurus parentibus fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500827.translation: Atitto, son of Atevali, aged 75, lies here; Uttu, daughter of Helvissonis, his wife; Mommus, son of Atitto; and Conginna, daughter of Quordaio, his daughter-in-law; his son and daughter-in-law made this for their parents.
EDCS-14500827.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Atitto, son of Atevali, and Uttu, daughter of Helvissonis, with their son Mommus and daughter-in-law Conginna having erected the monument for them.

EDCS-14500828.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Viis Semitibusque pro salute sua Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500828.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Roads and Paths, Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-14500828.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter and the roads for the well-being of the dedicator, Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades.

EDCS-14500829.text_edition: \n Caius Attius Iustus vivus fecit sibi et Tocatiae Cai filiae Romanae coniugi optimae obitae annorum L filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500829.translation: Caius Attius Iustus, while alive, made this for himself and for his excellent wife Romana, daughter of Caius, who had died, aged 50; his sons arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-14500829.summary: This tombstone records the self-funded burial of Caius Attius Iustus and his deceased wife Romana, arranged by their sons.

EDCS-14500830.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Lucius Petilius Alianus ex voto\n
EDCS-14500830.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Lucius Petilius Alianus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14500830.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Lucius Petilius Alianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14500831.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nius Victor aedilicius civitatis Iuvavensium Q Dignilla eiius sic uriae Marcianae filiae piissimae @(obitae) nnum XXX et suis vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500831.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Q. Dignilla made this for her most devoted daughter Marciana, aged 30, and for herself and her husband, formerly aedile of the city of Juvavenses, willingly.
EDCS-14500831.summary: This tomb inscription is a dedication by Q. Dignilla to her deceased daughter Marciana and for herself and her husband, a former aedile of Juvavenses.

EDCS-14500832.text_edition: \n Alpinus Silvani filius obitus nnorum VIII\n
EDCS-14500832.translation: Alpinus, son of Silvanus, died aged 8 years.
EDCS-14500832.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of a child named Alpinus at the age of 8.

EDCS-14500833.translation: To Antonius Gemellus, aged [---], and Occo [---].
EDCS-14500833.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the names and ages of two individuals, possibly a tomb inscription.

EDCS-14500834.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Albius Florus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500834.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Albius Florus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500834.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus by Albius Florus.

EDCS-14500835.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto nostro Pudentius Maximinus veteranus ex decurione alae Tampianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-14500835.translation: To our Hercules Augustus. Pudentius Maximinus, a veteran and former decurion of the Tampiana cavalry wing, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500835.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus by Pudentius Maximinus, a veteran military officer.

EDCS-14500836.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Venustinus summarum signum Iovis Arubiani cultoribus cum base donum dedit\n
EDCS-14500836.translation: Venustinus gave this statue of Jupiter Arubianus with its base as a gift to the worshippers of Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-14500836.summary: This inscription records a gift of a statue of Jupiter Arubianus by Venustinus to the cult followers of Jupiter.

EDCS-14500837.text_edition: \n Mercurio aedem fecit et signum posuit Caius Togionius Cupitus Caius Togionius Summus filius refecit\n
EDCS-14500837.translation: Caius Togionius Cupitus built a temple and set up a statue to Mercury; Caius Togionius Summus, his son, restored it.
EDCS-14500837.summary: This inscription records the original dedication of a temple and statue to Mercury by Caius Togionius Cupitus, and its restoration by his son, Caius Togionius Summus.

EDCS-14500839.text_edition: \n pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti [et Getae Caesaris?] Iuvavenses donum dederunt\n
EDCS-14500839.translation: The people of Iuvavum dedicated this gift for the safety of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus [and Caesar Geta?].
EDCS-14500839.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the people of Iuvavum (modern Salzburg) for the well-being of the Roman emperors Severus and Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500841.text_edition: \n Quintus Munatius Quinti filius Ouffentina sic Lupus Saena miles cohortis VIIII praetoriae |(centuriae) Ligusti vixit nnos XXX militavit nnos XI heres faciendum curavit Aulus Terentius Priscus\n
EDCS-14500841.translation: Quintus Munatius Lupus, son of Quintus, of the Ouffentina tribe, a soldier of the 9th praetorian cohort, in the centuria of Ligustus, lived for 30 years and served for 11 years. His heir had this made. Aulus Terentius Priscus.
EDCS-14500841.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Munatius Lupus, a praetorian guardsman who died at age 30 after 11 years of service, with his heir commissioning the monument.

EDCS-14500842.text_edition: \n Lucius Naevius Luci filius Proculus |(centurio) cohortis Asturum nnorum XXV stipendiorum V Pacciae Cai libertae Placidae et Pacciae Cai libertae Ansirae et Naeviae Luci libertae Clementi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500842.translation: Lucius Naevius Proculus, son of Lucius, a centurion of the Astur cohort, aged 25 and with 5 years of service, made this monument while alive for Paccia Placida, freedwoman of Gaius, and Paccia Ansira, freedwoman of Gaius, and Naevia Clementia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-14500842.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Naevius Proculus, a centurion, for himself and three women, likely his family members, indicating he commissioned it during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500843.text_edition: \n Honoratius vilicus et Avetonia Veneria coniuges vivi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500843.translation: Honoratius, the farm manager, and his wife Avetonia Veneria, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500843.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned during their lifetimes by Honoratius, a vilicus (farm manager), and his wife Avetonia Veneria.

EDCS-14500844.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Hermes vivus fecit sibi et Felici filio annorum VI\n
EDCS-14500844.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Hermes made this for himself and his son Felix, aged 6, while alive.
EDCS-14500844.summary: This tombstone was set up by Titus Flavius Hermes for himself and his young son Felix, indicating it was commissioned during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500845.text_edition: \n Fronto Cossonis filius annorum LXXX Banona Agisi filia obita annorum LXXXV\n
EDCS-14500845.translation: Fronto, son of Cossonis, aged 80. Banona, daughter of Agisi, aged 85.
EDCS-14500845.summary: This inscription simply records the names and ages of two deceased individuals, Fronto and Banona, likely a married couple.

EDCS-14500846.text_edition: \n sibi et Iuliae Emeritae coniugi pientissimae annorum LXXXX vivis fecit\n
EDCS-14500846.translation: He made this for himself and his most dutiful wife Iulia Emerita, aged 90, while alive.
EDCS-14500846.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned during the lifetime of a man for himself and his wife Iulia Emerita, who lived to be 90.

EDCS-14500847.text_edition: \n Iuliae Hilarae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XL Titus Cornelius Marcellinus et Paternia Florina coniux vivi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500847.translation: Titus Cornelius Marcellinus and his wife Paternia Florina made this for themselves while alive, for Iulia Hilara, their wife, deceased, aged 40.
EDCS-14500847.summary: This tombstone was commissioned during the lifetimes of Titus Cornelius Marcellinus and his wife Paternia Florina for their deceased wife/mother Iulia Hilara, who died at age 40.

EDCS-14500848.text_edition: \n Iuliae Procillae @(obitae) annorum XLVIII Lucius Cocceius Cupitus maritus et Iulia Iuniana et Coccei Proculus Florentina Cupitus filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500848.translation: Lucius Cocceius Cupitus, her husband, and his children Iulia Iuniana and Coccei Proculus and Florentina Cupitus made this for Iulia Procilla, deceased, aged 48.
EDCS-14500848.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the husband and children for Iulia Procilla, who died at the age of 48.

EDCS-14500849.text_edition: \n Iustiniae Aprae coniugi pientissimae @(obitae) nnorum XXXVII Marcus Lollius Victor\n
EDCS-14500849.translation: Marcus Lollius Victor [erected this] for his most dutiful wife Iustinia Apra, deceased, aged 37.
EDCS-14500849.summary: This tombstone was commissioned by Marcus Lollius Victor for his wife Iustinia Apra, who died at the age of 37.

EDCS-14500850.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Lollio Achillae @(obito) annorum LXV et Agilliae Gallae @(obitae) nnorum LXX Albia Melitine merentibus fecit\n
EDCS-14500850.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Albia Melitine made this for the deserving Lucius Lollius Achillas, who died at the age of 65, and Agillia Galla, who died at the age of 70.
EDCS-14500850.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Albia Melitine for her deceased relatives, Lucius Lollius Achillas and Agillia Galla.

EDCS-14500851.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lollius Noricus et Iulia Ingenua uxor et Iuliae Secundae sorori et Aquilino fratri sibi et suis vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500851.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lollius Noricus, his wife Iulia Ingenua, for their sister Iulia Secunda, their brother Aquilinus, and for themselves and their descendants, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500851.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Lollius Noricus and his family for themselves and deceased relatives, made during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500853.text_edition: \n Rennius Firmus Samianta Viveni Urso filio obito annorum XXX vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500853.translation: Rennius Firmus made this while alive for his son Vivienus Urso, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500853.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Rennius Firmus for his deceased son, Vivienus Urso, made during the father's lifetime.

EDCS-14500855.text_edition: \n Saxsioni sic Volovici liberto Aulia Venusta viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500855.translation: Aulia Venusta made this while alive for her freedman Saxsio, son of Volovicus, and for herself.
EDCS-14500855.summary: This is a tombstone established by Aulia Venusta for her freedman and herself, made during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500856.text_edition: \n Sextus Titianus vivus fecit sibi et suis\n
EDCS-14500856.translation: Sextus Titianus made this while alive for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-14500856.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Sextus Titianus for himself and his family, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500857.text_edition: \n Statuto filio dulcissimo obito annorum XXXI Albius Leonas et Statuta coniuges carissimi et sibi posterisque suis fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500857.translation: Albius Leonas and his wife Statuta made this for their dearest son, Statutus, who died at the age of 31, and for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-14500857.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Albius Leonas and his wife for their son and for their family, made during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500860.text_edition: \n Carissimae et filio Ingenuo obito annorum XXX vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500860.translation: He made this while alive for his dearest daughter and for his son Ingenuus, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500860.summary: This is a tombstone set up by an unnamed father for his deceased daughter and son, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500861.summary: This is a tombstone erected by someone identified only by the initials POM, made during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500866.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcello Prima Nivi viva fecit sibi et Mattio Seccio hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-14500866.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcella Prima Nivi made this monument while alive for herself, for Mattius Seccus, and she stipulated that no heir should inherit it.
EDCS-14500866.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Marcella Prima Nivi for herself and Mattius Seccus, with a clause excluding heirs.

EDCS-14500867.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae sacrum pro salutem sic ddominorum nnostrorum Maximini et Constantini et Licini semper Auggustorum Aurelius Senecio vir perfectissimus dux templum numini eius ex voto a novo fieri iussit per instantiam Valeri Sambarrae praepositi eqquitibus Dalmatis Aquesianis comitatensibus libens laetus merito ob victoriam factam IV Kalendas Iulias Andronico et Probo consulibus\n
EDCS-14500867.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. For the safety of our lords, the ever-August Emperors Maximinius, Constantine, and Licinius, Aurelius Senecio, a most accomplished governor, ordered a temple to her divine power to be rebuilt from a vow, through the initiative of Valerius Sambarra, prefect of the equites of the Dalmatian Aquesiani regiment, willingly, gladly, and deservedly, following a victory on the fourth day before the Kalends of July in the consulship of Andronicus and Probus.
EDCS-14500867.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a temple to Victoria Augusta by Aurelius Senecio, a governor, to celebrate a victory during the consulship of Andronicus and Probus.

EDCS-14500868.text_edition: \n rio Iuvavo et Veriae Verae Metilia Marciana marito et filiae et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500868.translation: To Iovius, Veria Vera, and for herself, Metilia Marciana made this while alive for her husband, her daughter, and herself.
EDCS-14500868.summary: A funerary monument for family members, dedicated by Metilia Marciana.

EDCS-14500869.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Victorinus Martialis filius obitus annorum LV Bessa Iuvenis filia uxor @(obita) annorum XLV Novella Essibni filia obita annorum XVIII Victorinus parentibus et coniugi et Victorinae filiae fecit qui per luem vita functi sunt Mamertino et Rufo consulibus et Aurelio Iustino fratri militi legionis II Italicae stipendiorum X @(obito) annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500869.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Victorinus Martialis, son, who died at the age of 55. Bessa Iuvenis, daughter and wife, who died at the age of 45. Novella Essibni, daughter, who died at the age of 18. Victorinus made this for his parents, his wife, and his daughter Victorina, who met their end through a flood, in the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus, and for his brother Aurelius Iustinus, a soldier of the II Italian Legion with 10 years of service, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-14500869.summary: A funerary inscription for a family, noting the cause of death as a flood, dating to the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus.

EDCS-14500870.text_edition: \n Lucio Terentio Vero IIviro Teurniae praefecto iure dicundo obito annorum et Seppiae Cai filiae Praesentinae Lucius Terentius Verinus et Terentius Praesentinus parentibus optumis sic fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500870.translation: For Lucius Terentius Verus, duovir, prefect for dispensing justice in Teurnia, who died at the age of [..], and for Seppia Praesentina, daughter of Caius. Lucius Terentius Verinus and Terentius Praesentinus made this for their excellent parents.
EDCS-14500870.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Terentius Verus and Seppia Praesentina, dedicated by their sons.

EDCS-14500871.text_edition: \n Lucio Attonio Adnamato aedilicio Teurniae et Secundae Vogitouti filiae uxori \n
EDCS-14500871.translation: For Lucius Attonius Adnamatus, aedilician official in Teurnia, and for his wife Secunda, daughter of Vogitoutos.
EDCS-14500871.summary: A dedication to Lucius Attonius Adnamatus and his wife Secunda, likely for an official position or a memorial.

EDCS-14500872.text_edition: \n Bellicus Secundi filius vivus fecit sibi et Paulinae coniugi obitae annorum L et Secundino filio\n
EDCS-14500872.translation: Bellicus, son of Secundus, made this while alive for himself, for his wife Paulina, who died at the age of 50, and for his son Secundinus.
EDCS-14500872.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Bellicus for himself and his deceased wife and son.

EDCS-14500873.text_edition: \n Cupito Secundi obito nnorum LXXXI et Aveton<t>iae Romanae matri Secundus filius fecit\n
EDCS-14500873.translation: For Cupito Secundi, who died at the age of 81, and for his mother Avetonia Romana. Secundus, his son, made this.
EDCS-14500873.summary: A funerary monument for Cupito Secundi and his mother Avetonia Romana, dedicated by Secundus.

EDCS-14500874.text_edition: \n Bedaio Augusto sacrum Alounarum sic Setonius Maximianus et Firminius Firminianus IIviri Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus\n
EDCS-14500874.translation: Sacred to Bedaius Augustus. Thus Setonius Maximianus and Firminius Firminianus, duovirs, [made this] in the perpetual consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelius.
EDCS-14500874.summary: A dedication to the divinity Bedaius Augustus, made by two duovirs during a perpetual consulship.

EDCS-14500875.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino maximo semper Augusto bono rei publicae natus sic milia passuum XX\n
EDCS-14500875.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, greatest ever august, good for the state, born 20 miles.
EDCS-14500875.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine I, possibly marking a distance or location related to him.

EDCS-14500877.text_edition: \n In honorem domus divinae Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano et sancto Bedaio Vindius Verus beneficarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae ex voto posuit Idibus Mais Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto II et Marcello II consulibus\n
EDCS-14500877.translation: In honor of the divine house, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus and the holy Bedaius. Vindius Verus, beneficarius consularis of the pious and faithful II Italian Legion Severiana, set this up in fulfillment of a vow on the Ides of May, during the second consulship of our lord Severus Alexander Augustus and the second consulship of Marcellus.
EDCS-14500877.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter and Bedaius by a military official of the II Italian Legion during the consulship of Severus Alexander and Marcellus.

EDCS-14500878.text_edition: \n Vivi fecerunt Acutus Luci Mari Nobilis libertus et Amanda Pametae libertae\n
EDCS-14500878.translation: While alive, they made this: Acutus, freedman of Lucius Marius Nobilis, and Amanda, freedwoman of Pameta.
EDCS-14500878.summary: A funerary monument for two freedpersons, Acutus and Amanda, who dedicated it for themselves while alive.

EDCS-14500879.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Cornelio Ca |(centurioni) legionis XX militi in praetorio annos |(centurioni) annos IIII vixit annos fecerunt Cornelii Niceforus et Euprepes liberti eius patrono bene merenti memoriam posuerunt opto terra sit levis tibi\n
EDCS-14500879.translation: To the Divine Shades and for eternal security. Cornelius Ca[nturio], a soldier in the Praetorian Guard of the Twentieth Legion, for four years served as centurion. His freedmen, Cornelii Niceforus and Euprepes, erected this in memory of their well-deserving patron. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-14500879.summary: A tombstone for Cornelius, a centurion in the Praetorian Guard, erected by his freedmen, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500880.text_edition: \n et Lucius Cassius Potentinus IIviri Cilone II et Libone consulibus XV Kalendas Novembres\n
EDCS-14500880.translation: and Lucius Cassius Potentinus, IIvir, in the consulship of Cilo II and Libo, on the 18th day of October.
EDCS-14500880.summary: A fragmentary record of a date in the consulship of Cilo II and Libo, mentioning Lucius Cassius Potentinus as a IIvir, likely from the late 1st century BC or early 1st century AD.

EDCS-14500881.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodio Mariano frumentario legionis VII geminae Lucanius Laurentius faceundum sic curavit\n
EDCS-14500881.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucanius Laurentius saw to the making of this for Clodius Marianus, a frumentarius of the Legio VII Gemina of Lucania.
EDCS-14500881.summary: A tombstone for Clodius Marianus, a frumentarius of Legio VII Gemina, dedicated by Lucanius Laurentius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500882.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano et Bedaio sancto Tullius Iuvenis beneficarius consularis legionis II Italicae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito Idibus Maiis domino [nostro] [Imperatore Antonino] II et Sacerdote consulibus\n
EDCS-14500882.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Arubianus and to the holy Bedaio, Tullius Iuvenis, a beneficarius consularis of the Legio II Italica Antoniniana, willingly paid his vow deservedly, on the Ides of May, under our lord [Emperor Antoninus] II and Sacerdos as consuls.
EDCS-14500882.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter and Bedaio by Tullius Iuvenis, a beneficarius consularis of Legio II Italica, during the consulship of Antoninus II and Sacerdos, likely in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500883.text_edition: \n Bedaio Augusto et Alounis sic sacrum Caius Catius Secundianus IIvir Imperatore Antonino II et Sacerdote consulibus\n
EDCS-14500883.translation: To Bedaio Augustus and Alounis, sacred. Caius Catius Secundianus, IIvir, in the consulship of Emperor Antoninus II and Sacerdos.
EDCS-14500883.summary: A religious dedication to Bedaio and Alounis by Caius Catius Secundianus, a IIvir, during the consulship of Antoninus II and Sacerdos, likely in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500884.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque Caius Restitutius Restitutianus <c=G>(enturio) <l=I>egionis e<t=I> Iulia Honorata coniux eius votum solverunt laeti libentes merito \n
EDCS-14500884.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses. Caius Restitutius Restitutianus, centurion of the Legio VI Ferrata, and his wife Honorata, willingly paid their vows with joy and with merit.
EDCS-14500884.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter and other deities by Caius Restitutius Restitutianus, a centurion of Legio VI Ferrata, and his wife, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500885.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Trebonio Firmo et Valeriae Iantumarae Caius Trebonius Faustus IIvirum et praefectus iure dicundo civitatis Aguontensium sic parentibus optimis et sibi et Treboniae Maximi filiae Exoratae coniugi fecit\n
EDCS-14500885.translation: To Gnaeus Trebonius Firmus and Valeria Iantumara. Caius Trebonius Faustus, IIvir and magistrate with judicial power of the civitas Aguontensium, made this for his most excellent parents and for himself and for his wife Trebonia Maximi, daughter Exorata.
EDCS-14500885.summary: A dedication by Caius Trebonius Faustus, a civic magistrate, for his parents, himself, and his family, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500886.text_edition: \n Fuscia Proculi filia Secunda obita nnorum XXX hic sita est heredes pudenti matri fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500886.translation: Fuscia Secunda, daughter of Proculus, who died at the age of 30, is buried here. Her heirs made this for their dutiful mother.
EDCS-14500886.summary: A tombstone for Fuscia Secunda, erected by her heirs, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500888.text_edition: \n Maximiae Publi filiae Maximae obitae annorum XXXV Iulia Avita mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500888.translation: For Maxima, daughter of Publius, who died at the age of 35. Her mother Iulia Avita saw to the making of this.
EDCS-14500888.summary: A tombstone for Maxima, erected by her mother Iulia Avita, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500889.text_edition: \n Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto III et Cassio Dione iterum consulibus Lucius Pomponius Constans et Marcus Ursinius Verus IIviri dicaverunt\n
EDCS-14500889.translation: Under Emperor our lord Severus Alexander Augustus, for the 3rd time, and Cassius Dio, for the 2nd time, consuls. Lucius Pomponius Constans and Marcus Ursinius Verus, IIviri, dedicated this.
EDCS-14500889.summary: A dedication by Lucius Pomponius Constans and Marcus Ursinius Verus, IIviri, during the consulship of Severus Alexander III and Cassius Dio II, in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500890.text_edition: \n Apollini Granno et Sironae AII N INIIIO votum solv--- liben--- laet-- merito\n
EDCS-14500890.translation: To Apollo Grannus and Sirona, [the dedicator] willingly and gladly fulfilled their vow as is fitting.
EDCS-14500890.summary: This is a votive inscription to the gods Apollo Grannus and Sirona, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500891.text_edition: \n Lucio Bellicio Luci filio Quartioni decurioni Iuvavensium IIviro iuris dicundi vixit anos LVIII Sapliae Belatumarae coniugi annorum LXII Bellicius Seccio et Bellicius Achilles cum coniugibus ex testamento faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14500891.translation: Lucius Bellicius Quartio, son of Lucius, decurion of the Iuvavenses and duovir with jurisdiction, lived for 58 years. Saplia Belatumara, his wife, aged 62. Bellicius Seccio and Bellicius Achilles had this made in accordance with the will, along with their wives.
EDCS-14500891.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Bellicius Quartio and his wife Saplia Belatumara, dated to the Roman Imperial period and mentioning local civic offices in Iuvavum (modern Salzburg).

EDCS-14500892.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Placidus Tinci Loli Honorati servus vivus fecit sibi et Firmae coniugi et Placidiano filio et suisque omnibus\n
EDCS-14500892.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Placidus, slave of Tincius Lollius Honoratus, made this himself while alive for himself and for his wife Firma, and for his son Placidianus, and for all his own.
EDCS-14500892.summary: This funerary inscription records Placidus, a slave, making a tomb for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500893.text_edition: \n Lucius Virius @(obit-) et Lucius Virius Maximianus decurio et IIvir Claudi Iuvavi et Viria Moderata et sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500893.translation: Lucius Virius [deceased] and Lucius Virius Maximianus, decurion and duovir of the Claudi Iuvavi, and Viria Moderata, and [they themselves] made this while alive for themselves.
EDCS-14500893.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Virius and potentially his family, with some members holding local civic offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500894.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Marci Lolli Prisci patroni bene merentis Ianuar(ius) Lupercus liberti\n
EDCS-14500894.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, for the safety of Marcus Lollius Priscus, the deserving patron. By Ianvarius Lupercus, his freedman.
EDCS-14500894.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mithras for the well-being of Marcus Lollius Priscus, made by his freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500895.text_edition: \n Quintus Septueius Faustus et Attica Placidi filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Quinto Septueio Marino filio annorum XIII obito\n
EDCS-14500895.translation: Quintus Septueius Faustus and Attica, daughter of Placidus, his wife, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Quintus Septueius Marinus, who died at age 13.
EDCS-14500895.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Septueius Faustus and his wife Attica making a tomb for themselves and their deceased son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500896.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Quietus vivus fecit sibi et Veratiae Serotinae coniugi et Veratio Primioni filio obito nnorum XXX posterisque suis hoc monumentum heredem non sequitur\n
EDCS-14500896.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Quietus made this himself while alive for himself and for his wife Veratia Serotina, and for his son Veratius Primio, who died at age 30, and for his descendants; this monument does not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500896.summary: This funerary inscription records Julius Quietus making a tomb for his family and explicitly stating it is not inheritable, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500897.text_edition: \n Sextus Iulius Aptus veteranus ex praetorio obitus annorum L Maximilla marito optimo et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-14500897.translation: Sextus Julius Aptus, veteran from the Praetorian guard, died at age 50. Maximilla made this for her excellent husband and for herself while alive.
EDCS-14500897.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Julius Aptus, a Praetorian veteran, made by his wife Maximilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500898.text_edition: \n Marcius Attius Marcellinus veteranus cohortis II praetoriae obitus annorum XXXV Marcius Marcus fratri ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500898.translation: Marcius Attius Marcellinus, veteran of the 2nd Praetorian cohort, died at age 35. Marcius Marcus had this made for his brother in accordance with the will.
EDCS-14500898.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcius Attius Marcellinus, a Praetorian veteran, commissioned by his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500899.text_edition: \n In memoriam Marci Proculei Iustini Marcus Proculeius Martialis decurio et IIvir et Gavia Iustina parentes\n
EDCS-14500899.translation: In memory of Marcus Proculeius Justinus. Marcus Proculeius Martialis, decurion and duovir, and Gavia Iustina, his parents.
EDCS-14500899.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Marcus Proculeius Justinus, set up by his parents who held civic offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500900.text_edition: \n Claudius Seneca obitus annorum LXV Senecio Terentiae Honoratae servus matri et patri et sibi et suis vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500900.translation: Claudius Seneca, aged 65 at death, a slave of Senecio, made this for Terentia Honorata, his mother, and father, and himself, and his own family, during his lifetime.
EDCS-14500900.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Seneca, a slave, erected by himself and other family members, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500903.text_edition: \n Quinto Sabinio Constituto @(obito) nnorum LXXX Constanti filio @(obito) nnorum XXX et Servandae filiae @(obitae) annorum XXV et Sabinia Optuma liberta et heres viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500903.translation: Constantius, son of Sabinius Constans, aged 80 at death, and Servanda, daughter, aged 25 at death, and Sabinia Optuma, freedwoman and heir, made this for Quintus Sabinius Constans, aged 80 at death, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500903.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Quintus Sabinius Constans and his children, erected by his freedwoman and heir, Sabinia Optuma, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500904.text_edition: \n Nymphis Caius Lollius Honoratus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500904.translation: Caius Lollius Honoratus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-14500904.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Caius Lollius Honoratus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500907.text_edition: \n Lucio Terentio Restituto et Terentio Quieto Erbonia Optata coniugi piissimo et filio faciundum! cur(avit)\n
EDCS-14500907.translation: Erbonia Optata arranged for the construction of this for her most dutiful husband, Lucius Terentius Restitutus, and her son, Terentius Quietus.
EDCS-14500907.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Erbonia Optata commissioned a monument for her husband, Lucius Terentius Restitutus, and son, Terentius Quietus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500908.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Saplius Agrippa decurio et II vir coloniae Ovilavensium sibi et Primiae Honoratae coniugi et Lucio Saplio Optato decurioni coloniae supra scriptae filio et Lucio Saplio Agrippae filio vivis fecit et Lucio Saplio Optato aedili coloniae supra scriptae fratri @(obito) annorum L et Lucio Saplio Honorato militi praetoriano filio @(obito) annorum XXI dierum Antiochia Syria et Sapliae Optatae nepti\n
EDCS-14500908.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Saplius Agrippa, decurion and duovir of the colony of Ovilavia, made this for himself and his wife, Primia Honorata, and his son, Lucius Saplius Optatus, decurion of the aforementioned colony, and his son, Lucius Saplius Agrippa, while they were alive, and for his brother, Lucius Saplius Optatus, aedile of the aforementioned colony, aged 50 at death, and his son, Lucius Saplius Honoratus, praetorian soldier, aged 21 years and days, from Antioch in Syria, and for his granddaughter, Saplia Optata.
EDCS-14500908.summary: This extensive funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by Lucius Saplius Agrippa, a prominent official in the colony of Ovilavia, for himself, his family, and other relatives, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500910.text_edition: \n na obita nnorum XVIIII bene merenti ser Florentin B Sura mater fil et sibi vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500910.translation: Florentina, daughter of B. Sura, made this for her well-deserving slave, Florentinus, aged 19 at death, and for herself, while alive.
EDCS-14500910.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by Florentina for her slave Florentinus and for herself, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500911.text_edition: \n Lollia Pocca viva fecit sibi et Viatori coniugi obito annorum L et Annoni filio obito annorum XXX posterisque suis\n
EDCS-14500911.translation: Lollia Pocca made this for herself, her husband Viator, aged 50 at death, her son Annon, aged 30 at death, and their descendants, while alive.
EDCS-14500911.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lollia Pocca erecting a monument for herself, her husband, son, and descendants, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500912.text_edition: \n nus sibi et Optatae coniugi benignae Genetivo filio vivo et Augustiano filio vilicus vilica\n
EDCS-14500912.translation: He made this for himself and his wife Optata, his son Genetivus, and his son Augustianus, the farm manager and farm manageress.
EDCS-14500912.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by an unnamed man for himself, his wife, and two sons who held positions as farm managers, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500915.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus et Attiania Matrona pro se et Restitutiis Restituto et Florino filiis votum solverunt laeti libentes meritae\n
EDCS-14500915.translation: Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus and Attiania Matrona joyfully and deservedly fulfilled their vow for themselves and for their sons Restitutus and Florinus, to Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-14500915.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Victoria Augusta by Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus and Attiania Matrona on behalf of themselves and their sons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500917.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Secundinus Augusti nostri vilicus stationis Escensis votum rettulit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-14500917.translation: Secundinus, farm manager of the station at Esca, belonging to our Emperor, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly offered his vow to the Unconquered God Mithras.
EDCS-14500917.summary: This is a votive inscription by Secundinus, a farm manager, dedicating a vow to the god Mithras, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500918.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Romanus Materni filius vivus sibi et Romanae Argentoniae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LXXX bene merenti\n
EDCS-14500918.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Romanus, son of Maternius, while alive, for himself and his deserving wife Romana Argentonia, who died at the age of 80.
EDCS-14500918.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Romanus for himself and his deceased wife, Romana Argentonia, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500920.text_edition: \n Masso Praetoriani et Valentina Flori vivi sibi fecerunt et Optato filio @(obito) annorum II\n
EDCS-14500920.translation: Masso of the Praetorian Guard and Valentina, alive, made this for themselves and for their son Optatus, who died at the age of 2.
EDCS-14500920.summary: A funerary epitaph for Masso, Valentina, and their young son Optatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period, commemorating family members.

EDCS-14500921.text_edition: \n Messorius Matuco veteranus legionis II Italica obitus nnorum LXX Mottu coniux viva\n
EDCS-14500921.translation: Messorius Matuco, a veteran of the II Italian Legion, died at the age of 70. His wife Mottu, alive.
EDCS-14500921.summary: A funerary epitaph for Messorius Matuco, a veteran of the II Italian Legion, and his living wife Mottu, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500922.text_edition: \n Lucius Cotinius Luci filius Martialis decurio Iuvavensium IIvir iure dicundo sibi et Pecciae Latinae uxori vivus\n
EDCS-14500922.translation: Lucius Cotinius Martialis, son of Lucius, a decurion of the Juvavenses, an aedile with judicial authority, while alive, for himself and for his wife Peccia Latina.
EDCS-14500922.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Cotinius Martialis, an official in the city of Juvavum, set up by himself for himself and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500923.text_edition: \n Novelli Optati patris @(obiti) annorum L- et Iuliae Secundinae matri @(obitae) annorum XC et Secundino Secundi avunculo @(obito) annorum X et Secundo \n
EDCS-14500923.translation: For Novellus, son of Optatus the father, who died at the age of 50, and for Julia Secundina the mother, who died at the age of 90, and for Secundinus, uncle of Secundus, who died at the age of 10, and for Secundus.
EDCS-14500923.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating several deceased family members: Novellus, his mother Julia Secundina, his uncle Secundinus, and Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500924.text_edition: \n Serenus Claudi Arionis filius Praesentinae coniugi optimae obitae annorum LIII et patri obito annorum CII et sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500924.translation: Serenus, son of Claudius Arion, while alive, made this for his excellent wife Praesentina, who died at the age of 53, and for his father, who died at the age of 102, and for himself.
EDCS-14500924.summary: A funerary monument erected by Serenus for his deceased wife, father, and himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500926.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto in memoriam Marci Modesti Marci fili Repentini Marcus Modestius Repentinus pater veteranus ex decurio alae I Aravacorum\n
EDCS-14500926.translation: To Apollo Augustus, in memory of Marcus Modestus Repentinus, son of Marcus. Marcus Modestius Repentinus, his father, a veteran and former decurion of the I Ala Aravacorum.
EDCS-14500926.summary: A dedication to Apollo Augustus, set up by a father in memory of his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500927.text_edition: \n Publi Aeli Flavi decurionis et IIviri et flaminis Aelium Cetiensium item decurionis et IIviri e<t> pontificis colonia Aurelia Antoniniana Ovilavensium tribuni legionis III Augustae et Aeliae Publi filiae Flavinae filiae eiusdem et Aeli Mansueti patris eius et Orgetiae Sisiae matris ex praecepto eius Orgetia Ursa propinqua inpendio heredis fieri instituit\n
EDCS-14500927.translation: For Aelius Cetiensium, son of Publius Aelius Flavius, a decurion and aedile and priest of the colonia Aurelia Antoniniana Ovilavensium, tribune of the III Augustan Legion, and for Aelia Flavina, daughter of Publius Aelius Flavius, of the same [colony], and for her father Aelius Mansuetus, and for his mother Orgetia Sisia. Orgetia Ursa, a relative, established this to be made at the expense of the heir, by her instruction.
EDCS-14500927.summary: A complex funerary monument inscription detailing familial relationships and official positions in Ovilava, initiated by Orgetia Ursa and funded by the heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500928.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Campestrinus veteranus et Iulia Exorata Iulio Exorato librario consulari filio obito annorum XX et Secundinio Candidiano beneficiario consulari genero vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500928.translation: Titus Flavius Campestrinus, a veteran, and Iulia Exorata, while alive, made this for their son Iulius Exoratus, a consular clerk, who died at the age of 20, and for their son-in-law Secundinius Candidianus, a consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-14500928.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Titus Flavius Campestrinus and Iulia Exorata for their deceased son and son-in-law, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500929.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Victorinus veteranus ex decurione alae Tampianae vivus fecit sibi et Flaviae Victorinae filiae @(obitae) annorum XXIII et Cosutiae Verae coniugi @(obitae) annorum L et Cosutio Firmo <f=E>(ilio dulcissimo @(obito) annorum XV \n
EDCS-14500929.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Victorinus, a veteran and former decurion of the Ala Tampiana, while alive, made this for himself, and for Flavia Victorina, his daughter, who died at the age of 23, and for Cosutia Vera, his wife, who died at the age of 50, and for Cosutius Firmus, his dearest son, who died at the age of 15.
EDCS-14500929.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Titus Flavius Victorinus, a veteran, for himself and his deceased daughter, wife, and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500930.summary: This is a tombstone or monument for Marcus Ulpius Romulus, likely erected in the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500931.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Ingenuus CS CA VCTI LII et Ingenuino nnorum meritae Supero L vivus \n
EDCS-14500931.translation: Titus Flavius Ingenuus erected this, (and) Ingenuinus his son, who lived 52 years, for their merits.
EDCS-14500931.summary: This funerary monument, likely a tombstone or altar, was dedicated by Titus Flavius Ingenuus and his son for their own merits, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500932.text_edition: \n Mogetius Martialis vivus fecit sibi et Melissae coniugi innocentissimae annorum XL filiae Capitonis\n
EDCS-14500932.translation: Mogetius Martialis made this himself while alive, and for Melissa, his most innocent wife, aged 40, daughter of Capito.
EDCS-14500932.summary: This tombstone was erected by Mogetius Martialis for himself and his wife Melissa, who died at 40, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500933.text_edition: \n Masculus Iantumari et Iulia Anna vivi fecerunt sibi et Constituto filio A \n
EDCS-14500933.translation: Masculus Iantumari and Iulia Anna made this themselves while alive, and for Constitutus their son.
EDCS-14500933.summary: This tombstone was erected by Masculus Iantumari and Iulia Anna for themselves and their son Constitutus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500934.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Racconi Lucani obito annorum LX et Sumeloni Secundini Successianus filius fecit parentibus\n
EDCS-14500934.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Successianus, son of Racconus Lucanus, erected this for his parents, Racconus, who died at 60 years, and Sumelona Secundini.
EDCS-14500934.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Successianus to the spirits of his parents, Racconus and Sumelona, who died at ages 60 and an unspecified age respectively, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500935.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Senecio Lucio Monia Secundina marito optimo viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-14500935.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Senecio Lucio erected this while alive for his most excellent husband, Monia Secundina, and for herself.
EDCS-14500935.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Senecio Lucio for her husband and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500936.text_edition: \n Ittu Ripani vivus fecit sibi et Viatorinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LX et Hilarae Ittunis filiae @(obitae) annorum XXX\n
EDCS-14500936.translation: Ittu Ripani erected this while alive for himself and for Viatorina, his wife, who died at 60 years, and for Hilara, daughter of Ittu, who died at 30 years.
EDCS-14500936.summary: This tombstone was erected by Ittu Ripani for himself, his wife Viatorina (died at 60), and his daughter Hilara (died at 30), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500937.text_edition: \n Secundus Vercai filius et Secunda Calventi filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Firmo filio @(obito) annorum XX\n
EDCS-14500937.translation: Secundus, son of Vercai, and Secunda, daughter of Calventius, erected this themselves while alive, and for Firmu, their son, who died at 20 years.
EDCS-14500937.summary: This tombstone was erected by Secundus and Secunda for themselves and their son Firmu, who died at age 20, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500938.text_edition: \n Lucius? Senecius Valens vivus fecit sibi et Brogiae coniugi @(obitae)? annorum XXXVII et Lucio Senecio Valentino filio equiti cohortis annorum XXVII stipendiorum VIIII\n
EDCS-14500938.translation: Lucius? Senecius Valens erected this himself while alive, and for Brogia, his wife, who died at 37 years, and for Lucius Senecius Valens, son of Brogia, a cavalryman of the cohort, who died at 27 years and served 9 years.
EDCS-14500938.summary: This tombstone was erected by Lucius? Senecius Valens for himself, his wife Brogia (died at 37), and his son Lucius Senecius Valens (died at 27, served 9 years), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500939.text_edition: \n Sul Ressatus et Atigenta coniux vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-14500939.translation: Sul Ressatus and his wife Atigenta erected this themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500939.summary: This tombstone was erected by Sul Ressatus and his wife Atigenta for themselves, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500940.text_edition: \n Carmo Adnami libertus sibi et Iuliae Titi libertae uxsori sic vivus fecit\n
EDCS-14500940.translation: Carmo, freedman of Adnamus, made this for himself and his wife Iulia, freedwoman of Titus, while alive.
EDCS-14500940.summary: A funerary epitaph for Carmo and his wife, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500941.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Verus pro salute Comaciae et Commagenorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14500941.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Verus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the health of Comacia and the Commageni.
EDCS-14500941.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Verus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500942.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Tiberi filiae Aquinae annorum XXXV Rufinius Rufus uxsori sic pientissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500942.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Rufinius Rufus had this made for his most devoted wife, Claudia Aquina, daughter of Tiberius, aged 35.
EDCS-14500942.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Aquina, erected by her husband Rufinius Rufus, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500943.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Germano veterano ex decurione et Cassiae Valentinae eius et Publiis Aeliis Sabiniano et Germano dec<c=G>(urionibus) II(viris) iure dicundo coloniae Aureliae <O=Q>vil<ei=AV>ensium a militiis et <Ael=P>(iae) Valentinae sorori e<g=Q>(regiae) memoriae puellae Publius Ae<li=T>us Rufinius decurio et IIvir <iure=L> dicundo A<e>l Cetiensium perfici curavit \n
EDCS-14500943.translation: Publius Aelius Rufinius, decurion and IIvir for administering justice in the colony of Aurelia Ovilia, had this completed for Publius Aelius Germanus, a veteran ex-decurion, and his wife Cassia Valentina, and their sons Publius Aelius Sabinianus and Germanus, IIviri, and his sister Aelia Valentina, a girl of regal memory.
EDCS-14500943.summary: A dedication on a public building or monument honoring local magistrates and their family members in the colony of Aurelia Ovilia, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500944.text_edition: \n Obiledo Quinti vivus fecit sibi et Speratiae Maximae vivae Speratius Lupulus annorum LXX\n
EDCS-14500944.translation: Obiledo Quinti made this for himself and his living wife Speratia Maxima, while alive. Speratius Lupulus, aged 70.
