EDCS-31000650.summary: This partially restored inscription indicates that someone named Cae[---]cus made an object, with the full context and object being uncertain.

EDCS-31000722.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Sextus Ili, likely a person's name or part of an epitaph.

EDCS-31000883.translation: Aco Acastus: life is short, hope is fragile; come, let us live while it is light, companions.
EDCS-31000883.summary: This inscription is a funerary epigram advising to live life to the fullest due to its brevity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31000884.translation: Caius Aco, freedman of Caius, Eros
EDCS-31000884.summary: This inscription records the name Caius Aco Eros, identifying him as a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31000921.summary: This inscription records that Abbo made something, possibly a commissioned work or object, with the abbreviation KIYL possibly indicating a unit of measure or material.

EDCS-31001479.summary: This inscription records that Conatius made something for Ursinus, possibly a votive offering or funerary monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31001482.summary: This inscription records that Costinius made something, possibly a commissioned work or object, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31001485.summary: This inscription records that Recentius made something, possibly a commissioned work or object, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31500293.summary: This inscription identifies the 'Officina Frontini' (Workshop of Frontinus), indicating a business or artisan's establishment, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31500304.summary: This inscription records that Pateratio made something, possibly a commissioned work or object, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31500309.summary: A statement of creation by Costius Priscus.

EDCS-31500312.summary: An indication of origin from the workshop of Intirius.

EDCS-31500317.summary: A statement of creation by Celer Malsius.

EDCS-31700018.translation: To Valerius, a citizen of Iuvavum, who lived for 71 years and died on the 8th day before the Kalends of August. Theodosius the presbyter, with his flock, helped his excellent brother.
EDCS-31700018.summary: A funerary inscription for Valerius, a citizen of Iuvavum, with a note about the date of death and communal support from Theodosius the presbyter and his flock.

EDCS-32100358.summary: A dedication to the spirits of the dead for Irina.

EDCS-32300320.summary: A dedication to Lucius Attius Fortunatus.

EDCS-32300321.translation: From the workshop of Titus Flavius Secundus.
EDCS-32300321.summary: An indication of origin from the workshop of Titus Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-32300430.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Quintus Mete, noting his age.

EDCS-32300598.summary: The name and cognomen of Gaius Priscius Oppidanus, likely an epitaph or identification.

EDCS-32600055.summary: A repetitive inscription likely identifying a person named Silvanus, possibly a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-32600069.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Secundini Silvanus Silvanus.

EDCS-32600072.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Silvanus Silvanus.

EDCS-32600092.translation: Gaius T. Successus, freedman of the emperor, (dedicates this).
EDCS-32600092.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication by Gaius T. Successus, possibly a freedman of the emperor, given the abbreviation 'CCAR'.

EDCS-32602009.translation: 60 pounds of olive oil from the Cibarii, from Histricius Julius Alpinus.
EDCS-32602009.summary: This inscription records a delivery or payment of 60 pounds of olive oil, specifying the origin and the name of the supplier, Histricius Julius Alpinus.

EDCS-32602010.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Tusidius Dexter.

EDCS-32700010.translation: Burial of Sirus and Severus, grandchildren, deceased on the Nones of December, aged 32 years and 4 months.
EDCS-32700010.summary: This inscription records the burial of two grandchildren, Sirus and Severus, specifying their age at death and the date of their passing.

EDCS-32700094.summary: This inscription commemorates Tiberius Claudius Saecularis, indicating he died peacefully.

EDCS-44200177.summary: This inscription identifies Titus Paetranius Profuturus and his place of origin, Luteva.

EDCS-44300036.summary: This inscription begins the name Clementinius Cl, likely part of a longer dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-44300037.summary: This inscription records a filiation, indicating Boniata is the son of Boniatus.

EDCS-44300038.summary: This inscription is a simple naming, possibly a tombstone or a label.

EDCS-44300042.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Minerva Augusta.

EDCS-44300046.summary: This inscription records a votive offering or monument made by a living individual.

EDCS-44700037.translation: To the Spirits of the departed, Caius Valerius Tertullus and Rettia Julia made (this) for their son, aedile, Claudia Teurnia, who died at the age of 25, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-44700037.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by parents to their deceased son, who held the office of aedile, and for the parents themselves.

EDCS-44700038.translation: The Superis Troop, the Troop of the Hunter Verus, the Troop of Verus Primus.
EDCS-44700038.summary: This inscription lists three military troops, likely for identification on a monument or banner.

EDCS-46601040.translation: The new workshop of Arelas, under the master Ursicinus.
EDCS-46601040.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop in Arelas and names its master, Ursicinus.

EDCS-48500283.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Felix as the slave of Marius.

EDCS-48500294.translation: Of Aulus Titius, the potter of Arretium
EDCS-48500294.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Aulus Titius as a potter from Arretium.

EDCS-48500301.summary: This inscription names Marcus Perennius Crescens, likely as the owner or subject of a monument.

EDCS-48700103.translation: Of Communis, son of Titus Kanius
EDCS-48700103.summary: This inscription records the name of Communis, son of Titus Kanius, likely identifying ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-48700105.translation: Borius, son of Savi, of the Ulkisiana tribe, a goldsmith and engraver, five gold denarii, one gold [coin].
EDCS-48700105.summary: This inscription records the sale of five gold denarii by a goldsmith and engraver named Borius, possibly from the Ulkisiana tribe.

EDCS-48700112.summary: This inscription identifies Princeps as the freedman of Peccius.

EDCS-48800041.translation: To Minerva on behalf of Ianuaria.
EDCS-48800041.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Minerva, made on behalf of a woman named Ianuaria.

EDCS-48800042.translation: Aurelius Dizo set up this vow to Hercules.
EDCS-48800042.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the god Hercules by Aurelius Dizo.

EDCS-48800053.translation: Albinus made this [dedication] in good health, in the service of our lords.
EDCS-48800053.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Albinus, possibly in a public or official capacity.

EDCS-48800083.summary: This inscription likely refers to a public supply or provision, possibly water, under the Iovian designation.

EDCS-48800086.translation: On the twelfth day before the Kalends of August, Rogatus of the II Legion.
EDCS-48800086.summary: This inscription records an individual named Rogatus, associated with the II Legion, with a specific date.

EDCS-48900554.summary: This inscription is very fragmentary and likely records something belonging to or done by someone named Lucia or Luciaes.

EDCS-48900566.translation: Aemilius Genuinus, son of Atpomarus, [aged] thirteen.
EDCS-48900566.summary: This inscription identifies Aemilius Genuinus, the son of Atpomarus, and states his age as thirteen.

EDCS-49300066.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by GEI opti during his lifetime.

EDCS-49300076.translation: Aurelius I, Aurelius Agation, Aurelius Chrestus IV, Aurelius Ar, Aurelius Dio, Aurelius Hermogenes, Billus Anus, Aurelius D., Aurelius Pe, Aurelius I., Aurelius.
EDCS-49300076.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly indicating ownership or participants in an event, with the names prefixed by 'Aurelius'.

EDCS-49300077.translation: NVElus D NI X L, Aurelius I, Aurelius D., Aurelius SI, Aurelius CI, Aurelius S.
EDCS-49300077.summary: This inscription is a fragmented list of names, primarily featuring the cognomen 'Aurelius', possibly related to a record or a dedication.

EDCS-52900103.translation: Ursus, a man of rank, and his wife Ursina, made this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-52900103.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Ursus and his wife Ursina, fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-53000381.translation: F O N made it, 100 feet.
EDCS-53000381.summary: This inscription records the measurement of a construction, possibly a boundary or a structure, with an abbreviated maker's name and dimensions.

EDCS-56500258.translation: Aprilis of Camerus, a mosaic, 5 and a half denarii.
EDCS-56500258.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of a sale or cost for a mosaic (lodicem murrinum) by Aprilis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-56500259.summary: This inscription records a transaction where Verus purchased something from Res, likely a commercial record.

EDCS-59200187.translation: Of Julius Laurentius, a painted [object], a quarter of a denarius.
EDCS-59200187.summary: This inscription seems to record the cost of a painted item belonging to Julius Laurentius, possibly an inventory or a receipt.

EDCS-60400090.translation: Having died at the age of 50 years, Ursula to her dearest husband.
EDCS-60400090.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from Ursula to her husband, commemorating his death at the age of 50.

EDCS-60500056.translation: Is poured out alive for the church of Irinc, of good memory.
EDCS-60500056.summary: This inscription seems to record a donation or endowment to a church, possibly related to a person named Irinc, suggesting a late Roman or early Christian context.

EDCS-62900018.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars, likely from a soldier or military unit.

EDCS-62900038.translation: On the twelfth day before the Kalends of July, Heraclides.
EDCS-62900038.summary: This inscription records a date and a personal name, possibly a marker for an event or a contract.

EDCS-62900041.translation: On the third Ides of August, Verinus.
EDCS-62900041.summary: This inscription records a date and a personal name, possibly marking an event or a tomb.

EDCS-67400463.translation: Marcus Successius Probatus, the aedile, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Claudia Celeia.
EDCS-67400463.summary: This is a vow fulfilled by Marcus Successius Probatus, an aedile, for Claudia Celeia, likely related to public office or a religious dedication.

EDCS-67400464.translation: For the children of Iessius, the divine Caius Fi Claud.
EDCS-67400464.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or memorial for the children of Iessius, possibly by Caius Fi Claud.

EDCS-67600202.translation: Bonius Maximinus, son of Vibius, made this for the god Mithras.
EDCS-67600202.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mithras, made by Bonius Maximinus, son of Vibius.

EDCS-67600207.translation: Lucius Iulius Senis, crocus for roughness.
EDCS-67600207.summary: This inscription likely refers to a medicinal or cosmetic use of crocus by Lucius Iulius Senis.

EDCS-67600217.summary: This is a dedication or dedication to a deity named Ariacna Augusta.

EDCS-67600231.translation: To the Holy Diana, Aurelius Cas...
EDCS-67600231.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Aurelius Cas[---].

EDCS-67600233.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a quantity of 11 modii, possibly related to a person named Hilarus.

EDCS-67600234.translation: Of Coxivae, of Essivi. 10 pounds of byssus (fine linen).
EDCS-67600234.summary: This inscription records a payment or quantity of 10 pounds of byssus, possibly for individuals named Coxivae and Essivi.

EDCS-67600235.translation: 8 pounds of Paenulae (a type of shoe or cloak). Lovan. Ate.
EDCS-67600235.summary: This inscription lists 8 pounds of Paenulae, along with the names Lovan and Ate, possibly indicating goods or their owners.

EDCS-67600236.translation: Monica, wife of Saci. Exuviae (remains/cast-offs) of rutile (reddish-brown color). 10 denarii for the engraver; one (item) extra.
EDCS-67600236.summary: This inscription records the value of reddish-brown cast-offs, with a payment of 10 denarii to an engraver, likely a record of a sale or transaction.

EDCS-67600237.translation: For Liccaia and Trauseus, and [---]anaeus. 8 gausapae (a type of garment), 10 denarii for the shoes, 1 sestertius and 5 asses for the shoemaker. One (item) extra.
EDCS-67600237.summary: This inscription details a transaction involving garments and shoes, with payments to a shoemaker, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-67600238.translation: Laetus, slave of Peccius, son of Lucius. For Agilis, slave.
EDCS-67600238.summary: This inscription is a dedication or record of ownership concerning slaves named Laetus and Agilis, possibly dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-67600240.translation: For Lascivus the fuller. A Spanish (item?) received, 3 pounds.
EDCS-67600240.summary: This inscription records the receipt of 3 pounds of a Spanish item for Lascivus, a fuller, likely a business record.

EDCS-69900003.translation: Black olives, excellent, from defrutum (boiled-down grape must).
EDCS-69900003.summary: This is a label or description for a product, likely indicating high-quality black olives preserved in defrutum.

EDCS-71200375.translation: Of Caius Iunius Paetus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-71200375.summary: This is a simple funerary or ownership inscription identifying Caius Iunius Paetus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-71800459.translation: To Publius Seppius Severus, decurion and former duovir, son of Claudius Iuvavus and Claudia Florentina. Pa[...]
EDCS-71800459.summary: This is the beginning of a dedication for a prominent local official, Publius Seppius Severus, mentioning his parents and civic roles, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-72300231.translation: Tertianus, freedman of Crispus Secundinus, and Secundina, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their son Camulianus, aged 10, and for Secundinus, son aged 10, and for Secundinus, son aged 30, and for Sperata, wife, while alive.
EDCS-72300231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Tertianus and Secundina for themselves and their deceased children and wife.

EDCS-75000196.translation: To the divine spirits. Primitivus, while alive, made this for Nammo, and for his wife Fortunata, the excellent, and for his deceased son Urso, aged 11. He died in Colonia Gemelis at age 25.
EDCS-75000196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Primitivus for his family, recording his wife's and son's names and his own death details.

EDCS-75300229.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules by Aelius Marcellus, likely a soldier indicating exemption from duties.

EDCS-75300230.translation: Aurelius Tertullus of the Second Italian Legion.
EDCS-75300230.summary: This is a dedication or identification of Aurelius Tertullus, a soldier of the Second Italian Legion.

EDCS-75600109.translation: Festus Banata, a sparrow, for four denarii.
EDCS-75600109.summary: This inscription likely records the sale or offering of a sparrow named Festus Banata for a price of four denarii.

EDCS-75700013.translation: Under Emperor Antoninus, the 8th day before the Ides of [month].
EDCS-75700013.summary: This is a fragment of a dated inscription, likely referring to a consular dating during the reign of Antoninus.

EDCS-75700056.translation: Martinus and Ingenua, daughter of Ingenuus, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their son Diastullus, aged 5, and for their son Amo, son of Junius, aged 2.
EDCS-75700056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Martinus and Ingenua for themselves and their young sons.

EDCS-77300064.translation: Pompeianus of the Second Italian Legion.
EDCS-77300064.summary: This is a dedication or identification of Pompeianus, a soldier of the Second Italian Legion.

EDCS-77300065.translation: Quintianus of the Second Italian Legion.
EDCS-77300065.summary: This is a dedication or identification of Quintianus, a soldier of the Second Italian Legion.

EDCS-77300082.translation: Melissus of the Second Italian Legion.
EDCS-77300082.summary: This is a dedication or identification of Melissus, a soldier of the Second Italian Legion.

EDCS-77300091.summary: This inscription identifies the Second Italian Legion, likely marking a military standard or a structure associated with the unit.

EDCS-79700078.translation: For Pegissa Brutio, aged 20, daughter, daughter-in-law, granddaughter, aged 20.
EDCS-79700078.summary: This epitaph records the ages of Pegissa Brutio and presumably her relatives, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-79700079.translation: Marcus and Ursula made this for themselves and for their daughter Ifigenia, aged 20, while alive.
EDCS-79700079.summary: This is a self-funded epitaph for Marcus, Ursula, and their daughter Ifigenia, indicating it was made during their lifetimes.

EDCS-80300007.translation: Ulpius Acutus and Ulpia Successa made this for themselves and for their daughter Prima while alive.
EDCS-80300007.summary: This is a self-funded epitaph for Ulpius Acutus, Ulpia Successa, and their daughter Prima, indicating it was made during their lifetimes.

EDCS-80300012.translation: The sons made this for Titus Titius, freedman of the Titi, Colonus, and for Titia Tertia, freedwoman of Titus, while alive.
EDCS-80300012.summary: This epitaph records the dedication by sons for Titus Titius and Titia Tertia, who were freedmen and freedwomen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80300015.translation: The troop of Alexander, a duplicarius.
EDCS-80300015.summary: This inscription refers to a military unit, specifically the troop of Alexander, and mentions a rank (duplicarius), suggesting a Roman military context.

EDCS-80300016.summary: This inscription simply records the name Marcus Ulpius Crescentinus, possibly as a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-80300020.translation: The Second Italian Legion, Lucanus the master.
EDCS-80300020.summary: This inscription identifies the Second Italian Legion and names Lucanus as its master, likely related to a military context or a guild.

EDCS-80300023.translation: Severa, his wife, made this while alive, aged 30.
EDCS-80300023.summary: This epitaph records Severa, the wife, making it for herself, noting her age and that it was made during her lifetime.

EDCS-80300025.summary: This fragmented inscription is a plea for labor or benefit, possibly an informal dedication or request found on a public surface.

EDCS-81200063.summary: This is a workshop marker, likely indicating ownership or origin, from the Vindobius workshop.

EDCS-81400062.translation: Cirvanc and Armib: Gaius Julius Dion gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-81400062.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Julius Dion, dedicated to the deities Cirvanc and Armib.

EDCS-81800006.translation: Florentina gladly fulfilled her vow as deserved.
EDCS-81800006.summary: This inscription records Florentina gladly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-81800008.translation: He took care to have this made for Vitalianus, dearest brother.
EDCS-81800008.summary: This dedication was commissioned by someone for their beloved brother, Vitalianus.

EDCS-81800013.translation: Andromeda, Perseus, the Charioteer, Pisces, Aries, Taurus, Gemini, March, April, May, June.
EDCS-81800013.summary: This inscription lists mythological figures, constellations, and months, likely related to astrological or calendrical purposes.

EDCS-81800064.translation: Titus, son of F.P. VE F. Baca, to his wife, aged [---] years.
EDCS-81800064.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Titus's wife, with Titus identified as the dedicator and the wife's age partially preserved.

EDCS-81800065.summary: This is a partial inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by an unnamed dedicator.

EDCS-82400159.translation: May Marianus Marianus Masculinus be well.
EDCS-82400159.summary: This is a cheerful wish for the well-being of Marianus Marianus Masculinus.

EDCS-82500081.summary: This inscription likely marks an object made by Cara for Aeternus, possibly as a gift or commission.

EDCS-82500095.summary: This inscription names Aulus Rinuis and Iulia Veria, possibly as dedicators or as the subjects of a monument.

EDCS-82500106.summary: This is a funerary or votive inscription recording Atillus as the maker for Masuatuca, likely a dedication.

EDCS-82500115.summary: This is a funerary or votive inscription recording Comitialis as the maker for Primi.

EDCS-82500120.summary: This is a funerary or votive inscription recording Cintusmus as the maker for Ruana.

EDCS-82500124.summary: This is a dedication by Amian Aurelius Bitus, likely to a deity or as a public monument.

EDCS-82500126.summary: This is a dedication by Talitiani Etaviti, likely to a deity or as a public monument.

EDCS-82500128.summary: This is a funerary or votive inscription recording Secundinus as the maker for Optati.

EDCS-82500163.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Mart VI to 'ernae, possibly a deity or a place name.

EDCS-82800008.translation: Ursa, servant of Primula, alive, made [this] for herself and for her deceased son Quintianus, and for Primula's freedman and her husband Tertullinus.
EDCS-82800008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a living woman, Ursa, for herself and several deceased family members and freedmen, indicating complex familial relationships.
